It wins by (1) casting big 4+ MV spells under Abaddon to chain cascade for value, (2) flooding the board with Demon/Daemon tokens via Mortarion, Magnus the Red, Pink Horror, Nurgle's Rot and The Lost and the Damned, (3) closing with go-wide beatdown under Bloodcrusher of Khorne's anthem/trample and tribal-count payoffs (The Horus Heresy, Herald of Slaanesh), with incidental lifeloss (Be'lakor, Great Unclean One) growing Abaddon. A light sacrifice subtheme (Sloppity Bilepiper as the lone sac outlet) and a small reanimation/recursion package (Poxwalkers, Nurgle's Conscription, Lucius) round it out.
fp: Deny Reality â‡„ Warstorm Surge (family 'bounce') â€” Warstorm Surge is an ETB-damage enchantment with no bounce interaction; the 'consumes bounce' tag is spurious
Assault Suit â‡„ Nurgle's Rot and Nurgle's Rot â‡„ Drach'Nyen (family 'attach') â€” coincidental equipment/aura attach pairing, no real synergy line

Drach'Nyen flagged as a rampâ†’sink target alongside Mortarion/Be'lakor â€” Drach'Nyen is an Equipment with a fixed cast cost, not an X-cost/mana-sink payoff, so its ~8 rampâ†’sink edges are weak inflation

Keeper of Secrets â‡„ cascade/noncreature spells (family 'cast') â€” Keeper triggers off OPPONENTS casting, not your own spells, so edges like Bituminous Blast/Let the Galaxy Burn â†’ Keeper of Secrets are miscast

Dark Ritual â‡„ Pink Horror / Exalted Flamer (family 'magecraft') counted as synergy â€” Dark Ritual is a ramp ritual; flagging it as a magecraft enabler double-dips the same card across families and overstates the spellslinger payoff count
fn: Cascade engine: Abaddon the Despoiler grants cascade to your 4+ MV spells and cards like Bituminous Blast and Deny Reality have cascade â€” this is the deck's central value engine and has NO family in the taxonomy (cascade/chain-cast is invisible)
Lifelossâ†’commander payoff: Abaddon gets +1/+1 counters when opponents lose life; Great Unclean One and Be'lakor produce 'lifeloss' events but Abaddon is not wired as a lifeloss payoff
Reanimation/recursion subtheme: Poxwalkers (recursive), Nurgle's Conscription, Lucius the Eternal form a small graveyard package that is not recognized as a family
Chaos/dice-coin payoffs (Mutalith Vortex Beast diceCoin) â€” Warhammer randomness mechanic has no payoff event
issues: No cascade / chain-cast family â€” for an Abaddon-helmed deck this misses the literal commander ability and the deck's defining line
No lifelossâ†’payoff / commander-counter family even though 'lifeloss' is already emitted as an event by cards

Hub-card inflation: a single sac outlet (Sloppity Bilepiper) generates 31 sac-fodderâ†’outlet edges and two anthem/count hubs (Bloodcrusher, The Horus Heresy) generate ~76 lordâ†’tribe + tribal-count edges, producing 100% largestWebShare and a high cohesion that reflects fan-out from 3 cards, not multi-card synergy depth
rampâ†’sink over-fires by treating any creature with a mana-consuming cost (Drach'Nyen) as a sink
'cast' family does not distinguish 'when you cast a spell' (your spells) from 'whenever a player casts' (opponents), creating directionally wrong edges (Keeper of Secrets)

rawEvals.name: Mardu Surge (Tarkir Dragonstorm Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Tokens-go-wide aristocrats midrange in Mardu (W/B/R). Zurgo Stormrender rewards attacking with creatures (draw/lifeloss/token on connect). The deck floods the board with creature tokens (Neriv, Ainok Strike Leader, Adeline, Hero of Bladehold, Beetleback Chief, Legion Warboss, Siege-Gang, Ophiomancer, Twilight Drover, Lingering Souls, Release the Dogs, Tempt with Vengeance, Shadow Summoning), then wins two ways: (1) go-wide combat alpha strikes amplified by anthems/overrun effects (Commander's Insignia, Goldnight Commander, Hero/Adeline, Hour of Reckoning sweep-and-swing, Will of the Mardu); and (2) aristocrats attrition â€” sac the wide board to Viscera Seer / Chittering Witch / Yahenni / Aron, draining via Bastion of Remembrance and Morbid-Opportunist-style draw. Token doublers (Divine Visitation, Kaya Geist Hunter, Legion Loyalty, Goldlust Triad, Blade of Selves myriad) act as force multipliers.
fp: Neriv â‡„ Ainok Strike Leader / many token-maker pairs flagged with family 'tokens' (s1) â€” two independent token producers do not actually synergize with each other; the edge models mere co-membership in a theme, not a real interaction, inflating avgDeg

Zurgo â‡„ Swords to Plowshares / Deadly Dispute / Kaya via 'leaves' â€” a removal spell or a draw spell 'leaving' the battlefield is not a synergy with Zurgo; these are noise edges from the over-broad 'leaves' event

rampâ†’sink edges like Sol Ring â‡„ Hour of Reckoration / Eliminate the Competition / Tempt with Vengeance â€” generic mana rocks feeding any X-cost spell is a deckbuilding truism, not a deck-defining synergy, yet it generated 55 instances

Abrade/Bitter Triumph/Stroke of Midnight â‡„ own creatures via 'destroy' â€” tagging your own removal as interacting with your own bodies (e.g. Abrade â‡„ Yahenni) is a false edge; that's removal aimed at opponents
Emeria Angel â‡„ Solemn Simulacrum / Wayfarer's Bauble via 'landfall' â€” extremely thin: Emeria is the only landfall payoff and these barely trigger it (one extra land), not a real engine
fn: Divine Visitation â€” left as an islandCard with deg 0, but it is the deck's signature payoff: it replaces every 1/1 token with a 4/4 flying Angel, supercharging EVERY token producer in the list. The taxonomy has no 'token-replacement / token-buff doubler' event, so the most important card in the deck registered zero interactions.

Skullclamp â€” flagged as an island, yet it is a premier aristocrats engine: equip a 1/1 token, it dies, draw two. With dozens of 1-toughness tokens and sac outlets this is a core card-advantage loop; the classifier's 'attach' consume found no matching producer event.

Tocasia's Welcome â€” island, but it is a go-wide card-draw payoff (draw whenever a creature/token with low power enters). In a deck making piles of tokens it draws constantly; no 'token-etbâ†’draw' family exists to catch it.
Goad as a wincon enabler â€” Grenzo and Zurgo-style political pressure are under-modeled; goad appears once and isn't tied to the combat plan.

Token-doubling family generally â€” Kaya's token-double on attack is only caught as 'counter-multiplier', and there is no first-class 'token doubler' family linking Divine Visitation, Legion Loyalty, Kaya, and myriad effects together as the multiplicative layer they form.
issues: No event for token-replacement/token-buff payoffs (Divine Visitation) â€” a glaring gap given how common these effects are in token decks; results in the deck's best card scoring as an island.

No 'creature-ETB â†’ draw' or 'death â†’ draw' producer event to catch Skullclamp, Tocasia's Welcome, Mindblade Render, Morbid Opportunist as engine payoffs â€” Skullclamp specifically is a famous token-aristocrats engine and shows as an island.
'tokens' family is double-counted symmetrically between producers, inflating degree and edges (99 instances, avgDeg 19.35) without representing real card-to-card synergy.
'leaves' is too broad â€” any permanent leaving (including your own removal resolving) generates edges with Zurgo and others; should be gated to self-sacrifice/blink contexts.
rampâ†’sink is a near-universal truism for mana rocks + X-spells; weighting it as a synergy family rewards generic goodstuff rather than intentional engines.
No first-class 'token-doubler' family to express the multiplicative go-wide layer; it is scattered across counter-multiplier and copyâ†’trigger.

rawEvals.name: Revival Trance (Final Fantasy Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mardu (WBR) reanimator / graveyard-value midrange. Self-mill (Stitcher's Supplier, Millikin, Locke, Combustible Gearhulk, Ruin Grinder) fills the yard, then reanimation (Reanimate, Stitch Together, Sun Titan, Priest of Fell Rites, Sepulchral Primordial, The Falcon, Phoenix Down, Rejoin the Fight, Rise of the Dark Realms) recurs big threats. The commander Terra anims the graveyard's worth into a +1/+1-counter / go-wide angel-token engine, and General Leo + Umaro provide a 'creatures matter / overrun' aggressive finish. Wins via reanimated bombs plus a wide counters/tokens attack, backed by treasure ramp.
fp: General Leo Cristophe â‡„ Espers to Magicite / Big Score / Solemn Simulacrum / Meteor Golem (tribal-countâ†’payoff) â€” Leo's payoff scales with creatures you control, not with copy spells, treasure rocks, or one-shot ETB utility bodies; the family is wired to every card with a body and over-counts width.

Cyan, Vengeful Samurai â‡„ Combustible Gearhulk / Millikin / The Warring Triad (graveyard, s1) â€” these are coincidental 'both touch the yard' links, not a functional synergy; Cyan is a cost-reducer/counters card, not a graveyard payoff.

Espers to Magicite â‡„ Solemn Simulacrum / Meteor Golem (copyâ†’trigger) â€” flagged as copy synergy but these are filler value ETBs; the copy line is real only on the strong reanimation/bomb targets, so most of these 13 edges are noise.

Kefka, Dancing Mad â‡„ Bedevil / Mortify / Ruinous Ultimatum (destroy) â€” these removal spells are generic interaction, not a designed combo with Kefka; the 'destroy' family conflates 'deck contains removal' with synergy.
Siegfried/Edgar â‡„ every mana rock (rampâ†’sink) â€” technically a sink exists but these are weak incidental links inflating weightedAvgDeg, not a deliberate ramp-payoff package.
fn: +1/+1 counters subtheme â€” Terra, Celes, Cyan, Siegfried, General Leo, Gau, Mog, Flayer all carry 'has-counters' but there is no counters-matters / proliferate family in the taxonomy, so a genuine cross-card theme is entirely missed.

Terra's anthem/attack payoff â€” Terra makes Angel tokens and pumps the team on attack; the deck's go-wide + counters board is a combat win condition, but Terra is modeled mainly as a graveyard sink rather than a combat/go-wide payoff.

Reanimation-target quality â€” the engine's strength comes from reanimating specific high-impact bombs (Sun Titan loop with Phoenix Down/cheap permanents, Sepulchral Primordial stealing yards), but reanimateâ†’target value is treated as flat s1 edges with no notion of payoff size.

Treasure/sacrifice loop nuance â€” Pitiless Plunderer + sac outlets (Crackling Doom, Phoenix Down) is a real value loop but only one weak 'sacrifice' edge (2 instances) was found; the aristocrats-lite line is under-detected.
issues: No +1/+1 counters / proliferate family despite 8 cards tagged 'has-counters' â€” a recurring blind spot for counters-themed precons.

'tribal-countâ†’payoff' / go-wide payoff hubs over-fire onto every card with a body (37 Leo edges), inflating avgDeg (8.27) and largestWebShare; needs gating to actual creature-count payoffs and exclusion of noncreature utility.

'graveyard' family is an undifferentiated mega-bucket (159 edges) that links any yard-toucher to any other â€” it should distinguish self-mill enabler â†’ reanimation outlet â†’ reanimation target rather than a fully-connected blob.
'destroy' family equates having removal with synergy (Kefka + generic removal), producing meaningless edges.

weightedAvgDeg of 2.59 vs avgDeg 8.27 correctly hints most edges are weak (s1); the cohesion of 52 lands in a fair 'Cohesive' band, but it is propped up by the over-fired graveyard/Leo hubs rather than the counter theme it misses.

rawEvals.name: Chaos Incarnate (Starter Commander Decks Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: placeholder
fp: Kardur, Doomscourge â‡„ Hate Mirage / Wild Ricochet (copyâ†’trigger, s2) â€” copying a noncreature spell does not retrigger Kardur's ETB goad/drain; these are not real synergies with the commander

Kardur, Doomscourge â‡„ Rakshasa Debaser / Wildfire Devils (copyâ†’trigger) â€” these copy/reanimation effects don't interact with Kardur's death-drain in any meaningful copy-trigger way; spurious commander edge

Brash Taunter â‡„ Guttersnipe / Thermo-Alchemist (damage) and the dozen other Brash Taunter 'damage' edges â€” many are inflated; pinging Taunter for 1 with Guttersnipe is not a real combo line, padding its degree to 19
Geode Rager â‡„ Solemn Simulacrum / Wayfarer's Bauble (landfall) â€” Geode Rager cares about an opponent's landfall to escape exile, not your own fetch lands; mis-modeled landfall edge
Reign of the Pit / Coveted Jewel â‡„ multiple mana rocks (rampâ†’sink) â€” generic 'rock feeds X-spell/expensive spell' edges that don't reflect deliberate synergy
fn: GOAD as a win engine: Kardur goads ALL enemy creatures every combat; combined with Kazuul (makes tokens when attacked) and the deathâ†’drain payoff this is the deck's primary line, but goad has only 1 edge instance â€” no 'goad â†’ forced-combat â†’ death â†’ drain' family

Dictate of the Twin Gods (island, deg 0): doubles all damage, amplifying every burn spell, Spiteful Visions, Brash Taunter, Kaervek, Magmatic Force, Tectonic Giant â€” a core payoff with zero detected edges

Symmetric-pain / punisher payoff family: Spiteful Visions, Kaervek the Merciless, Magmatic Force, Theater of Horrors all deal incremental table-wide damage as a wincon, but there is no 'pinger/punisher â†’ life-total payoff' family tying them together

Lifeloss/drain group-slug as a damage clock: Kardur's drain, Breath of Malfegor, etc. all chip every opponent simultaneously â€” no edges connect these symmetric-lifeloss sources to each other or to the damage-doublers
issues: No goad family/event: the taxonomy reduces the deck's signature mechanic to a single weak edge, missing goadâ†’forced-combatâ†’death-trigger chains entirely
No damage-amplifier/doubler event: Dictate of the Twin Gods becomes an island despite touching every damage source â€” needs a 'damage-doubling â†’ damage-source' enablement family

magecraft over-counted: every noncreature spell generates an edge to each spellcast payoff (44 instances), structurally inflating cohesion for any deck running 2 pingers and a high instant/sorcery count
copyâ†’trigger applied too loosely to the commander and to spell-copy effects, manufacturing s2 edges that don't retrigger the named ability
Brash Taunter's redirect ability creates a hub of low-value 'damage' edges, padding degree without representing intentional combos
No symmetric-lifeloss/punisher payoff family, so the deck's actual group-slug win condition is invisible to the cohesion metric

rawEvals.name: Family Matters (Bloomburrow Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Jeskai (WUR) go-wide tokens + spell-copy midrange. Zinnia, Valley's Voice makes 1/1 tokens when you cast creature spells and copies your next instant/sorcery when you've cast enough creatures, so the deck floods the board with token bodies (Murmuration Birds, Elspeth Soldiers, Siege-Gang Goblins, Thopter Engineer/Blade Splicer artifact tokens), then converts the wide board into damage and card advantage via combat payoffs (Curiosity Crafter, Bident of Thassa, Jacked Rabbit) and anthems (Jazal Goldmane, Elspeth). Secondary engines: ETB-value/blink (Restoration Angel + Aether Channeler/Cloudblazer/Skyclave Apparition), creature-copy (Helm of the Host, Echoing Assault), a +1/+1-counter "Offspring"-ish subtheme (Jacked Rabbit, Rapid Augmenter, Boss's Chauffeur), and board wipes that leave you ahead (Martial Coup, Elspeth ult, Storm of Souls).
fp: Elspeth, Sun's Champion â‡„ ~30 creatures (Solemn Simulacrum, Circuit Mender, Cloudblazer, Tetsuko, Arthur, etc.) tagged lordâ†’tribe â€” Elspeth is NOT a tribal lord; she makes Soldier tokens and has an emblem/destroy ult but gives no creature-type anthem, so these lordâ†’tribe edges are spurious and create a fake 38-degree hub
Zinnia â‡„ Murmuration / Elspeth lordâ†’tribe â€” Zinnia is not a lord either; she is a token-maker/spell-copier, so 'lordâ†’tribe' mislabels the relationship

rampâ†’sink Signetâ†’spell edges (e.g. Arcane Signet â‡„ Skyclave Apparition, Fellwar Stone â‡„ Pull from Tomorrow) â€” generic mana-rock-to-any-mana-user pairings that are true by definition but represent no deck-specific synergy; 71 such edges massively inflate graph density
copyâ†’trigger from Echoing Assault/Helm onto near-vanilla bodies (e.g. Angel of the Ruins, Inferno Titan) flagged strength-2 though re-triggering their ETB is marginal value, not a real combo line
fn: Zinnia's core engine â€” casting creature spells to trigger her instant/sorcery COPY ability (the 'Family Matters' spell-copy payoff) â€” has no event in the taxonomy; the deck's actual identity (creature-cast â†’ copy spell â†’ tokens) is completely uncaptured

Bloomburrow Offspring / +1/+1-counter subtheme under-modeled: Jacked Rabbit, Rapid Augmenter, Pollywog Prodigy, Boss's Chauffeur, Shield Broker all traffic in counters yet 'counters' fired only 3 edges; no counter-payoff/doubling family
Anthem/static-pump go-wide payoff (Jazal Goldmane's team pump, Elspeth +1 making tokens then ult overrun) is folded into generic lordâ†’tribe rather than a tokensâ†’anthem 'go-wide payoff' family
Token-doubling / mass-token-to-payoff lines (Martial Coup, Siege-Gang sac, Devilish Valet doubling on token creation) not recognized as a finisher package
issues: Lord detection has a false-positive class: token-producing planeswalkers/creatures (Elspeth) are tagged is-lord, producing dozens of bogus lordâ†’tribe edges and an artificial mega-hub that props up cohesion
No event for commander-specific spell-copy / cast-trigger mechanics â€” Zinnia (and similar 'spend mana â†’ copy spell' designs) are reduced to is-mana-sink + tokens, missing the central engine
rampâ†’sink is over-weighted and combinatorial (every rock Ă— every sink), inflating edge count and largestWebShare without measuring real synergy
counters family is under-tuned for sets with a dedicated counter/Offspring theme â€” only 3 edges despite 5+ counter cards

Because real engine is missed and fake edges (Elspeth-lord, rampâ†’sink) are added, the 39 cohesion lands in roughly the right band for a precon by coincidence rather than by correctly identifying the synergies

rawEvals.name: Grave Danger (Starter Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Dimir (UB) Zombie tribal go-wide / aristocrats midrange led by Gisa and Geralf. It builds a Zombie board via token-makers (Liliana's Devotee, Curse of Disturbance, Open the Graves, amass cards) and Zombie lords (Cemetery Reaper, Lord of the Accursed, Liliana's Mastery, Vela), grinds value off the graveyard (Gisa recasting Zombies from yards, reanimation, mill), and wins by either combat with a pumped Zombie horde OR by death-triggered life drain (Diregraf Captain, Vengeful Dead, Undead Augur, Gray Merchant) fueled by a sac outlet (Vampiric Rites, Spark Reaper). Classic "death-drain aristocrats with a tribal lord shell."
fp: Arcane Signet / Sol Ring / Dimir Signet â‡„ Gray Merchant of Asphodel (rampâ†’sink): Gray Merchant is a devotion-based ETB drain finisher, not a mana sink â€” its impact scales with black pips on the board, not with how much mana the Signets make, so this is a spurious rampâ†’sink edge.

Signets â‡„ Liliana's Standard Bearer (rampâ†’sink): Standard Bearer's mana cost just makes its draw-on-death-of-creatures payoff cheaper as a one-time cast; it is not a repeatable mana sink that ramp meaningfully feeds.

Curse of Disturbance â‡„ Lazotep Plating / Lazotep Reaver / Necrotic Hex (combatâ†’payoff): Curse triggers a token on combat-damage-to-a-player; pairing it generically with every token-maker as combatâ†’payoff double-counts â€” those cards don't enable or amplify Curse's attack trigger.

Lotleth Giant â‡„ Unbreathing Horde (damage): both happen to touch counters/damage tags but there is no real synergy line between Lotleth Giant's mill-damage finisher and Unbreathing Horde's zombie-count toughness.
Vela the Night-Clad lordâ†’tribe edges to non-Zombies and the sheer count of deg-32 Vela edges over-credit a single anthem/leaves card as if it ties the whole deck together.
fn: Gisa and Geralf's actual marquee ability â€” casting a Zombie creature card from ANY graveyard each turn (and the cast-trigger mill) â€” is flattened into a generic one-strength 'graveyard' tag, so the commander's central recursion engine is undervalued relative to how much it defines the deck.

Amass / proliferate-style +1/+1 counter accumulation on a single Zombie Army (Eternal Skylord, Gleaming Overseer, Vizier of the Scorpion, Lazotep Reaver, Enter the God-Eternals) is only partly captured as 'tokens/counters' â€” the real synergy is repeatedly growing one Army token, which the taxonomy has no event for.

Self-mill-into-payoff is underweighted: cards like Mire Triton/Geralf's Mindcrusher/Pilfered Plans milling to enable reanimation (Gravespawn Sovereign, Necromantic Selection, Zombie Apocalypse) and Lotleth Giant's damage are a real fill-yardâ†’exploit line that reads as flat strength-1 graveyard noise.
ETB-recursion of Havengul Lich (recast creatures from any graveyard each turn) is tagged only as a graveyard consumer; its engine-y repeatable value is missed.
issues: rampâ†’sink family over-triggers on devotion/board-scaling finishers (Gray Merchant) and on creatures whose only 'sink' is their fixed casting cost (Standard Bearer), inflating edge count with non-synergies.

No dedicated event for Gisa-style 'cast creature type from any graveyard' or for Havengul Lich-style repeatable recursion â€” these collapse into a generic, strength-1 graveyard tag and are undervalued.
combatâ†’payoff family fires too broadly: it links a combat-damage token-maker (Curse of Disturbance) to every token producer rather than to things that actually enable the attack trigger.
No amass/single-army-grows-by-counters event, so a whole sub-theme of the precon is only loosely modeled via generic tokens+counters tags.

islandCards correctly flags interaction-light cards (Murder, Undermine, Feed the Swarm, Crippling Fear, Wayfarer's Bauble) but also lists Distant Melody and Grimoire of the Dead as islands when both are real tribal/graveyard payoffs â€” Distant Melody draws per Zombie and Grimoire mass-reanimates, so the taxonomy missed both.

rawEvals.name: Silverquill Influence (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mono-themed W/B Auras "enchantress" voltron with a goad/politics combat shell. It plays a critical mass of cheap Auras and enchantments plus enchantress-style payoffs (Kor Spiritdancer, Sram, Sage's Reverie, Hateful Eidolon, Doomwake Giant, Pearl-Ear/Tomik typecount payoffs) that draw cards and grow as you cast enchantments, then suits up a creature (Eldrazi Conscription, Angelic Destiny, Shielded by Faith, Gift of Immortality protection) and swings. The secondary engine is goad/forced-combat politics (the Impetus auras, Breena, Mangara, Coercive Impetus, Combat Calligrapher) plus lifeloss/lifegain attrition (Eriette, Killian commander triggers). It wins through enchanted-creature beatdown and incidental drain, not a combo.
fp: Sage's Reverie â‡„ Nils/Inkshield/Keen Duelist/Eriette via 'tribal-countâ†’payoff' â€” Sage's Reverie counts Auras/enchantments YOU control for its draw, so pairing it with non-enchantment creatures like Keen Duelist or instant Inkshield as a typecount payoff is spurious; they don't feed it.

Hateful Eidolon â‡„ many bodies via 'tribal-countâ†’payoff' â€” Hateful Eidolon scales off Auras specifically and draws on enchanted-creature death/lifeloss; linking it to generic creatures (Combat Calligrapher, Keen Duelist, Eriette) as a count payoff is a false edge.
Killian, Decisive Mentor â‡„ Firemane Commando / Eiganjo Dynastorian via 'attack' (s1) â€” these are weak generic 'something attacks' links rather than a designed synergy.
rampâ†’sink edges (Sol Ring/Arcane Signet/Fellwar/Talisman â‡„ Nils/Eriette/Herald of Amity) â€” generic mana-rock to mana-sink pairings that exist in almost any deck; not a real engine here.
tribal-countâ†’payoff treated as creature-tribal when it is actually enchantment-permanent-count â€” a family-labeling false positive across ~141 edges.
fn: Lifegain/lifeloss DRAIN payoff axis â€” Killian's commander lifeloss trigger, Eriette of the Charmed Apple (lifegainâ†’opponents lose), Hateful Eidolon, Parasitic Impetus, Sheltered by Ghosts; there is no lifegain-payoff / drain family tying these together as a win/attrition line.

Auras-voltron protection & recursion package â€” Shielded by Faith, Gift of Immortality, Flickering Ward (re-equip dodge removal), Spirit Mantle/Darksteel Mutation as protection/answers; the deck's resilience engine (re-suiting a single threat) is not modeled as its own family.

Aura reanimation/value loop â€” Animate Dead, Changing Loyalty, Screams from Within, Forum Filibuster, Eiganjo Dynastorian // Replenish (mass enchantment recursion) form a graveyard-enchantment recursion subtheme only weakly touched by scattered 'reanimate'/'graveyard' edges.
Counters/+1+1 subtheme â€” Scriv, Intermediate Chirography, Nils, Shadrix, Breena, Starfield Mystic all make counters but there is no counters-matters family connecting them.
issues: 'tribal-countâ†’payoff' family is mislabeled and over-applied: it links typecount payoffs to ALL nonland cards rather than only the cards of the counted type (enchantments), producing ~141 edges and badly inflating cohesion/degree.
enchantress family creates near-complete bipartite blowup (every enchantment â‡„ every payoff) so degree and largestWebShare are driven by card-count, not genuine distinct synergy density.
No lifegain/lifeloss/drain payoff event despite it being a real W/B attrition line (Eriette, Killian, Hateful Eidolon).
No protection/totem-armor or aura-recursion family to capture voltron resilience engines.
No counters-matters family; counter producers are left disconnected on that axis.
islandCards correctly flagged removal/draw spells (Anguished Unmaking, Vanishing Verse, Secret Rendezvous, Promise of Loyalty) as non-synergistic, which is appropriate.

rawEvals.name: Witherbloom Pestilence (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Golgari (B/G) aristocrats / sacrifice-drain midrange. The deck makes a wide board of expendable tokens (Pest tokens, Saprolings via Tendershoot Dryad / Creakwood Liege / Awakening Zone / Pawn of Ulamog / Jadar / Ophiomancer / Gyome), throws them at free sac outlets (Viscera Seer, Yahenni, Woe Strider, Wight of the Reliquary, Umbral Collar Zealot, Priest of Forgotten Gods, Dina Soul Steeper), and wins through incremental death-drain payoffs (Blood Artist, Zulaport Cutthroat, Blight Mound, Teacher's Pest) plus a lifegain-to-damage axis (Dina Essence Brewer pings + ramps on lifegain; Dina Soul Steeper converts gained life into drain; Beledros Witherbloom as a life/untap engine). Secondary plan is a tokens-go-wide beatdown with Blossoming Bogbeast/Stensian/Defiling Daemogoth as combat finishers.
fp: Most lifegain edges are spurious co-occurrence, not synergy: e.g. Witch of the Moors â‡„ Haywire Mite (f:lifegain), Trudge Garden â‡„ Teacher's Pest (lifegain), Mortality Spear â‡„ Ribtruss Roaster (lifegain) â€” these cards both happen to gain/care-about life but do nothing for each other; only the two Dinas, Beledros, and Veinwitch are real lifegain payoffs.

lordâ†’tribe inflated by miscast lords: Ohran Frostfang is keyword-granting not a tribal anthem yet is tagged is-typed-lord (e.g. Ohran Frostfang â‡„ Creakwood Liege f:lordâ†’tribe), and Blight Mound / Feral Appetite / Tendershoot are Saproling/Pest payoffs not generic lords feeding a tribe.
Gorma, the Gullet â‡„ Blight Mound and Gorma â‡„ Feral Appetite tagged lordâ†’tribe (s:2) is wrong â€” Gorma is a counters/lifegain creature, not part of a token tribe with those enchantments.

counter-multiplier edges like Mycoloth â‡„ Merchant of Venom / Ribtruss Roaster (s:3) overstate a +1/+1 counters subtheme that is incidental; these cards don't actually multiply each other's counters.
rampâ†’sink treating Sol Ring / Arcane Signet â‡„ Immoral Bargain or Defiling Daemogoth as synergy is generic 'mana exists, sink exists' co-occurrence, not deck-specific synergy.
fn: Recursive/self-returning sac fodder as an engine: Bloodghast (returns on landfall) and Nether Traitor (returns when a black creature comes in) are the repeatable fuel that powers the aristocrats loop â€” the taxonomy has no 'recurs-itself â†’ outlet' family, so it only sees them as ordinary one-shot fodder.

Wight of the Reliquary + Bloodghast interaction: Wight sacs a creature to fetch a land, and the land drop returns Bloodghast â€” a near-loop of fodder generation that is invisible to the classifier (only a plain sac-fodder edge is drawn).

Beledros Witherbloom's untap-lands â†’ repeatable big-mana / repeatable token engine is only captured as scattered untapâ†’tap-ability edges to mana rocks; the real payoff (paying 10 life every turn to untap all lands for a mana-sink go-off) has no representative family.

Token-doubler / token-multiplier effects (Mycoloth devour, Creakwood Liege making 1/1s) feeding a go-wide aristocrats plan are not modeled as a token-amplification family distinct from generic 'tokens'.
issues: lifegain family is over-weighted and forms a near-complete subgraph (125 edges): it should require a genuine lifegain PAYOFF on one endpoint (Dina Soul Steeper, Beledros, Veinwitch) rather than pairing any two lifegain-producing cards.
No 'self-recurring fodder' / persistent sac-engine event in the taxonomy â€” this is the single biggest miss for aristocrats decks (Bloodghast, Nether Traitor, Reassembling Skeleton-style cards).
is-typed-lord is applied too loosely: keyword-granters (Ohran Frostfang) and narrow token-payoffs get tagged as lords, inflating lordâ†’tribe count (43).
counter-multiplier strength is too aggressive (s:3 on Mycoloth/Gorma pairings) for a deck where +1/+1 counters are a minor incidental subtheme, not a build-around.

Three islandCards (Cultivate, Night's Whisper, Plumb the Forbidden) are correctly isolated, but Plumb the Forbidden actually wants creatures to sacrifice for cards in an aristocrats shell â€” its 'sac creatures to draw' text is a real outlet/draw synergy the parser dropped, a minor false negative within an otherwise-correct island call.

rawEvals.name: Token Triumph (Starter Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Selesnya (GW) go-wide token swarm. Flood the board with creature tokens (Emmara, Selesnya Evangel/Guildmage, Jade Mage, Scatter the Seeds, March of the Multitudes, White Sun's Zenith, Hornet Queen, Verdant Force, Trostani), buff the team with anthems/lords (Dictate of Heliod, Collective Blessing, Commander's Insignia, Trostani), and close with an overrun-style mass-pump alpha strike (Overrun, Thunderfoot Baloth, Camaraderie, Valor in Akros, Ajani). Card advantage scales off the wide board (Slate of Ancestry, Collective Unconscious, Mentor of the Meek, Dawn of Hope, Overwhelming Instinct). No combo; it just wins via combat with a buffed token army.
fp: Rootborn Defenses â‡„ Eternal Witness (copyâ†’trigger) â€” populate copies a token you control, it does NOT re-trigger Eternal Witness's ETB
Rootborn Defenses â‡„ Farhaven Elf / Great Oak Guardian / Reclamation Sage / Voice of Many / Rishkar (copyâ†’trigger) â€” same error: populate cannot copy these nontoken creatures or their ETBs

Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Talisman of Unity / Commander's Sphere â‡„ Hour of Reckoning / Nissa's Expedition / Harvest Season / Devouring Light / Conclave Tribunal (rampâ†’sink) â€” generic 'mana can pay for spells', true of any deck, not a token synergy; ~170 such edges inflate the graph
Avacyn's Pilgrim / Leafkin Druid / Jaspera Sentinel â‡„ assorted X-spells (rampâ†’sink) â€” same generic mana-to-cost padding rather than a real engine
fn: Convoke as an Emmara enabler â€” March of the Multitudes and Scatter the Seeds convoke by TAPPING creatures (including Emmara), and Emmara makes a token each time she becomes tapped; there is no convokeâ†’token-producer / tap-commander event so this signature line is essentially unmodeled
Curse of Bounty / untap effects re-tapping Emmara for repeated token triggers is only weakly flagged (single s:1 'tap' edge) rather than recognized as an Emmara value loop
Selesnya Guildmage's repeatable token-pump activation as a token-producer + go-wide payoff in one card is partly flattened into generic go-wide edges
Populate as a 'copy your best token' payoff (Hornet Queen swarm, Verdant Force) is conflated with the bogus ETB-copy reading rather than recognized as a width multiplier
issues: rampâ†’sink is massively over-weighted (170 edges, the largest family) via full bipartite fan-out of every mana source to every expensive/X spell; this is generic and inflates cohesion across essentially all green/ramp decks

lordâ†’tribe and go-wideâ†’payoff form dense all-anthems Ă— all-creatures cliques (avgDeg 16 vs weightedAvgDeg 6.57), so degree-based metrics over-state engine tightness for what is a simple linear deck
copyâ†’trigger family does not distinguish populate (copy a token) from clone/ETB-copy effects, producing systematic false positives on populate cards
Taxonomy lacks a convoke event and a 'becomes tapped'â†’trigger event, missing the commander's defining mechanic
hasCombo=false is correct, but the cohesion number leans high because mechanical fan-out (rampâ†’sink + anthem cliques) substitutes for genuine synergy density

rawEvals.name: Jeskai Striker (Tarkir Dragonstorm Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Jeskai spellslinger / magecraft midrange. You cast a high density of cheap-to-midrange instants and sorceries to trigger spellcast payoffs (Guttersnipe, Young Pyromancer, Monastery Mentor, Archmage Emeritus, Storm-Kiln Artist, Veyran, Third Path Iconoclast, Caldera Pyremaw, Adaptive Training Post), generating tokens, card draw, Treasure/mana, and direct damage. Veyran/Elsha/Shiko double or copy those spell triggers. It wins either by going wide with prowess/spirit/elemental tokens that get buffed (Monastery Mentor, Aligned Heart, Elsha) and swinging, by burn from pinger payoffs, or by ramping into a huge X/copy payoff (Electrodominance, Expansion//Explosion, Mana Geyser, Storm-Kiln Treasures). Treasure + cost-reduction (Goblin Electromancer, Baral and Kari Zev, Haughty Djinn) fuels chaining multiple spells per turn.
fp: rampâ†’sink edges from mana rocks (Sol Ring, Arcane/Izzet/Boros/Azorius Signet, Fellwar Stone, Talisman) to Manaform Hellkite / Curse of the Swine / Electrodominance are treated as deck synergy, but ramp feeding a spell is universal goodstuff, not a deck-defining interaction â€” these edges pad cohesion in any blue deck
Curse of the Swine flagged as a mana-sink consuming ramp: it is X-cost removal, not a payoff/sink the deck is built around, so rampâ†’sink to it is a weak/incidental edge

combatâ†’payoff edges anchored on Curse of Opulence / Shiny Impetus to creatures like Young Pyromancer, Third Path Iconoclast, Mangara are mostly incidental â€” these Auras attach to opponents and the treasure/goad is not really a synergy with each token-maker individually

Swords to Plowshares / Pongify / Abrade / Vanquish the Horde shown as magecraft 'synergies' with payoffs is technically true (they are noncreature spells) but conflates removal-as-spell-fuel with intentional engine pieces, slightly overcounting
fn: Spell-copy as a payoff multiplier is under-modeled: Shiko and Narset, Veyran (doubles magecraft triggers), and Elsha (cast off top, copy) interact directly with the magecraft payoffs to DOUBLE their output â€” this 'copy/doubler â†’ spellcast-payoff' relationship is the deck's biggest synergy and is largely flattened into generic magecraft edges rather than recognized as a trigger-multiplier family

Cost reduction as a chaining enabler is missed: Goblin Electromancer and Haughty Djinn are ISLANDS (deg 0) despite being core engine pieces â€” reducing instant/sorcery cost is what lets you cast multiple spells per turn to fuel every magecraft payoff; the taxonomy has 'cost-reductionâ†’ability' but never connected these to the spell payoffs, a real false negative

Lier, Disciple of the Drowned is an island but is a genuine engine: it gives all your instants/sorceries flashback, letting you re-cast the graveyard for more magecraft triggers â€” graveyard-recastâ†’spellcast-payoff is unrecognized
Velomachus Lorehold (island) cheats free instants/sorceries on attack, directly feeding the magecraft/copy engine â€” its attack-trigger free-cast is missed entirely

Storm-Kiln Artist + Veyran + cheap cantrips is a near-combo Treasure loop (each spell makes a Treasure, doubled by Veyran, fueling more spells); the classifier sees the magecraft and treasure edges but never escalates this to a combo/loop, so hasCombo=false and loops=0 slightly understates the engine ceiling
issues: No 'trigger-doubler / copy â†’ spellcast-payoff' family: Veyran and Shiko literally double magecraft triggers, the single most important synergy, yet it is absorbed into flat magecraft edges with no strength escalation

cost-reduction capability (is-spell-cost-reducer) is detected on Goblin Electromancer/Haughty Djinn/Vanquish but produces NO edges to the spell payoffs it enables â€” the cost-reductionâ†’ability family appears unwired for spellslinger, leaving core enablers as islands

Graveyard-recast/flashback enablers (Lier) and free-cast-on-attack (Velomachus) have no event, so signature engine creatures fall out as islands â€” taxonomy gap for 'recast/extra-cast â†’ spellcast-payoff'

magecraft family is over-weighted via near-complete bipartite expansion (every noncreature spell x every payoff), inflating edge count (210) and avgDeg in a way that could overstate cohesion relative to decks whose synergies are sparser but more meaningful
rampâ†’sink fires on generic mana-rock-into-X-spell, which is not deck-specific and adds noise to cohesion across all blue/ramp decks

rawEvals.name: Vampiric Bloodline (Innistrad: Crimson Vow Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Vampire tribal aristocrats. Strefan, Maurer Progenitor pings creatures to make 1/1 Vampire tokens off attack, fueling a go-wide Vampire board buffed by lords (Stromkirk Captain, Bloodsworn Steward) and tribal payoffs (Malakir Bloodwitch, Champion of Dusk). It wins two ways: (1) combat damage from a wide, anthem-pumped Vampire team with evasion/first strike, and (2) an aristocrats drain loop â€” sac fodder (tokens, Vampires) into outlets (Indulgent Aristocrat, Laurine, Butcher of Malakir) with death-drain payoffs (Blood Artist, Falkenrath Noble, Sanctum Seeker, Cordial Vampire counters). +1/+1 counter and lifegain subthemes feed Crossway Troublemakers/Necropolis Regent. It is a casual midrange tribal precon, not a combo deck.
fp: rampâ†’sink edges (e.g. Arcane Signet â‡„ Olivia's Wrath, Sol Ring â‡„ Damnable Pact) â€” connecting every mana rock to every X-cost spell is generic mana goodstuff, not a deckbuilding synergy

sac-fodderâ†’outlet inflation: e.g. Vampire Nighthawk â‡„ Indulgent Aristocrat or Crimson Honor Guard â‡„ Laurine â€” these are nominal 'any creature can be sacrificed' edges, not intentional fodder pairings; 114 instances vastly overstates the aristocrats package
Molten Echoes â‡„ Shadowgrange Archfiend (copyâ†’trigger, s2) â€” Molten Echoes copies on ETB by creature type, a marginal interaction flagged as moderate

etbâ†’blink edges via Timothar, Baron of Bats (e.g. Champion of Dusk â‡„ Timothar at s3) â€” Timothar returns Vampires that died to its dice mechanic, it is not a reliable ETB-blink engine; strength 3 overstates it
fn: Strefan's signature ability â€” when a Vampire deals combat damage to a player, sac that Vampire's 'blood' to make a 1/1 token (his actual token engine) â€” is reduced to a generic 'tokens' edge and not modeled as the deck's central attack-trigger payoff

Sanctum Seeker / 'whenever a Vampire attacks' and Cordial Vampire 'whenever a Vampire dies, +1/+1 counter' triggered tribal payoffs have no dedicated tribe-trigger-on-event family â€” folded into generic combat/counters
Lifegain-as-resource: Crossway Troublemakers, Vampire Nighthawk, Bloodline Necromancer lifegain has no payoff family (no lifegainâ†’drain/draw event), so the deck's lifegain subtheme is invisible
Blood token mechanic (Bloodtithe Harvester, Glass-Cast Heart, Arterial Alchemy) is treated only as generic 'tokens'/discard, not as a card-filtering/fodder engine
issues: sac-fodderâ†’outlet has no gating on whether fodder is intentional â€” it fully connects every body to every sac outlet, producing 114 edges and dominating the graph; should be down-weighted when fodder is just incidental creatures
rampâ†’sink (63 edges) is a generic goodstuff family that rewards any rock + any X-spell; inflates cohesion without representing real synergy
No 'attack-trigger tribal payoff' or 'lifegain payoff' event in the taxonomy, so attacks-matter (Sanctum Seeker) and lifegain-matters lines are missed
Commander's signature mechanic (Strefan combat-damageâ†’token) is not specially modeled despite being the deck's namesake engine
hasCombo=false and no comboCriticalPairs is correct â€” this is a fair-deck precon; the gate did not over-fire here

rawEvals.name: Timey-Wimey (Doctor Who Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Jeskai suspend/time-counter superfriends-midrange with a tokens-go-wide sub-theme
fp: The Eleventh Hour â‡„ Crack in Time / Atraxi Warden / Grasp of Fate / Out of Time (copyâ†’trigger): Eleventh Hour copies an instant/sorcery or makes Doctor tokens; pairing it with arbitrary ETB enchantments/permanents as 'copy targets' is not a real synergy

All of History, All at Once â‡„ Crack in Time / Grasp of Fate / Atraxi Warden / Adipose (copyâ†’trigger): All of History copies spells/abilities by suspend-recast, not these specific ETB permanents â€” these edges are spurious

As Foretold / Adipose / Idris / Wilfred â‡„ every mana rock (Sol Ring, Mind Stone, Talismans, Thought Vessel, Arcane Signet) as rampâ†’sink: most are generic-rock-meets-generic-payoff noise inflating the count to 47, not deliberate engine pairs
Donna Noble â‡„ Coward // Killer / Gallifrey Falls (damage): these are unrelated burn/removal spells, not a damage-synergy package with Donna
The Parting of the Ways â‡„ Everybody Lives! (destroy) and Everybody Lives! â‡„ The Moment (destroy): coincidental shared 'destroy' tag, not an intentional combo
fn: TIME COUNTERS / SUSPEND is the entire deck and has NO family: The Tenth Doctor's attack trigger removing time counters, Rose Tyler, As Foretold, Clockspinning, Inspiring Refrain (suspend), Wibbly-wobbly Timey-wimey, The Wedding of River Song, All of History All at Once, Out of Time, The Moment, Time Beetle â€” these all interact through time/finality counters and are split across islands/unrelated families

FINALITY / VANISHING / counter-manipulation: Clockspinning (island, deg 0) literally proliferates/manipulates time counters and is the strongest enabler for the Doctor's plan, yet is flagged as a zero-interaction island

'Doctor's companion'/attack-trigger payoff: The Tenth Doctor consumes 'attack' but the model only connected attackâ†’Star Whale lord, missing that attacking is the activation key for the whole suspend-acceleration line

Recast-from-exile / suspend payoff engine: cards like The Day of the Doctor and Fractured Identity (both islands) work with the exile/copy/recast theme but the taxonomy has no event for cast-from-exile or suspend-payoff
issues: No time-counter / suspend / vanishing family in the taxonomy â€” fatal for any set built on these mechanics (Doctor Who); the deck's defining engine scores zero
copyâ†’trigger family is too loose: it treats any 'is-copy' card paired with any 'has-etb' permanent as a synergy, generating false positives
rampâ†’sink is wildly over-weighted via combinatorial pairing of every rock with every sink (47 edges), inflating familyBreakdown and masking which sinks actually matter
Counter-manipulation cards (Clockspinning, has-counters tag) produce no events, so key enablers land on the island list despite being central

Cohesion 14 ('Pile of good-stuff') is too low: the deck has a genuine, designed engine (suspend/time counters) that the model is structurally blind to, so it under-credits a precon that is actually moderately cohesive

rawEvals.name: Sliver Swarm (Commander Masters Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: 5-color Sliver tribal "go-wide" aggro/midrange. Roughly 40 Slivers share keyword/anthem buffs via typed lords (Bonescythe, Blade, Sinew, Megantic, Cleaving, Might Sliver, etc.), ramp through mana Slivers (Gemhide, Manaweft) plus rocks, and win by overwhelming the table with a collectively-buffed, evasive, haste-enabled swarm. Sliver Gravemother adds a graveyard/recursion angle (encore-style flashback + copying tokens) but the deck fundamentally functions as a snowballing tribal board built on shared abilities, not a combo deck.
fp: The 595 lordâ†’tribe edges are massively over-counted: it draws an edge from every typed-lord to every Sliver AND counts lord-to-lord pairs, turning one mechanic (anthem sharing) into a near-complete clique that fakes avgDeg 19.78 and 84% web share â€” non-lord Slivers like Crypt/Clot/Sinew/Winged appear in dozens of edges purely as buff recipients, not as distinct synergies

Sliver Gravemother â‡„ Sol Ring / Fellwar Stone / Arcane Signet (rampâ†’sink): generic 'mana sink' tagging; the commander is not a meaningful mana sink that pays off ramping into rocks â€” this is filler ramp, not a synergy line
Capricious Sliver / Realmwalker / For the Ancestors â‡„ Vanquisher's Banner (cast): tenuous 'cast a creature' coincidence rather than a built engine

Cleansing Nova / Decimate / Harsh Mercy â‡„ Sliver Hivelord (destroy): Hivelord granting indestructible to Slivers is a real protective interaction, but pairing it with the deck's OWN wraths as a 'destroy' synergy mislabels a defensive keyword as an offensive interaction

Brood Sliver â‡„ Synapse Sliver / Titan of Littjara go-wideâ†’payoff edges are real-ish but redundantly stacked on top of the combat-enabler/lord noise, double-representing the same 'attack with tokens' line
fn: Sliver ability-SHARING as a family: the defining Sliver mechanic is that each Sliver grants its printed ability to ALL Slivers (Gemhide/Manaweft mana to every Sliver, Galerider flying, Cloudshredder flying+haste, Quick Sliver flash, Crystalline shroud, Striking first strike, Venom deathtouch, Diffusion ward). The taxonomy has no 'keyword-grantâ†’tribe' event and lumps all of it into generic lordâ†’tribe, missing the actual combinatorial payoff

Mana-Sliver ramp SCALING: Gemhide + Manaweft make EVERY Sliver (and untapper) tap for mana, so each additional creature ramps â€” a genuine engine with go-wide token producers (Brood, Lazotep) that is invisible here

Sliver Gravemother graveyard recursion / encore: casting Slivers from graveyard and copying tokens â€” the commander's signature line and the only reason the deck is in these colors â€” has no graveyard/recursion family edge at all
Lifegain payoff loop: Syphon Sliver gives all Slivers lifelink and Lavabelly drains on ETB; with a wide board this is meaningful incremental drain, not represented
Goad/political: Taunting Sliver (goad) and Regal Sliver (monarch) are detected as capabilities but produce no political-pressure edges to the go-wide combat plan
issues: lordâ†’tribe is allowed to dominate the graph (595/630 edges) and is counted as a full clique including lord-to-lord pairs, hugely inflating avgDeg, weightedAvgDeg, and largestWebShare for any tribal deck â€” tribal lords should likely be capped or counted as a shared-hub rather than a dense clique
No taxonomy event for keyword/ability granting to a creature type (the core Sliver/Changeling mechanic), so a deck whose entire identity is shared abilities reads as plain 'tribal anthem'
No graveyard-recursion / cast-from-graveyard family despite a commander whose text is built on it; Gravemother's 'graveyard' consume tag produces zero edges
rampâ†’sink fires on generic 'is-mana-sink' commander tags, producing low-value rockâ†’commander edges that pad the interactive %

9 islandCards include real ramp (Farseek, Nature's Lore, Three Visits, Cultivate) and real tribal payoffs (Distant Melody draws per Sliver, Icon of Ancestry is a literal Sliver anthem+recursion, Crippling Fear is a tribal-asymmetric wrath) â€” these are core synergy pieces wrongly flagged as non-interactive because the taxonomy lacks 'fetch-land ramp' and 'tribal-count payoff' events

rawEvals.name: Elven Empire (Kaldheim Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Golgari (B/G) Elf tribal "go-wide" deck led by Lathril, Blade of the Elves. It floods the board with Elf tokens via token-makers (Imperious Perfect, Lys Alana Huntmaster, Elvish Promenade, Elven Ambush, Wolverine Riders, Cultivator of Blades, Prowess of the Fair) and lords (Imperious Perfect, Dwynen, Elvish Archdruid, Canopy Tactician, Pride of the Perfect, Numa), then converts the wide board into wins two ways: (1) Lathril's tap-ability to drain each opponent for the number of Elves tapped and make 1/1 tokens, and (2) mass-damage/drain payoffs that scale with Elf count â€” Shaman of the Pack and Skemfar Shadowsage (drain = Elves), Jagged-Scar Archers (fight/ping = Elf count), plus Elderfang Venom and Poison-Tip Archer as death-drain. A heavy Elf mana-dork suite (Elvish Mystic, Llanowar Tribe, Marwyn, Wirewood Channeler, Jaspera Sentinel, Archdruid) ramps into expensive sinks and an End-Raze Forerunners overrun finisher. Secondary aristocrats/recursion subtheme in black (Return Upon the Tide, Moldervine Reclamation, Golgari Findbroker).
fp: Lathril â‡„ Return Upon the Tide / Wolverine Riders / Eyeblight Cullers / Lys Alana Huntmaster / Cultivator of Blades tagged 'combatâ†’payoff' (s1) â€” these are just other token-makers; there is no real combat synergy edge with Lathril, the link is incidental co-membership in the go-wide plan, not a directed interaction

rampâ†’sink edges from mana dorks (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Elvish Mystic) to Timberwatch Elf and Lys Alana Scarblade are weak/generic â€” every mana source trivially 'feeds' every activated ability, so these s1 edges pad the graph without representing a tuned synergy

The fully-connected tribal-countâ†’payoff mesh (e.g. Elven Ambush â‡„ Springbloom Druid, Crown of Skemfar â‡„ Wood Elves) treats any Elf as a 'payoff' for any token source; most of these pairs are members of the same critical mass, not pairwise synergies, so the 353 instance count is heavily inflated
fn: Lathril's own tap-ability (tap 10 Elves to drain each opponent for the Elf count AND make twice that many tokens) is the deck's marquee combo/payoff and was NOT modeled as a tribal-countâ†’payoff or commander-sink edge â€” Lathril is treated as a generic body, missing the deck's primary kill
Elf-count anthem/scaling on Timberwatch Elf (tap: +X/+X equal to Elf count) is reduced to a generic rampâ†’sink mana-consumer; its 'count Elves' payoff nature is lost
Elvish Archdruid taps for mana equal to number of Elves â€” a tribal-countâ†’ramp engine â€” is only seen as lord/ramp, not as count-scaling mana that snowballs the whole board

Recursion/aristocrats subtheme (Return Upon the Tide reanimation, Moldervine Reclamation, Golgari Findbroker, Eyeblight Cullers self-mill) is under-modeled â€” Moldervine Reclamation is a 0-degree island despite being a sac-payoff card-draw engine
issues: No 'commander tap-ability as tribal-count payoff' detection: Lathril, the card the deck is built around, registers only generic combat/lord edges, so the model can describe an Elf deck but not its actual win condition

tribal-countâ†’payoff is over-weighted by full pairwise meshing â€” token-makers and Elves are all cross-linked, so a 73-card tribal precon racks up 353 instances and 567 edges, making cohesion sensitive to deck SIZE/tribe purity rather than genuine engine quality

'is-typecount-payoff' capability exists (Elvish Archdruid, Jagged-Scar Archers, Crown of Skemfar) but is not used to scale mana/pump payoffs differently from token-count payoffs â€” count-of-X-for-mana vs count-of-X-for-damage are conflated

Island detection is reasonable here (interaction/removal like Putrefy, Casualties of War, Eyeblight Massacre, Binding the Old Gods correctly islanded), but Moldervine Reclamation and Serpent's Soul-Jar are mis-islanded â€” both are real engine pieces (sac-draw payoff; token storage/recursion) with no event in the taxonomy

rawEvals.name: First Flight (Starter Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Azorius fliers-tribal go-wide midrange/control
fp: Isperia, Supreme Judge â‡„ Hanged Executioner / Storm Herd / Migratory Route / Sharding Sphinx / Bident / Ever-Watching / Windreader (combatâ†’payoff): Isperia's draw triggers when an OPPONENT attacks with one or more creatures (defensive Coastal Piracy on the enemy), NOT when YOUR creatures attack. Tagging her as 'is-combat-payoff' fed by the deck's own go-wide attackers inverts how the card works.

Kangee's Lieutenant â‡„ Banishing Light (copyâ†’trigger, s2): Banishing Light's ETB exiles a permanent; copying a Bird's-ETB-style trigger does not meaningfully 'copyâ†’trigger' an enchantment removal effect here â€” and Kangee's Lieutenant copies an ETB it shares, not Banishing Light's. This is a spurious enabler edge.

Kangee's Lieutenant â‡„ Pilgrim's Eye / Skyscanner / Cloudblazer / Sphinx of Enlightenment / Inspired Sphinx (copyâ†’trigger, s2): these are real-ish (doubling a small ETB) but mostly trivial value, not synergy worth s2 weighting; many are marginal one-shot ETBs that barely move the deck.
Angler Turtle â‡„ Ever-Watching Threshold (goad) and Bident â‡„ Ever-Watching (goad): goad here is incidental, not a goad-payoff package â€” there is no goad engine in the deck.

Archon of Redemption â‡„ Time Wipe / Aetherize / Cartographer's Hawk (bounce): Archon's trigger is lifegain on flyers dying/entering, not a bounce payoff; pairing it with board-bounce spells is not a real synergy line.
fn: FLYING / Birds tribal lords & anthems â€” the deck's actual core â€” are almost entirely missed: Favorable Winds (deg 0, island), Gravitational Shift (deg 0, island), Empyrean Eagle (deg 1) all pump every flyer, and Skycat Sovereign / Steel-Plume Marshal / Thunderclap Wyvern / Warden of Evos Isle / Kangee are flyer-matters. There is no 'flying anthem â†’ flyer' or evasion-tribe lordâ†’tribe family, so these landed in islandCards or as degree-0/1 dead nodes.

Evasion-as-enabler: Windreader Sphinx, Tide Skimmer, Bident of Thassa, Ever-Watching Threshold, Staggering Insight all reward COMBAT DAMAGE specifically, which flying reliably delivers. The classifier has no 'evasion enables combat-damage payoff' link, so the flyer-anthems never connect to the draw payoffs they actually power.

Staggering Insight (deg 0, island): a curiosa/aura granting flying+lifelink+draw-on-damage â€” a textbook payoff-aura in a flyers deck â€” completely orphaned because auras/attach aren't linked to combat-damage draw payoffs.

Sephara, Sky's Blade cost/affinity to flyers and her flyer-protection (sacrifice/indestructible to your flyers) is missed; she only shows up via 'destroy' edges (board wipes) rather than as a go-wide flyers payoff/protector.
issues: No flying/evasion tribe family: there is no 'evasion-lordâ†’tribe' or 'anthemâ†’flyers' event, so a deck whose entire identity is flyers reads as generic combat â€” pushing flyer lords/anthems (Favorable Winds, Gravitational Shift, Empyrean Eagle) into islandCards.

tribal-countâ†’payoff is over-collapsed onto a single hub: 28 of the edges route through Aven Gagglemaster alone (it counts creatures for lifegain). The classifier represents 'tribal' as one card's ETB count, inflating avgDeg and masking that there is no broad creature-type synergy web.

Commander combat-payoff direction error: 'is-combat-payoff' does not distinguish 'when YOU attack' from 'when an OPPONENT attacks' â€” Isperia is a defensive draw engine but is wired as an offensive go-wide payoff.

No 'combat-damage / evasion â†’ on-damage draw' linkage: payoffs like Windreader Sphinx and Tide Skimmer that specifically reward connecting in combat are not tied to the evasion sources that make them reliable.
rampâ†’sink is somewhat inflated: 16 edges, but the only true X-sinks are Storm Herd and Sphinx's Revelation; every mana rock gets an edge to both, double-counting a thin payoff base.

rawEvals.name: Cavalry Charge (March of the Machine Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Orzhov (W/B) Knights tribal â€” a go-wide token/attack deck with an aristocrats drain sub-theme. It floods the board with Knight tokens (Adeline, Silverwing Squadron, Hero of Bladehold, Worthy Knight, Sigiled Sword), pumps them with anthems/lords (Knight Exemplar, Valiant Knight, Arvad, Herald of Hoofbeats, Wintermoor Commander), and converts the wide board into damage via combat payoffs (Chivalric Alliance, Exsanguinator Cavalry) and death-triggered life drain (Locthwain Lancer, Corpse Knight, Knights' Charge, Elenda and Azor, Smitten Swordmaster). It wins by combat damage from a buffed wide board plus incidental Orzhov lifedrain; the commander Sidar provides evasion/card advantage by attacking and recurs creatures from graveyard.
fp: Every mana rock â‡„ every X-spell/mana-sink (e.g. Sol Ring â‡„ Pull from Tomorrow, Orzhov Signet â‡„ Painful Truths, Fellwar Stone â‡„ Elenda and Azor) flagged as rampâ†’sink â€” these are generic good-stuff staples, not a deliberate rampâ†’payoff synergy line; 40 such edges are noise

Vona, Butcher of Magan / Aryel â‡„ Unbreakable Formation under 'destroy' â€” Unbreakable Formation is an anthem/indestructible combat trick, not a removal synergy partner for these destroy effects; the shared 'destroy' family is spurious

Knight Exemplar / Wintermoor Commander â‡„ Time Wipe & Fell the Mighty under 'destroy' â€” these are symmetric board wipes the deck does not want to point at its own lords; flagging them as a destroy synergy is wrong

Syr Konrad â‡„ Time Wipe / Fell the Mighty under 'sacrifice' â€” Konrad pings on creature death generally, but pairing him with one-sided/symmetric wraths as a 'sacrifice' engine overstates a real combo line
Herald's Horn â‡„ Read the Bones under 'scry' â€” Herald's Horn's scry-on-upkeep and Read the Bones' scry are unrelated incidental selection, not a synergy
fn: Sidar Jabari's combat-damage-triggered reanimation engine: when a Knight deals combat damage to a player you mill+reanimate, which is the commander's whole identity and a real attackâ†’graveyardâ†’reanimate line â€” not represented as a distinct family (only a generic 'graveyard' edge to Syr Konrad)

Anthem/static +1/+1 buffs as a go-wide multiplier: Knight Exemplar, Valiant Knight, Arvad, Herald of Hoofbeats give the token board lethal reach, but these are folded into generic lordâ†’tribe rather than recognized as anthemâ†’token-board payoff
Monarch / political card-advantage engine (Knights of the Black Rose grants monarch, Path of the Enigma) â€” no event family captures the monarch incidental draw engine

Equipment/Aura 'attach' payoffs on a wide creature base (Maul of the Skyclaves, Sigiled Sword of Valeron, Conjurer's Mantle) are only weakly captured via 'attach'/'combat-enabler'; the token-generation-on-equip line (Sigiled Sword makes a Knight when it attaches) is undercounted
issues: lordâ†’tribe and tribal-countâ†’payoff are O(n^2) over the tribe â€” 224 of 356 edges come from 'both cards are Knights', massively inflating avgDeg (11.34) and largestWebShare (92%) without reflecting distinct synergy lines

rampâ†’sink family fires on every mana-rock Ă— every mana-sink pair, generating dense noise (40 edges) from generic staples; it should require an intentional big-X or mana-hungry payoff, not any incidental sink
No event for commander-specific 'combat-damage â†’ reanimate/mill' lines, so the deck's defining engine (Sidar) is invisible

Symmetric/one-sided board wipes (Time Wipe, Fell the Mighty) are mis-grouped into 'destroy' synergy with the deck's own lords â€” the taxonomy doesn't distinguish 'helps me' destroy from 'hurts me' destroy
No monarch family despite monarch being a recurring incidental-advantage engine in Orzhov precons

rawEvals.name: Ahoy Mateys (The Lost Caverns of Ixalan Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Grixis Pirate tribal go-wide / combat-damage aggro, with a Treasure subtheme funding mana and a light graveyard-reanimation package from the commander. It wins by flooding the board with Pirates behind tribal lords (Admiral Beckett Brass, Corsair Captain), exploiting "deals combat damage to a player" triggers (the deck's true engine), generating Treasure for explosive turns, and using Admiral Brass to recur key Pirates from the yard. Combat-damage payoffs + go-wide + Treasure ramp are the spine; reanimation is a value rider, not a combo.
fp: Broadside Bombardiers â‡„ ~30 creatures (sac-fodderâ†’outlet): the classifier pairs the lone sac outlet with every body in the deck, generating 36 sac edges. In reality this deck has almost no sac payoff/aristocrats engine â€” Broadside just pings for value, so treating every creature as meaningful 'fodder' is noise, not synergy.

Lethal Scheme â‡„ Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Izzet Signet / Dimir Signet / Rakdos Signet (rampâ†’sink): convoke on a single removal spell is generic mana use, not a rampâ†’sink engine the deck is built around. Flagging each rock as a synergy edge is a false positive.

Captivating Crew / Coercive Recruiter / Merchant Raiders 'untapâ†’tap-ability' capability: these are threaten/tap-down effects, not part of any untap-for-value engine here; the untapper tag implies a combo-ish interaction that does not exist in this deck.
Vanquisher's Banner â‡„ Breeches/Hostage Taker/Ramirez (cast): these are just 'cast a creature' draw triggers off a tribal anthem â€” generic tribal value, mislabeled as a discrete cast-synergy edge.
fn: Pirate-specific raid / 'attacked this turn' triggers (Storm Fleet Negotiator, Kari Zev, Fathom Fleet Captain making tokens on attack) are partly captured under combatâ†’payoff but the raid mechanic itself has no dedicated event, so attack-trigger payoffs are conflated with damage payoffs.

Steal/threaten subtheme (Captivating Crew, Coercive Recruiter, Captain Lannery, Admiral Beckett Brass stealing on combat damage, Hostage Taker, Zara) â€” 'gain control of opponent's permanents' is a real recurring line here with NO family in the taxonomy; these cards show up only via lordâ†’tribe, missing their actual shared theme.

Monarch/political card-advantage axis (Azure Fleet Admiral monarch, Bident goad, Black Market Connections) â€” the deck's grindy card-advantage engine via monarch/goad is essentially invisible; monarch produces an event consumed by nothing.
Explore mechanic (Francisco, Storm Fleet Negotiator, Don Andres' renegade interactions) is barely modeled (explore:1) despite being an LCI signature value source.
issues: No 'steal/threaten/gain-control' family â€” a defining repeated theme of this Pirate set goes entirely unmodeled.

sac-fodderâ†’outlet is over-weighted by combinatorially pairing a single sac outlet with every creature, inflating edge/avgDeg counts; should be gated by presence of actual death/sacrifice payoffs (aristocrats), which this deck lacks.
No distinct 'raid/attack-trigger' event separate from combat-damage payoff; raid and saboteur effects are conflated.
Monarch and goad produce no consumable events, so a real political card-advantage engine scores zero.
Commander reanimation (Admiral Brass) is flattened to weak s1 graveyard edges, undervaluing the deck's namesake recursion line.

rawEvals.name: Enduring Enchantments (Commander Masters Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Abzan (GWB) enchantress midrange built around Anikthea, Hand of Erebos. The engine is a dense pile of constellation/enchantress draw payoffs (Eidolon of Blossoms, Enchantress's Presence, Mesa/Verduran/Satyr Enchanter, Sythis, Setessan Champion, Setessan-style payoffs) that refill the hand as you flood the board with enchantments. It wins by (1) going wide with token-makers (Heliod, Archon of Sun's Grace, Sigil of the Empty Throne, Battle for Bretagard, Dreadhorde Invasion) and sweeping opposing boards with Doomwake Giant constellation -1/-1; (2) bestow/aura beaters (Nyxborn Behemoth, Demon of Fate's Design, Boon of the Spirit Realm) that 'endure' through wraths; and (3) Anikthea recurring/copying enchantments from the graveyard as token enchantment creatures, supported by Starfield of Nyx, Calix, The Mending of Dominaria, Nyx Weaver self-mill. It is a value/grind deck, not a combo deck.
fp: The bulk of the 639 'enchantress' edges are mechanically real but quadratically over-counted: e.g. Eidolon of Blossoms â‡„ Nyxborn Behemoth, Eidolon â‡„ Dryad of the Ilysian Grove, Sigil of the Empty Throne â‡„ Khalni Heart Expedition are scored strength-3 'strong' pairwise synergies, but the payoff triggers off the global 'cast an enchantment' event, not off that specific partner â€” counting it as N-squared discrete strong edges inflates degree far beyond the real one-to-many relationship.

lordâ†’tribe edges like Boon of the Spirit Realm â‡„ Mirari's Wake or Heliod â‡„ Mirari's Wake / Sanctum Weaver treat mana-doubler/aura-anthem 'enchantment lords' as buffing each other; Mirari's Wake and Sanctum Weaver are mana sources, not creatures that benefit from an enchantment anthem, so several of these tribe edges are spurious.

rampâ†’sink edges Sol Ring â‡„ Narci, Fable Singer and Arcane Signet â‡„ Narci are weak/incidental: Narci is not a meaningful mana sink in this deck (her ability is a small activated draw), so flagging generic ramp into her as synergy is noise.
Ghoulish Impetus â‡„ Cunning Rhetoric 'goad' edge and the single 'goad' family instance overstate a political theme that barely exists (one goad aura) as deck synergy.
fn: No 'constellation' event exists in the taxonomy. The deck's defining mechanic â€” payoffs that trigger on ANY enchantment entering (Doomwake Giant's -1/-1 sweep, Archon of Sun's Grace tokens, Sigil tokens, Setessan Champion counters/draw) â€” is being shoehorned into 'enchantress' (cast-trigger) and undercounts ETB-on-enchantment triggers like Anikthea's own token copies feeding constellation.

The 'bestow / aura-on-creature' synergy (Nyxborn Behemoth, Demon of Fate's Design, Boon of the Spirit Realm, Ghoulish Impetus) is invisible â€” there is no event for 'enchantment that can attach to a creature and survive as an aura,' which is literally the 'Enduring' theme of the deck.

Anikthea's core combo line is missed: she reanimates an enchantment from the graveyard as a token copy, which itself is a new enchantment ETB that re-triggers constellation/enchantress payoffs (a recursive value loop). The classifier reports loops:0 and only weak strength-1 'reanimate'/'graveyard' edges, missing the engine that names the deck.

Self-mill enablers (Nyx Weaver, Mindwrack Harpy, The Binding of the Titans) feeding Anikthea/Starfield/Calix graveyard reanimation are only weakly linked via 'graveyard'; the millâ†’reanimate pipeline as a directed engine is under-modeled.
issues: Enchantress/constellation payoffs create O(payoffs x enchantments) strong edges, quadratically inflating avgDeg (25.16) and cohesion. A one-to-many board-watching trigger should be modeled as a hub/role bonus, not as a clique of pairwise strength-3 edges.
No 'constellation' or 'enchantment-ETB' event distinct from 'cast an enchantment' (enchantress); the two real but different triggers are collapsed.
No event for bestow/aura attachment, so the deck's titular 'Enduring' (aura-on-creature survives) theme is unrepresented.
Combo/loop detection (hasCombo:false, loops:0) misses Anikthea's recursive graveyard-copy-into-constellation value engine because graveyard/reanimate edges are only strength-1 and not chained.
lordâ†’tribe over-fires on noncreature 'enchantment lords' (Mirari's Wake, Sanctum Weaver) that don't actually receive the anthem buff.

rawEvals.name: Mishra's Burnished Banner (The Brothers' War Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Grixis artifacts-matter midrange ("Mishra big-artifacts / artifact aristocrats"). It generates and recurs cheap artifacts (Servo Schematic, Ichor Wellspring, Mycosynth Wellspring, treasures), abuses their ETB/death triggers, and Mishra copies an artifact each turn to double those triggers and bodies. It leverages mana rocks into big mana-sink payoffs (Metalwork Colossus, Hellkite Igniter, Muzzio, Geth, Terisiare's Devastation) and an artifact-aristocrats engine (sac outlets Slobad/Fain/Oni-Cult Anvil/Audacious Reshapers/Smelting Vat feeding death triggers + Geth reanimation). It mostly wins via a swarm of artifact tokens/Brudiclad token-doubling, big-mana beaters, and incidental drain.
fp: Generic sac-fodderâ†’outlet spam: e.g. Traxos, Scourge of Kroog â‡„ Smelting Vat and Jhoira â‡„ Oni-Cult Anvil â€” you would essentially never sacrifice these value/engine pieces to a random outlet; these are combinatorial fodderĂ—outlet edges, not real synergies.
Master Transmuter â‡„ every sac outlet (e.g. Master Transmuter â‡„ Smelting Vat): Master Transmuter bounces artifacts, it is not sacrifice fodder; tagging it as sac-fodder is a capability misread.
rampâ†’sink edges like Sol Ring â‡„ Glint Raker / Fellwar Stone â‡„ Geth treat every rock against every X-cost or mana-using card as a synergy edge â€” generic mana, not a deck-specific line.
Mishra â‡„ Metalwork Colossus / Mishra â‡„ Herald of Anguish tagged sac-fodderâ†’outlet: Mishra is a copier/token-maker, not a sac outlet, so these directionally mislabel the interaction.

copyâ†’trigger pairs with no relevant trigger, e.g. Prophetic Prism â‡„ Lithoform Engine and Mycosynth Wellspring â‡„ Strionic Resonator â€” copying a tapped-for-mana rock or a fetch-a-basic ETB is rarely a meaningful payoff.
fn: Artifact-count / metalcraft payoffs: Idol of Oblivion, Padeem (artifact lord/draw), Workshop Elders, Glint Raker scaling with artifacts, and Metalwork/Hellkite Igniter cost/size keyed to artifact count â€” there is no 'artifact-count matters' or 'affinity/cost-reduction-by-artifacts' family, so the deck's core 'go wide on artifacts' engine is invisible.

Treasure as a recurring ramp+fodder engine: many cards both make and consume treasure (Fain, Oni-Cult Anvil, Herald, Audacious Reshapers, Terisiare's Devastation) â€” treasure shows only 9 instances and is not modeled as a self-feeding ramp/sac loop.

Reanimation / recursion payoff: Geth reanimating artifacts from any graveyard and Metalwork Colossus/Spine of Ish Sah self-recurring from the yard is a real value engine, only partially captured by the generic 'graveyard' family.

Brudiclad token-transformation as a wincon: Brudiclad turning a swarm of artifact tokens into copies of a huge token is a near-combo finisher with the deck's token makers (Servo Schematic, Mirrorworks, Oni-Cult Anvil), modeled only as generic 'tokens' (9) edges.

Cost-reduction-by-artifacts: Emry and Thoughtcast/Thoughtcast-style affinity are flagged as is-spell-cost-reducer but there is no enablement family connecting 'many artifacts in play/yard' to those reductions.
issues: No 'artifact-count / affinity / metalcraft' family â€” for an artifacts-matter precon this is the single biggest taxonomy gap and drives the false-negative list.

sac-fodderâ†’outlet is computed as a near-complete bipartite product (fodder Ă— outlet) without gating on whether a card is actually expendable fodder vs. an engine piece; 244 instances grossly over-weight the count and inflate degree.
Capability misclassification: Master Transmuter (bounce) and Mishra/copiers tagged as sac-fodder or outlets; cards that produce 'leaves' on sacrifice get treated as fodder regardless of value.

Cohesion 19 ('Pile of good-stuff') is too low: this is a tightly themed mono-strategy artifact precon, not a goodstuff pile â€” but because the real glue (artifact-count, treasure loop, Brudiclad finish) sits in untaxonomized families, the score can't see the cohesion that the over-counted sac edges paradoxically failed to convert into a higher band.

hasCombo=false is defensible (no true infinite here), but the scoring conflates 'high raw edge count from generic fodder spam' with the genuinely synergistic copyâ†’trigger/treasure lines, so the headline number under-represents true cohesion.

rawEvals.name: Miracle Worker (Duskmourn Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Esper (WUB) enchantments / enchantress value midrange built around Aminatou, Veil Piercer. It floods the board with enchantments to trigger "whenever you cast an enchantment" payoffs (Mesa Enchantress, Secret Arcade, Sigil of the Empty Throne, Ondu Spiritdancer, Doomwake Giant) for card draw, angel tokens, and constellation -X/-X board control, then grinds out advantage via Aminatou's top-of-library cast engine, blink/flicker effects, and a reanimation sub-theme (Redress Fate, Spirit-Sister's Call, The Eldest Reborn, Athreos). It wins through incremental card/token advantage plus Sphere of Safety + token armies, not a combo.
fp: Aminatou, Veil Piercer â‡„ Redress Fate / Metamorphosis Fanatic / etc. (graveyard) â€” Aminatou's text is blink/flicker + casting off the top of library, NOT a graveyard/reanimation outlet; these graveyard edges misrepresent the commander entirely

Sigil of the Empty Throne â‡„ Mesa Enchantress / Ondu Spiritdancer â‡„ Doomwake Giant (enchantress) â€” payoff-to-payoff edges: two enchantresses don't synergize with EACH OTHER, they independently trigger off the same enchantment casts, so these clique edges double-count one cast trigger

Soaring Lightbringer â‡„ Monologue Tax / Entreat / Shark Typhoon (combatâ†’payoff s1) â€” weak/spurious: a lone flyer attacking has no real interaction with a tax enchantment or instant-speed shark maker
Athreos, Shroud-Veiled â‡„ Time Wipe and Time Wipe â‡„ Timely Ward (destroy) â€” incidental same-keyword matches, not intended synergy lines
fn: Aminatou's core blink/flicker + cast-from-exile / top-of-library engine has NO event family â€” the deck's signature mechanic (re-triggering ETBs like Solemn, Dream Eater, Metamorphosis Fanatic; casting permanents off the top) is entirely invisible

constellation as a distinct payoff is folded into generic 'enchantress' â€” Doomwake Giant's -1/-1 board sweep and Sigil's angel-per-enchantment are constellation triggers, a different and important payoff type than card-draw enchantresses

scry/top-of-library manipulation as a deliberate package (Ponder, Brainstorm, Portent, Telling Time, Diabolic Vision, Brainstone, Verge Rangers, Prognostic Sphinx) feeding Aminatou's top-card-cast plan is missed â€” these were dumped into islandCards instead of recognized as a library-control engine

enchantment-recursion loop (Auramancer / Starfield Mystic synergy and Mirrormade copying key enchantments) under-weighted; Starfield Mystic is flagged a total island despite being an on-theme cost-reducer/enchantress-adjacent payoff
issues: No taxonomy event for blink/flicker or cast-from-exile/top-of-library â€” a whole commander identity (Aminatou) and the cards built around it are unrepresented

Enchantress/lord family is modeled as a bidirectional clique (payoffâ‡„payoff and payoffâ‡„source), inflating edge counts and largestWebShare; a lord should radiate to its tribe, not interlink with other lords
Cost-reducers (Starfield Mystic, Ancient Cellarspawn) and pure card-selection (Brainstorm/Ponder/Portent) get zero credit, so a deliberate top-deck/cantrip engine reads as 17 dead islands
'graveyard' family is being used as a catch-all that swallows blink and ETB-reuse effects, producing the false commander characterization
weak combatâ†’payoff and destroy s1 edges add noise without representing real intended lines

rawEvals.name: Undead Unleashed (Innistrad: Midnight Hunt Commander 2021 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mono-black Zombie tribal aristocrats. Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver is the engine commander: whenever another Zombie dies it makes a Decayed Zombie token (which itself must attack and be sacrificed), and you can pay 1+discard to draw. The deck wants to go wide on Zombies via lords and token-makers, recur the graveyard, and grind value by sacrificing the endless stream of Decayed/Zombie tokens into death-payoffs (Diregraf Captain drain, Undead Augur draw, Spark Reaper/Eater of Hope outlets) â€” winning through repeated death triggers + tribal beatdown, not via a single combo line.
fp: rampâ†’sink edges (49 instances) treat every mana rock â€” Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Charcoal/Sky Diamond, Talisman, Commander's Sphere â€” as 'synergizing' with any X-spell/mana-sink like Drown in Dreams, Endless Ranks, Stitcher Geralf, Hour of Eternity; this is generic 'rocks make mana, things cost mana' filler, not a real deck-specific synergy

Drown in Dreams â‡„ all mana rocks (rampâ†’sink): Drown in Dreams is essentially a removal/draw spell that happens to cost X; flagging 9 separate rock edges as synergy is noise inflating its degree to 18

deathâ†’drain edges that point at non-death-trigger token-makers, e.g. Eternal Skylord â‡„ Diregraf Captain and Gleaming Overseer â‡„ Diregraf Captain: those token-makers don't reliably cause deaths, the payoff only fires when tokens die, so the static pairing overstates the link

Crowded Crypt â‡„ Eternal Skylord/Gleaming Overseer (tokens): Crowded Crypt makes tokens only when it dies and is mostly a ramp rock, so grouping it into the go-wide token family is a weak/spurious edge
fn: Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver's signature engine â€” 'another Zombie dies -> create a 1/1 Decayed Zombie' â€” is not modeled as a self-replacing death-to-token loop; it should be the central node tying every sacrifice to fresh fodder, but the classifier only gives Wilhelt degree 8

The Decayed keyword itself (tokens that attack then must be sacrificed) is invisible to the taxonomy; these tokens are the deck's primary recurring sac-fodder and free death triggers, yet there's no Decayed/'must-be-sacrificed' event

Rooftop Storm (Zombie cost reduction to free) is a complete island with deg 0 â€” its cost-reductionâ†’ability synergy with the expensive Zombies (Gisa and Geralf, Stitcher Geralf, Overseer of the Damned, Gravespawn Sovereign) is a real, named engine the classifier missed entirely
Empty the Laboratory and Ghouls' Night Out are islands but are real Zombie reanimation/token payoffs that belong in the graveyard/reanimate web
Distant Melody (draw a card per Zombie) is an island despite being a tribal-payoff card-draw engine tied directly to the go-wide Zombie count â€” no 'tribe-count payoff' event exists
issues: sac-fodderâ†’outlet is the single most over-weighted family (126 edges) â€” it forms a near-complete bipartite graph between token-makers and the ~4 outlets, inflating cohesion/degree without distinguishing strong loops from incidental pairs
No event models Wilhelt's death->token replacement loop; an aristocrats commander whose whole identity is a self-feeding sacrifice engine reads as merely degree-8

cost-reductionâ†’ability family failed to fire for Rooftop Storm (free Zombies), leaving a marquee build-around as an island â€” likely because Rooftop Storm's text isn't a per-spell cost reducer the classifier recognizes
No tribal 'count-the-Zombies' payoff event: Distant Melody and Wilhelt's draw scale with board width but are unconnected to the lordâ†’tribe web
graveyard family (86) is a coarse catch-all tag applied between any mill/self-mill source and any recursion card, not weighted by whether the recursion actually targets what was milled
