hasCombo=false is correct, but the loosely-connected band (46) undersells a precon that is unusually internally consistent â€” the score is dragged down by 8 islands that are mostly real cards the taxonomy failed to connect

rawEvals.name: Quandrix Unlimited (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Simic (GU) +1/+1 counters midrange/ramp deck. It ramps hard (Sol Ring, signets, Kami of Whispered Hopes, dorks, big land-ramp), dumps mana into X-spells and hydras (Primordial/Lifeblood/Hydroid/Goldvein Hydra, Stonecoil, Hangarback), then multiplies counters with doublers (Tanazir Quandrix, Altered Ego, Primordial Hydra, Fractal Harness, Kinetic Ooze, Hardened Scales) and proliferates them (Expansion Algorithm, Ozolith). Magecraft/spell-payoffs (Deekah, Quandrix Apprentice, Unbound Flourishing, Nev) reward the instant/sorcery shell. It wins by going tall/wide with counters and swinging â€” capped by an overrun (Nev, the Practical Dean) and big Fractal/hydra beats. Classic precon "counters matter" goodstuff, not a combo deck.
fp: Guardian Augmenter lordâ†’tribe edges to nearly every creature (Benevolent Hydra, Goose Mother, Troyan, etc.) â€” Guardian Augmenter only buffs/protects creatures with counters; treating it as a tribal 'lord' over the whole board is a generic stat-bonus, not a focused synergy line, and these 14 edges are largely filler

counter-multiplier edges among the doublers themselves (e.g. Altered Ego â‡„ Primordial Hydra, Tanazir â‡„ Fractal Harness) flagged at strength 3 â€” two doublers don't 'combo' with each other in any meaningful way beyond additive stacking; over-weighting these inflates cohesion

Astral Cornucopia / Mana Bloom / Elementalist's Palette tagged as counter-multiplier targets (s3) â€” these have charge counters, NOT +1/+1 counters, so doublers and most counter payoffs do nothing for them; this is a counter-type confusion false positive

rampâ†’sink edges from Arcane Signet/Sol Ring to removal like Curse of the Swine and Entrancing Melody â€” these are X-cost spells but they are interaction/removal, not a true mana 'sink' payoff line that defines deck cohesion
fn: Landfall / lands-matter engine is entirely absent: Zimone, Quandrix Prodigy (deg 1!), Eureka Moment, Open the Way, Animist's Awakening, Nature's Lore, Three Visits all feed a lands-matter / extra-lands subtheme that flips Zimone and powers Zimone, All-Questioning's prime-number land trigger â€” no 'landfallâ†’payoff' family connects them

Zimone, Infinite Analyst's actual ability (drawing a card / making a Fractal when a creature's power becomes prime, and reducing creature costs) and Zimone, All-Questioning's perfect-number land payoff are not modeled â€” the commander's signature engine is reduced to generic 'counters'

X-spell / big-mana payoff theme (Hydroid Krasis, Goldvein Hydra, Stroke of Genius, Pull from Tomorrow as scaling X-draw) is only seen as generic rampâ†’sink, missing that the counters on these hydras scale WITH the X paid â€” the rampâ†’counters bridge is the deck's real backbone
issues: No distinction between +1/+1 counters and other counter types (charge counters on Astral Cornucopia/Mana Bloom/Palette), so counter-multiplier and counters families produce false edges across counter-type boundaries

Counters-matter decks generate near-complete bipartite subgraphs (every counter-haver â‡„ every doubler/proliferator), structurally inflating edges (600) and avgDeg (17.84); cohesion may read high for any counters precon regardless of true redundancy
No landfall/lands-matter family, so a whole supporting subtheme (and the commander's prime-power/land payoff) is invisible â€” Zimone, Quandrix Prodigy ends up at degree 1
lordâ†’tribe is mis-applied to generic stat-anthem/protection cards (Guardian Augmenter), over-counting tribal cohesion that isn't really tribal

rawEvals.name: Urza's Iron Alliance (The Brothers' War Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Esper (WUB) artifacts midrange/go-wide. Urza, Chief Artificer makes a Construct each turn, gives artifact creatures menace, and reduces artifact-creature costs. The deck floods the board with artifacts/thopters (Thopter Spy Network, Sai, Whirler Rogue, Sharding Sphinx, Myr Battlesphere), pumps them with artifact lords (Master of Etherium, Chief of the Foundry, Tempered Steel) and artifact-count payoffs (Darksteel Juggernaut, Bronze Guardian, Filigree Attendant), and grinds value via ETB/sacrifice loops (Sai sac outlet, Marionette Master + Disciple-of-the-Vault-style drain, Teshar/Sharuum recursion, Tawnos copy/recur). It wins by going wide with buffed artifact creatures and attacking with evasion (Urza menace, thopter flyers), with Marionette Master / death-drain as an aristocrats backup.
fp: Sai, Master Thopterist â‡„ nearly every artifact creature (Etched Champion, Etherium Sculptor, Indomitable Archangel, Vedalken Humiliator, etc.) tagged sac-fodderâ†’outlet â€” Sai only sacks artifacts to draw, and you would never sacrifice your lords/payoffs to it; treating all 30+ as 'fodder' synergy massively over-counts a one-way value ability

Tawnos, Solemn Survivor â‡„ essentially the entire creature base via sac-fodderâ†’outlet â€” Tawnos copies a dying nontoken artifact creature once per turn; pairing it as a sac engine with every body is incidental, not a designed sacrifice loop

Sol Ring / Signets â‡„ Sphinx's Revelation, Armix, Steel Hellkite as rampâ†’sink â€” these are generic 'spend mana' cards, not a dedicated ramp-into-mana-sink synergy; flagged for any X-cost or activated ability

Cranial Plating â‡„ Swiftfoot Boots and Skullclamp â‡„ Cranial Plating tagged 'attach' â€” these are unrelated equipment that share a keyword, not a synergy (Plating wants the boots' hexproof on nothing in particular)
Preordain â‡„ Kayla's Music Box on 'scry' â€” coincidental shared keyword, no real interaction
fn: The Teshar, Ancestor's Apostle recursion engine (cast a 0-3 CMC artifact creature -> return another from graveyard) is a genuine value/combo-adjacent engine that the taxonomy only weakly catches via 'cast'/'graveyard' edges with no dedicated recurâ†’recast family

Marionette Master as the deck's drain wincon (artifacts dying = life loss scaled by counters) is captured only as deathâ†’drain weight 2-3, undercounting it as the primary aristocrats payoff alongside sac outlets
Urza's own Construct-token generation scaling off artifact count (more artifacts = bigger Constructs) is a self-referential go-wide engine not represented
Etched Champion / Indomitable Archangel granting metalcraft/shroud protection to the artifact army (a board-protection synergy enabling the go-wide plan) has no family
Cranial Plating as a real +X/+0 artifact-count payoff equipment is missed entirely â€” its degree is 2 and it is treated as junk, when it is a legitimate one-card kill threat in artifact decks
issues: sac-fodderâ†’outlet is structurally over-weighted: any sac-for-value outlet (Sai) gets linked to every creature as fodder, producing 80 edge instances and inflating perceived aristocrats density far beyond the deck's actual sacrifice intent
No 'recurâ†’recast' / reanimation-loop family â€” Teshar + Sharuum + artifact recursion is a defining engine of this archetype and is invisible

Equipment attach is keyword-matched rather than payoff-aware: Cranial Plating (a count payoff) and Skullclamp (a sac-draw engine with Sai/Marionette) should produce strong synergy edges but instead Skullclamp deg=1 and Cranial Plating deg=2 land near-island
Generic mana rocks get spurious rampâ†’sink edges to any activated/X-cost card, padding edge count without representing real synergy

Removal/wrath spells (Despark, Swords to Plowshares, Urza's Ruinous Blast, One with the Machine) flagged as islands â€” reasonable, but One with the Machine (draw = artifact with greatest power) is actually a strong card-advantage payoff in this deck and being a full island understates it

rawEvals.name: Masters of Evil (Doctor Who Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Grixis (UB-leaning) villains tribal go-wide deck. Davros, Dalek Creator punishes opponents (they lose a creature or you cast a creature -> spawn 3/3 Dalek tokens) to flood the board, then closes with two overlapping typal lords: The Dalek Emperor (Dalek payoff + cost reduction) and The Cyber-Controller (Cyberman lord + mill). A strong secondary axis is goad/menace politics (Day of the Moon, The Sound of Drums, Sontaran General, Renegade Silent, The Master Mesmerist, The Rani, Vislor Turlough) forcing opponents to attack each other while the villain army grows. It also has a soft lifeloss/drain finish (Cult of Skaro, Davros drains, Wound Reflection doubler, Time Reaper) plus a sacrifice/aristocrats-lite shell (Ashad, The Dalek Emperor sac outlet, Doomsday Confluence). Wins by overwhelming wide artifact-creature board + lord buffs, supplemented by goad chaos and incidental life drain.
fp: The Dalek Emperor â‡„ ~30 unrelated creatures (tribal-countâ†’payoff): Emperor counts your creatures, but edging it to every single body (Time Reaper, The Flood of Mars, The Master Multiplied, Solemn Simulacrum, Missy, etc.) is generic boardsize, not targeted synergy â€” this single node holds ~34 of the deck's edges and artificially props up cohesion

Solemn Simulacrum copyâ†’trigger edges (Auton Soldier, Ashad, Dalek Squadron, Cybermen Squadron, Rassilon, Hunted by The Family, The Master Gallifrey's End): these 'copy' creatures copy creatures/clone, but flagging them as triggering Solemn's ETB-on-copy is a stretch â€” Solemn is just a ramp body, not a clone target the deck cares about
Laser Screwdriver â‡„ Cybership / Time Reaper (attack): tenuous combat-tap chaining that isn't an intended line
Arcane Signet / Talismans / Thought Vesset â‡„ Cybermat (rampâ†’sink): Cybermat is a marginal incidental sink; tagging every mana rock to it is mechanical, not a real engine
fn: Wound Reflection lifeloss-doubler â‡„ Cult of Skaro / Davros / Cybermen drain: the deck has a genuine 'double the life loss' finisher line, but Wound Reflection is a deg-0 island â€” there is no lifeloss-sourceâ†’lifeloss-amplifier family

Davros, Dalek Creator's token-on-opponent-loss engine: Davros makes Daleks whenever opponents lose creatures OR you cast creatures â€” a punisher/feedback engine â€” but it only got 3 edges; the 'opponent suffers an event -> you get tokens' relationship has no family
Sacrifice/aristocrats line (The Dalek Emperor sac outlet + Ashad + token fodder + Doomsday Confluence): sac-fodderâ†’outlet is in the taxonomy but the deck's tokens-as-sac-fuel loop was not connected
Vashta Nerada / counters payoffs: several has-counters cards (Ashad, Renegade Silent, The Master Mesmerist, Cult of Skaro) suggest a +1/+1 / counter subtheme with no counters-synergy family captured
issues: No lifeloss-amplifier family: a lifeloss source -> lifeloss doubler (Wound Reflection) edge type is missing, stranding a real finisher as an island

tribal-countâ†’payoff fans out to every creature, letting one node (The Dalek Emperor, deg 34) dominate the graph and inflate cohesion â€” payoff edges should be weighted down or capped when they connect to the entire board
No 'punisher / opponent-event -> own-benefit' family to model Davros's core engine (opponents lose creatures -> you make tokens)
copyâ†’trigger over-fires on generic clone/copy bodies paired with any ETB creature (Solemn), creating phantom combo-ish edges
Goad family is captured but treated as flat weak edges; for a deck this goad-dense it under-credits a genuine win-condition axis

rawEvals.name: Jump Scare! (Duskmourn Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Simic landfall/ramp value-midrange led by Zimone
fp: Thunderfoot Baloth â‡„ Kefnet the Mindful / Rashmi / Skaab Ruinator / Tatyova / Trygon Predator / Yavimaya Elder etc. (lordâ†’tribe) â€” Thunderfoot is a Beast that pumps only OTHER Beasts, not a global lord; ~30 of its lordâ†’tribe edges are to non-Beasts and are fake

Ashaya, Soul of the Wild â‡„ nearly every creature (tribal-countâ†’payoff, deg 35) â€” Ashaya is just a single size-payoff (P/T = #Forests); turning every creature into a synergy edge inflates the web with noise, e.g. Ashaya â‡„ Trygon Predator / Hydra Omnivore / Kefnet are not meaningful synergies
Beanstalk Giant // Fertile Footsteps â‡„ ~24 creatures (tribal-countâ†’payoff, deg 36) â€” same problem: Beanstalk is one vanilla land-count beater, not a hub that synergizes with every creature
Zimone, Mystery Unraveler â‡„ Thunderfoot Baloth (lordâ†’tribe) â€” Zimone is not a Beast and gets no buff
Body of Knowledge â‡„ Temur War Shaman / Hydra Omnivore (damage) â€” these share a 'damage' tag but have no actual interaction in this deck

Giggling Skitterspike â‡„ Beast Within / Trygon Predator (destroy) â€” Skitterspike grows when a permanent is destroyed, but pairing it specifically with the deck's own removal as a 'synergy' overstates a marginal incidental effect
fn: Manifest / face-down / megamorph theme â€” the deck's signature Duskmourn mechanic (Whisperwood Elemental, Scroll of Fate, Primordial Mist, Trail of Mystery, Deathmist Raptor, Kheru Spellsnatcher, Temur War Shaman, Growing Dread) has NO event/family in the taxonomy; only a generic 'transform' produce-tag exists with no consumer, so these cards register as near-islands (Scroll of Fate deg 0, Whisperwood deg 3, Primordial Mist deg 2)

Multi-land ramp sorceries are not counted as landfall enablers â€” Cultivate and Explosive Vegetation are flagged as zero-interaction islandCards despite being core to feeding Zimone/Tatyova/Aesi/Scute Swarm landfall; the landfall family only credits spells explicitly tagged 'landfall' (Rampant Growth, Growth Spiral) and misses search-to-battlefield ramp

Zimone's own 'play your second land â†’ draw + Glimmer transform' engine and the extra-land-drop enablers (Aesi, Retreat to Coralhelm second land drops) are not modeled as a payoff loop with the ramp package â€” the deck is built around hitting two land drops a turn, which the model only sees as ordinary landfall
Reanimation / self-mill value (Skaab Ruinator delve, Dig Through Time, Multani recursion, Shigeki return-lands) is captured only as generic 'graveyard' edges, missing the graveyard-as-resource engine
issues: No 'manifest/face-down/morph/megamorph' family despite it being a flagship set mechanic; 'transform' is produced but never consumed, so the whole theme falls through to islands

tribal-countâ†’payoff treats single-card P/T-scaling payoffs (Ashaya, Beanstalk Giant) as hubs connected to every creature, producing ~46 low-value edges and two artificially high-degree nodes that inflate avgDeg/largestWebShare
lordâ†’tribe does not distinguish typed lords (Thunderfoot Baloth = Beasts only) from global anthems, generating ~30 false lord edges to non-Beasts and making Thunderfoot a deg-34 false hub
landfall family ignores fetch-to-battlefield ramp (Cultivate, Explosive Vegetation), wrongly islanding core enablers and under-counting the deck's central landfall loop

Cohesion 59 ('Cohesive') is inflated: a large share of the 276 edges come from the three over-fit hubs (Ashaya, Beanstalk, Thunderfoot ~115 edges combined); strip those and the real cohesion is moderate value-deck tier, not high

rawEvals.name: Grand Larceny (Outlaws of Thunder Junction Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Sultai (BUG) "theft / play-with-other-people's-cards" goodstuff midrange led by Gonti, Canny Acquisitor. It wins by repeatedly stealing/casting opponents' cards (exile-and-cast and steal-permanents effects) to snowball card and mana advantage, supported by a secondary evasive-attacker package whose connecting damage triggers theft and card draw. Ramp into big "steal a chunk of their library" sinks (Villainous Wealth, Plasm Capture) closes games.
fp: Predators' Hour â‡„ Cold-Eyed Selkie / Cazur / Ohran Frostfang / Shadowmage Infiltrator (combat-enabler s3): Predators' Hour grants menace deck-wide; flagging it as a strong enabler specifically for these four attackers is arbitrary and overweighted â€” it is a global combat trick, not a targeted synergy with these cards.

Thieving Varmint â‡„ Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Fellwar Stone / Prismatic Lens / Darksteel Ingot etc. (rampâ†’sink): these are generic mana-rock-to-mana-sink couplings present in nearly every EDH deck; 52 of 78 edges are this near-universal rampâ†’sink mesh, which inflates avgDeg without reflecting deck-specific synergy.

Darksteel Ingot â‡„ Feed the Swarm / Putrefy / Trygon Predator / Bladegriff Prototype (destroy): the classifier treats Darksteel Ingot as a destroy target for the deck's own removal â€” these are opponent-facing removal spells, not an intentional self-synergy; the edge is an artifact of Ingot being indestructible/an artifact.

Orochi Soul-Reaver â‡„ Cold-Eyed Selkie / Cazur / Ohran Frostfang / Shadowmage Infiltrator (combatâ†’payoff): grouping a token-maker with assorted combat-damage payoffs is a generic 'creatures attack' inference, not a real built-in line in this deck.
fn: THEFT / cast-from-opponent's-library is the entire deck and has no family: Gonti Canny Acquisitor, Gonti Lord of Luxury, Thief of Sanity, Silent-Blade Oni, Fallen Shinobi, Smirking Spelljacker, Brainstealer Dragon, Mind's Dilation, Stolen Goods, Diluvian Primordial, Extract Brain, Siphon Insight, Arcane Heist, Nashi Moon Sage's Scion, Chaos Wand, Oblivion Sower, Villainous Wealth â€” nearly all dumped into islandCards.

Steal/gain-control of permanents (Hostage Taker, Thieving Skydiver, Thieving Amalgam, Heartless Conscription) is unmodeled as a coherent family â€” Hostage Taker and Heartless Conscription are flagged as islands despite being core theft pieces.

Evasion â†’ connect-to-trigger-theft engine: unblockable/evasive enablers (Slither Blade, Triton Shorestalker, Silhana Ledgewalker, Ghostly Pilferer, Edric) that exist specifically to push through the combat-damage theft creatures are treated as islands rather than enablers of the theft payoffs.

Doc Aurlock / Gonti / Sage of the Beyond / Savvy Trader cost-reduction â†’ casting-stolen-spells: the cost-reducers (is-spell-cost-reducer) are not linked to the theft payoffs they exist to power out, so a real reduce-to-cast-foreign-spells line is missed.
issues: No 'theft / cast-from-opponent / gain-control' interaction family exists â€” this entire precon's identity is invisible to the taxonomy, producing 32 islandCards that are mostly the deck's signature cards.

rampâ†’sink family is structurally over-weighted: it forms a near-complete clique among generic mana rocks and X-spells (52/78 edges), dominating avgDeg/largestWebShare while contributing little deck-specific signal.

Cohesion of 11 ('Pile of good-stuff') is an artifact of the missing theft family â€” the deck is in fact a tightly themed steal deck, so the score should be meaningfully higher; the model conflates 'no detectable family' with 'no synergy'.
is-spell-cost-reducer capability is detected but never connected to payoffs (no cost-reductionâ†’cast-foreign-spell edge), so cost-reducers sit as low-degree islands.
Combat-payoff and combat-enabler families fire on generic 'creature attacks' heuristics and attach to thematically irrelevant pairs, creating noise that is not real synergy.

rawEvals.name: Goblin Storm (Secret Lair Commander 2026 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mono-red Zada, Hedron Grinder Goblin go-wide + spellslinger/storm hybrid. It floods the board with goblin tokens (Krenko's Command, Dragon Fodder, Empty the Warrens, Mogg War Marshal, Krenko Mob Boss, Siege-Gang) and then casts a cheap single-target instant/sorcery at Zada to copy it once per creature. Zada + a cantrip (Crimson Wisps, Expedite, Renegade Tactics, Fists of Flame) becomes draw-a-card-per-goblin, refueling a ritual-powered storm turn (Battle Hymn, Brightstone Ritual, Seething Song, Mana Geyser, Storm-Kiln Artist treasures) that ends with Grapeshot/Empty the Warrens or a Haze of Rage/Fists of Flame/Bushwhacker overrun. Two intertwined win lines: wide goblin beatdown/overrun and a Zada-fueled draw-into-storm kill.
fp: General Kreat â‡„ Mogg War Marshal / Krenko's Command / Dragon Fodder (combatâ†’payoff, s1): tagged as combat synergy but these are just generic token-makers feeding any attacker â€” weak, near-noise edges that inflate degree

Goblin Bombardment / Broadside Bombardiers â‡„ Sol Ring, Roaming Throne, Conspicuous Snoop, Skirk Prospector (sac-fodderâ†’outlet): mass-pairing every body in the deck as 'fodder' for two sac outlets overcounts; Sol Ring and Roaming Throne are not meaningfully sac fodder

rampâ†’sink edges from rituals (Seething Song, Brightstone, Mana Geyser, Sol Ring) to Krenko/Throne of Eldraine/Goblin Negotiation: technically a sink exists, but pairing one-shot rituals to creature-tap sinks is a loose generic-ramp link, not a real designed synergy

Zada â‡„ Vandalblast / Chaos Warp / Blasphemous Act (magecraft): these are not Zada-copyable in a useful way (Vandalblast/Chaos Warp/Blasphemous Act are not single-target-at-your-creature spells that benefit from the copy), so the magecraft edge overstates synergy
fn: Storm mechanic itself: Grapeshot, Empty the Warrens, Haze of Rage and Past in Flames are storm/storm-payoff cards, but there is no 'storm count' / spells-cast-this-turn family. The classifier only sees copyâ†’trigger, missing that ritual mana + cheap cantrips literally build a storm kill â€” the deck's namesake

Zada-copies-cantrip = explosive card draw engine: Crimson Wisps/Expedite/Renegade Tactics/Fists of Flame targeting Zada draw a card PER creature. This draw-multiplier-on-a-wide-board line is the deck's engine and is collapsed into generic 'magecraft', not recognized as a draw payoff scaled by board width

Impact Tremors and Boggart Shenanigans are flagged as islands but are real go-wide payoffs (damage per ETB / per goblin death) that should connect to every token-producer and to Goblin Bombardment/Skirk Prospector â€” a damage-on-ETB / damage-on-death family is missing
Skullclamp (island) is a key go-wide draw engine on 1-toughness goblin tokens â€” sacrificing a token to draw two â€” the attach-to-cheap-body â†’ draw payoff is entirely unmodeled
Conspicuous Snoop + Goblin tribal (top-of-library casting / Snoop copying ability goblins) and Goblin Recruiter/Matron tutor lines are not recognized as engines
issues: No 'storm' / spells-cast-this-turn family despite the deck being literally named Goblin Storm â€” storm payoffs (Grapeshot, Empty the Warrens, Haze of Rage, Past in Flames) are only seen as copyâ†’trigger
ETB-damage and death-damage payoffs (Impact Tremors, Boggart Shenanigans) have no event, so go-wide damage engines fall to islands (false-negative island bug)
Skullclamp's sac-a-1-toughness-token-for-cards engine is invisible (attachâ†’draw on cheap bodies not modeled), wrongly an island

magecraft is overloaded as a catch-all for both Zada's copy and Storm-Kiln treasure and ignores whether a spell is actually a beneficial Zada target â€” produces some false-positive magecraft edges (Vandalblast, Chaos Warp, Blasphemous Act)

hasCombo=false and no comboCriticalPairs even though Zada + cheap cantrip on a wide board is a draw-your-deck / storm-kill engine close to combo-grade; the combo gate is too strict for board-scaled copy payoffs
weak generic combatâ†’payoff and rampâ†’sink edges inflate avgDeg (15.08) relative to the truly meaningful synergy density

rawEvals.name: Vampiric Bloodlust (Commander 2017 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mardu (W/B/R) Vampire tribal go-wide aggro with an aristocrats/lifedrain subtheme. Edgar Markov's eminence makes a 1/1 Vampire token on every Vampire cast and his attack trigger puts +1/+1 counters on the whole team, so the plan is: flood the board with Vampires, buff them with lords (Stromkirk Captain, Captivating Vampire, Bloodsworn Steward, Door of Destinies, Kindred Boon), and swing wide. The backup/finisher line is aristocrats drain: Blood Artist / Falkenrath Noble / Malakir Bloodwitch / Sanguine Bond converting all the dying tokens and gained life into life loss for the table, with sac outlets (Butcher of Malakir, Patron of the Vein, Blade of the Bloodchief) feeding it.
fp: Vampire Nighthawk â‡„ Well of Lost Dreams (lifegain) and similar â€” Nighthawk's lifelink is incidental combat lifegain; Well only converts life GAINED into draw if you pay mana, there is no engine pairing here, just two cards that touch 'life'
Tithe Drinker â‡„ Well of Lost Dreams (lifegain) â€” Tithe Drinker's extort is marginal incidental lifegain, not a meaningful sink-feed for Well of Lost Dreams
Bloodline Necromancer â‡„ Well of Lost Dreams (lifegain) â€” Necromancer gains life once on ETB; this is not a repeatable lifegain engine for Well

Curse of Vitality â‡„ Sanguine Bond (lifegain) â€” the Curse only gains life when a chosen player is attacked; pairing it as a Sanguine Bond drain enabler massively overstates a once-per-combat trickle

Skullclamp â‡„ Curse of Disturbance / Curse of Vitality (attach) â€” Skullclamp attaches to creatures, the Curses are Auras that attach to PLAYERS; these are not interacting objects, the 'attach' family conflated two unrelated attachment types
Heirloom Blade â‡„ Curse of Disturbance / Curse of Vitality (attach) â€” same error: an Equipment grouped with player-targeting Curse Auras

Edgar Markov â‡„ Skeletal Scrying / Consuming Vapors / Outpost Siege (cast) â€” these 'cast' edges imply a casting payoff relationship that does not exist; Edgar only triggers on casting VAMPIRES, and Scrying/Vapors/Outpost Siege are not creatures
fn: Edgar Markov's eminence (make a 1/1 Vampire token whenever you cast a Vampire) is the deck's central engine and has NO dedicated event â€” every Vampire creature should be a token-producing trigger off the commander; the taxonomy only sees generic 'cast', so the deck's signature synergy is missed

Edgar Markov's attack trigger distributing +1/+1 counters to the whole Vampire team (an anthem-on-attack / mass-counter payoff) is not modeled â€” counters are tracked as a capability but Edgar's team-wide counter pump on attack is absent from combatâ†’payoff edges

Sanguine Bond + big lifegain as a near-combo / soft win condition (Sanguine Bond + Licia/Blood Baron/Blood Tribute = large untargeted-equivalent drain) is captured only as weak 'lifegain' edges, not flagged as a payoff combo line

Skullclamp as a draw engine off the many 1-toughness Vampire tokens (equip a 1/1 token, it draws 2 on death) is missed â€” Skullclamp is deg 2 and not connected to Edgar's token production or the aristocrats sac theme it actually fuels

Aristocrats overlap: the same dying tokens trigger Blood Artist, Falkenrath Noble, AND Malakir Bloodwitch simultaneously â€” this stacked death-payoff density is the deck's true power and is only partially represented by scattered deathâ†’drain pairs
issues: No 'eminence' / 'cast-typed-creature â†’ token' event exists, so commanders whose entire engine is token-on-tribal-cast (Edgar Markov) are systematically under-scored

The 'lifegain' family is too loose: it links any lifegain producer to any lifegain consumer/payoff regardless of whether the lifegain is repeatable or incidental, producing many phantom edges (34 lifegain edge-instances here are largely noise)
The 'attach' family conflates Equipment (attaches to creatures) with Aura/Curse (attaches to players), creating false edges like Skullclampâ‡„Curse of Disturbance
Generic 'cast' edges from the commander treat all noncreature spells as casting payoffs even when the commander only cares about a creature subtype, inflating Edgar's apparent connectivity
Kindred Charge as a hub gets a tribal-countâ†’payoff edge to literally every Vampire (deg 37), heavily weighting one card and skewing avgDeg/cohesion toward a single payoff

Cohesion of 41 ('Loosely connected') under-rates a famously tight, focused precon tribal deck â€” the band/threshold appears miscalibrated for go-wide tribal where the synergy is concentrated in the commander's untracked eminence rather than in pairwise card edges

rawEvals.name: Planeswalker Party (Commander Masters Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Superfriends / planeswalker tribal. The deck plays a high density of planeswalkers (Commodore Guff at the helm) and protects/grows them: ramp into walkers, defend with cheap walls/pillowfort (Fog Bank, Wall of Denial, Silent Arbiter, Norn's Annex), then accumulate loyalty advantage and grind out the table with walker ultimates. Its real engines are (1) proliferate (Thrummingbird, Flux Channeler, Grateful Apparition, Gatewatch Beacon) plus loyalty-counter doublers (Deepglow Skate, Ajani Steadfast, counter-doubling Spark Double/Jace, Mirror Mage clones) to vault walkers toward ultimates; (2) The Chain Veil + Commodore Guff / Chandra, Legacy of Fire style untap-and-reactivate engine for extra walker activations; (3) go-wide token defense from Elspeth, Saheeli, Sarkhan, Oath of Gideon, Teyo. It wins by ultimate-ing walkers (Elspeth's emblem, Chandra Awakened Inferno's recurring damage, Guff's draw/token snowball) or a wide token board, not by a tight combo.
fp: Fog Bank â‡„ The Chain Veil / Chandra, Legacy of Fire (tribal-countâ†’payoff): Fog Bank is a 0/x defensive blocker, not a planeswalker â€” it does not feed any 'planeswalker count' payoff; same for Wall of Denial and Silent Arbiter being counted as tribe members

Elspeth, Sun's Champion â‡„ Fog Bank / Wall of Denial / Silent Arbiter (lordâ†’tribe): Elspeth's +1 makes Soldier tokens and her anthem/destroy only matter for attackers; these are defensive walls that never attack, so 'lord buffs tribe' is not a real line here

Mangara, the Diplomat 'combatâ†’payoff' edges (e.g. with Kazuul, Saheeli, Teyo): Mangara rewards opponents attacking/casting, it is not a go-wide combat payoff being enabled by token makers â€” the combat-payoff coupling is mislabeled
rampâ†’sink: 102 instances inflate cohesion by pairing every mana rock with every mana-sink walker; real but heavily double-counted and generic to any walker deck
Saheeli â‡„ Sarkhan / Kazuul (combatâ†’payoff): these are loosely related token/attack effects, not a deliberate enablerâ†’payoff synergy
fn: The Chain Veil's untap-planeswalker / extra-loyalty-activation engine with Commodore Guff and Chandra, Legacy of Fire â€” the actual chase combo of the deck (repeated walker activations) is modeled only as flat 'tribal-countâ†’payoff', with no untapâ†’activate or near-loop event; hasCombo=false misses this

Loyalty 'ultimate' as a payoff/win family: the deck's whole plan is reaching ultimates (Elspeth emblem, Chandra Awakened Inferno emblem, Guff/Teferi-style ults), but there is no event linking proliferate/counter-doublers to an ultimate payoff â€” only generic counter math

Pillowfort / 'can't attack me' defense as a synergy family: Norn's Annex, Silent Arbiter, Fog Bank, Wall of Denial, Kazuul deterrent, and The Wanderer collectively form a protect-the-walkers shell that the taxonomy has no event for â€” these mostly land in islandCards or get mislabeled
Planeswalker protection via token chump-blockers (Elspeth/Sarkhan/Saheeli/Teyo tokens defending walkers) â€” modeled as combat payoff, not as defense for the loyalty engine
issues: No pillowfort / damage-prevention / 'must-attack-elsewhere' family, so the deck's defensive core (Norn's Annex, Silent Arbiter, Fog Bank, Wall of Denial, The Wanderer) becomes islands or false lord/tribe edges

No 'planeswalker untap / extra activation' event â€” The Chain Veil + Guff + Chandra, Legacy of Fire engine is flattened into tribal-countâ†’payoff and missed by the combo gate (hasCombo=false despite a real grindy loop)
No 'loyalty ultimate payoff' concept; proliferate and counter-doublers have nothing to point at as a finisher, so the deck's actual win path is invisible to the score
tribal-countâ†’payoff over-fires by counting non-planeswalker permanents (walls, defensive artifacts) as tribe members for The Chain Veil / Chandra, Legacy of Fire
rampâ†’sink heavily double-counts (102 edges) and inflates avgDeg/largestWebShare for what is just standard rocks-into-walkers ramp
Removal/wrath suite (Path, Swords, Blasphemous Act, Urza's Ruinous Blast, Nevinyrral's Disk) correctly islanded, but the model has no way to credit board-control as supporting a slow walker plan

rawEvals.name: Buckle Up (Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Azorius (WU) Vehicles + artifacts midrange/aggro. The deck crews and attacks with Vehicles/Mechs (Smuggler's Copter, Skysovereign, Parhelion II, Weatherlight, the Neon Dynasty Mechs) under Kotori, Pilot Prodigy, who reduces crew costs and protects Vehicles. It supports that with an artifact-tribal/Affinity subtheme (Master of Etherium, cost reducers, Affinity payoffs), Thopter token generation to crew and to feed Sai, and a small artifact-recursion engine (Teshar + Emry + Dance of the Manse + Hanna). It wins via evasive crewed Vehicles and a go-wide artifact board.
fp: Sai, Master Thopterist â‡„ Gold Myr / Silver Myr (sac-fodderâ†’outlet) â€” Sai only sacrifices artifacts and you would essentially never sac your mana dorks; technically-legal but not a real synergy line

Sai, Master Thopterist deg 35 as universal sac-outlet â€” many sac-fodder edges to creatures you want to keep (Indomitable Archangel, Vedalken Engineer, Teshar) inflate the hub far beyond real play patterns

Master of Etherium â‡„ Access Denied / Release to Memory (tribal-countâ†’payoff) â€” these make non-artifact-creature thopter tokens or are instants; counting them toward Master's artifact-creature lord/Affinity payoff is partly spurious
Dance of the Manse â‡„ every mana rock as rampâ†’sink â€” Dance is a one-shot reanimation X-spell, not a repeatable mana sink; the dense rampâ†’sink fan-out overstates a single edge
fn: Vehicle + Crew is the entire deck and has NO taxonomy event â€” crewers (Kotori, every creature, Pilots) enabling Vehicles (Smuggler's Copter, Skysovereign, Parhelion II, Weatherlight, Raiders' Karve, Aerial Surveyor, Colossal Plow, Peacewalker Colossus, the Mechs) is uncaptured; most Vehicles are islandCards
Kotori, Pilot Prodigy's crew-cost reduction + Vehicle ward is the commander's core ability and produces zero relevant edges
Reconfigure / equip-as-pilot interactions: Swift Reconfiguration, Imposter Mech, Surgehacker Mech, Armed and Armored are islands despite being crew/equip payoffs
Artifact-recursion loop Teshar + Emry + Dance of the Manse (recur cheap artifact creatures, value-grind) is only partially seen via 'graveyard' edges; the Teshar cast-trigger engine is undervalued

Affinity / artifact-cost-reduction as a cohesive subtheme (Etherium Sculptor, Foundry Inspector, Emry, Thoughtcast) â€” cost reducers are flagged individually but not as a unified affinity engine feeding the artifact count
issues: No crew/Vehicle family in the taxonomy â€” a flagship MTG mechanic and this precon's defining theme is entirely unmodeled, producing 17 islands and an artificially low cohesion
Reconfigure / pilot-equipment has no event, so those cards are inert
sac-fodderâ†’outlet is over-inclusive: it should respect the sacrifice constraint (Sai = artifacts only) and de-weight fodder you would never sacrifice (mana dorks, lords)
rampâ†’sink mis-classifies one-shot X-spells (Dance of the Manse) as repeatable sinks, fanning out spurious edges
lordâ†’tribe / tribal-countâ†’payoff over-concentrate on a single hub (Master of Etherium deg 33) making the web look fragile/centralized when the artifact theme is genuinely broad
cohesion penalizes the deck for islands that are actually on-theme Vehicles, so the band 'Loosely connected' understates real synergy

rawEvals.name: Tricky Terrain (Modern Horizons 3 Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Simic (G/U) landfall / lands-matter ramp-value midrange. It ramps and drops extra lands (Dryad of the Ilysian Grove, Growth Spiral, Eureka Moment, Urban Evolution, Elvish Rejuvenator, Sylvan Scrying, Expedition Map) to fuel landfall payoffs (Scute Swarm, Avenger of Zendikar, Rampaging Baloths, Rampant Frogantua, Tatyova, Uro, Seer's Sundial), draws a ton off land drops, and wins by going wide/tall with tokens and big land-count creatures (Ulvenwald Hydra, Scute Swarm army, Avenger plants pumped by landfall). Omo, Queen of Vesuva is a type-matters/MV-shrink toolbox commander that turns permanents into creatures and sets nonland MV to 0, enabling land-untap and type-count shenanigans. Land-untap mana engines (Magus of the Candelabra, Sage of the Maze, Finale of Revelation, Nissa) plus Mana Reflection feed big X-spells/mana sinks.
fp: Scute Swarm â‡„ Acidic Slime / Floriferous Vinewall / Skullwinder / Terastodon / Jyoti (copyâ†’trigger): Scute Swarm only copies ITSELF on landfall; it never copies these creatures' ETBs, so these are invented combo edges

Replication Technique â‡„ nearly every ETB creature (copyâ†’trigger s2): Replication Technique copies ONE chosen creature once; treating it as a strong combo edge to Satyr Wayfinder, Elvish Rejuvenator, Skullwinder, Terastodon, Jyoti, Acidic Slime, Uro overstates a single flexible spell
Ulvenwald Hydra â‡„ ~every creature (tribal-countâ†’payoff): Ulvenwald Hydra's P/T = number of LANDS, not creature-type count, so the whole star of 'typecount payoff' edges is a misclassification
Vivien Reid as is-lord generating lordâ†’tribe edges to most creatures: Vivien is a card-advantage/removal planeswalker, not a tribal lord; the 25 lordâ†’tribe edges are spurious
Omo, Queen of Vesuva â‡„ Vivien Reid (lordâ†’tribe) and â‡„ Ulvenwald Hydra (tribal-countâ†’payoff): both rest on the above mislabels
Combinatorial rampâ†’sink blowup (54 edges): pairing every mana rock with every X-spell/sink (e.g. Sol Ring â‡„ Curse of the Swine) is real in spirit but heavily over-counted
fn: Omo, Queen of Vesuva's core ability â€” granting creature types and setting each nonland permanent's mana value to 0 â€” has no event/family; the commander's entire identity is unmodeled (Omo reduced to has-counters/is-body, degree 4)
Land-untap â†’ mana engine: Magus of the Candelabra, Sage of the Maze, Finale of Revelation and Nissa untap lands for explosive mana, but the untapâ†’tap-for-mana payoff line isn't captured
Mana Reflection's land-mana doubling is missed entirely â€” it is on the island list despite doubling all the mana that feeds the deck's X-spell sinks
Lands-from-graveyard recursion: Ramunap Excavator (and fetch/sac lands) replaying lands for repeated landfall is a real landfall engine not modeled as such

Extra-land-drop enablers (Dryad of the Ilysian Grove, Growth Spiral, Eureka Moment, Urban Evolution) as MULTIPLIERS of landfall triggers are treated as plain landfall sources, missing the per-turn-trigger-count amplification that makes Scute Swarm explosive
issues: No event/capability for type-granting or mana-value-setting effects â€” commanders like Omo whose whole point is changing types/MV are invisible to the taxonomy
is-typecount-payoff conflated with land-count payoff: Ulvenwald Hydra (lands matter) is treated as creature-type-count, creating a giant false tribal star
Planeswalkers with anthem-ish or removal text (Vivien Reid) get mislabeled is-lord, generating large spurious lordâ†’tribe webs
copyâ†’trigger family is too loose: self-copy-on-landfall (Scute Swarm) and single-target copy spells (Replication Technique) are treated as broad combo manifolds, badly inflating edge count
Land-untap â†’ mana family appears absent (Magus of the Candelabra, Sage of the Maze, Finale untap-lands not linked to mana payoff)
rampâ†’sink is combinatorial (every source Ă— every sink) rather than capped, inflating cohesion
Mana-doubling effects (Mana Reflection) have no capability and fall to island status despite being central enablers

rawEvals.name: Most Wanted (Outlaws of Thunder Junction Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mardu (WBR) Outlaws "crime"/treasure go-wide aristocrats-lite. Olivia, Opulent Outlaw rewards committing crimes (each combat she counts treasures/tokens you control and grows + makes tokens), so the deck floods the board with Treasure and creature tokens, abuses outlaw-type creatures (Assassins/Mercenaries/Pirates/Rogues/Warlocks) for crime/attack triggers, then converts the wide board into damage via combat payoffs (Tenured Inkcaster, Breena, Dead Before Sunrise overrun) or drains via Life Insurance / Witch of the Moors. Secondary aristocrats angle: Fain and Laurine as sac outlets feeding death-drain and treasure value. It wins through combat damage from a treasure/token-fueled wide board plus incidental drain.
fp: Graywater's Fixer â‡„ Charred Graverobber / Misfortune Teller / Aetherborn Marauder / Dire Fleet Ravager / Tenured Inkcaster (copyâ†’trigger, s:2) â€” Graywater's Fixer copies a token/creature on attack, it is NOT an ETB-doubler, so these 'copy your ETB' edges are wrong

Impulsive Pilferer â‡„ Charred Graverobber / Aetherborn Marauder / Dire Fleet Ravager (copyâ†’trigger) â€” Impulsive Pilferer is a treasure-maker with a blitz/dies trigger, not an ETB copier; flagging it as a copy engine for other ETBs is a false synergy

We Ride at Dawn â‡„ Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Rakdos Signet / Orzhov Signet (rampâ†’sink) â€” generic mana rocks counted as dedicated sinks for a single anthem inflates rampâ†’sink; these are filler-grade, not real engine edges
Shoot the Sheriff â‡„ Boros Charm and Feed the Swarm â‡„ Boros Charm (destroy) â€” unrelated removal spells linked merely by both being able to destroy; not a synergy
Shiny Impetus â‡„ Lightning Greaves (attach) and â‡„ Trailblazer's Boots â€” equipment/aura 'attach' edges are coincidental, not intended interactions
fn: The 'crime' mechanic / outlaw creature-type matters-theme is entirely absent from the taxonomy â€” committing crimes is the set's signature trigger (Mari, Bandit's Haul, Olivia's count) and the outlaw subtype gates payoffs; the only nod is Bandit's Haul consuming 'crime' with no producers, so the whole web around it is missed

Monarch as a card-advantage/aggro engine â€” Queen Marchesa makes you the monarch and Breena/attack triggers interact with it, but there is no monarch payoff family (Marchesa's 'monarch' produce has no consumers)

Goad-into-Grenzo/Olivia value loop is underweighted: Shiny Impetus/Laurine/Grenzo goad, and goad forces attacks that feed Grenzo's cast-trigger and combat payoffs, but goad (only 2 instances) is treated as a near-island rather than an attack-forcing engine
Olivia's own treasure/token-count-as-combat-scaling is not modeled as a distinct payoff â€” she is the deck's keystone but reads as just another s:1 combat/treasure node
issues: No event/family for 'crime' or outlaw creature types â€” a set-defining mechanic that is the literal cohesion glue here produces orphan capabilities (Bandit's Haul consumes 'crime' with zero producers)
No monarch family â€” monarch is a real EDH engine; Queen Marchesa's 'monarch' produce dangles with no consumer

copyâ†’trigger family is too loose: it conflates 'copy a token on attack' (Graywater's Fixer) and treasure/blitz creatures (Impulsive Pilferer) with true ETB-doublers, generating 24 edge-instances that are largely spurious

rampâ†’sink over-fires: any anthem/mana-sink (We Ride at Dawn) links to every mana rock, so a 35-land precon with normal ramp shows 88 rampâ†’sink instances, inflating perceived cohesion in low-strength s:1 edges

Cohesion 37 / 'Loosely connected' is too low: with 94% interactive, largest web 94%, and four genuinely interlocking engines (treasure economy, sac-outlet aristocrats, go-wide combat payoff, death-drain), this is a clearly-themed mono-strategy precon that the score treats as loose because most edges are weak s:1 â€” strength weighting penalizes a deck that is intentionally redundant rather than combo-y

Island detection is fine but the islands (Council's Judgment, Curtains' Call, Hex, Mass Mutiny) are correctly removal/utility, confirming the deck is otherwise tightly themed â€” reinforcing that 37 understates real cohesion

rawEvals.name: Living Energy (Aetherdrift Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Temur/Jeskai energy-matters midrange led by Saheeli, Radiant Creator. The deck generates energy counters (Aether/energy producers like Servant of the Conduit, Solar Transformer, Whirler Virtuoso, Rogue Refiner, Attune with Aether, Decoction Module, Era of Innovation) and spends them on big payoffs (Aetherwind Basker, Aethertide Whale, Aethersquall Ancient, Aetherworks Marvel, Architect of the Untamed). It also runs a secondary artifact/thopter go-wide + token shell (Pia and Kiran Nalaar, Whirler Rogue/Virtuoso, Sai, Master Thopterist, Saheeli token-copying ETB creatures) that fuels Saheeli's copy ability and Aetherwind Basker's go-wide pump. It wins by overwhelming with energy-pumped tokens/big creatures, with Saheeli copying value ETBs. No true infinite combo.
fp: Attune with Aether â‡„ Servant of the Conduit (and the whole producerâ‡„producer energy mesh): two cards that both MAKE energy are linked as synergistic, but neither consumes the other's output â€” energy synergy is producerâ†’payoff, not producerâ†’producer. This pairwise meshing massively over-counts the 173 energy edges.

Pia and Kiran Nalaar â‡„ Bootleggers' Stash / Ornithopter of Paradise / Stridehangar Automaton as sac-fodderâ†’outlet: Pia's sac ability only sacs ARTIFACTS for damage, and listing it against every artifact/creature in the deck (24 edges) treats it as a universal sac engine when it is a narrow, mostly-irrelevant ability rarely used on these payoff permanents.

Saheeli, Radiant Creator â‡„ Druid of Purification / Soul-Guide Lantern as copyâ†’trigger strength-2: Saheeli copies an artifact OR token creature, and these one-shot ETBs give negligible value when copied â€” flagged at the same weight as genuinely strong copy targets like Combustible Gearhulk.

Aetherwind Basker linked to nearly every nonland permanent via tribal-countâ†’payoff: Basker's energy is per-creature on ETB, not an ongoing tribe payoff, so the 31 tribal-count edges overstate a recurring lord-like synergy that does not exist.
fn: Panharmonicon is an ISLAND with deg 0, yet it doubles every ETB trigger in a deck full of ETB energy/token creatures (Rogue Refiner, Whirler Virtuoso, Solar Transformer, Territorial Aetherkite, Solemn Simulacrum) â€” a major payoff/multiplier the taxonomy has no event for (no 'trigger-doubler' capability).

Saheeli, Radiant Creator's own copy interaction with the deck's token PRODUCERS (Pia and Kiran Nalaar, Whirler Rogue/Virtuoso, Loyal Apprentice) is undercounted â€” copying a token-maker compounds the go-wide plan, but the model only tags generic copyâ†’trigger, missing the token-multiplication engine.

Aetherflux Conduit is an island (deg 0) but is an energy/artifacts payoff and storm-style draw/win engine in an artifact-heavy energy deck â€” the classifier has no 'cast-count payoff' or energy-as-resource-for-card-advantage event to connect it.

One with the Machine is an island but is card draw that scales off the deck's biggest creature (powerful in a deck running Aetherwind Basker/Elder Gargaroth/Aethertide Whale) â€” no 'power-matters draw' event exists.

Energy as a shared resource that crosses producerâ†’sink is captured, but the model misses that energy production scales Saheeli's identity and the deck's snowball â€” there is no notion of an energy 'critical mass' threshold that several payoffs (Aethersquall Ancient at 8, Aetherwind Basker) gate on.
issues: Resource-pool families (energy) are modeled as pairwise cliques rather than producerâ†’payoff bipartite edges, inflating edge count (173), avgDeg (9.05) and largestWebShare (84%). Producerâ‡„producer pairs should not be edges.
No 'ETB-trigger-doubler' / Panharmonicon capability exists, so a marquee enabler is a dead island in an ETB-heavy deck.
No 'cast-count / storm payoff' event (Aetherflux Conduit) and no 'power-matters draw' event (One with the Machine), leaving real payoffs as islands.
copyâ†’trigger and sac-fodderâ†’outlet are applied uniformly at fixed strength regardless of how good the target actually is when copied/sacrificed, over-counting low-value pairs.
tribal-countâ†’payoff is over-applied: a one-time ETB energy payoff (Aetherwind Basker) is treated like a standing tribal lord, generating 31 inflated edges.

rawEvals.name: Exit from Exile (Battle for Baldur's Gate Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Gruul (RG) "exile-matters" / impulse-draw midrange. Faldorn, Dread Wolf Herald turns "exile a nonland card from a hidden zone" into Wolf tokens, so the deck stacks impulse-draw / cast-from-exile engines (Outpost Siege, Light Up the Stage, Escape to the Wilds, Ignite the Future, Stolen Strategy, Etali Primal Storm, Chaos Wand, Wild-Magic Sorcerer, Dire Fleet Daredevil, Laelia, Tectonic Giant, Dream Pillager, Volcanic Torrent, Aurora Phoenix) to generate continuous card advantage and a board of Wolves. It ramps hard (lots of land-ramp + Sol Ring/Signet/Jeska's Will) into big beaters, then closes with go-wide overrun effects (End-Raze Forerunners, Return of the Wildspeaker, Sandwurm Convergence) and Faldorn's Wolf army. Passionate Archaeologist is the dedicated payoff that pings each first cast-from-exile each turn.
fp: Faldorn â‡„ (almost every spell) via 'cast' s1 â€” Faldorn's relevant trigger is exile-of-a-nonland-card making Wolves, NOT 'a player casts a spell'; her discard-on-cast clause does not synergize with simply having spells in the deck, so the ~30 'cast' edges off the commander are noise

Beanstalk Giant // Fertile Footsteps tribal-countâ†’payoff with ~25 cards â€” Beanstalk Giant's P/T scales with LANDS, not with a creature tribe/typecount; flagging it as a tribal/typecount payoff connected to every creature (Arasta, Hornet Queen, Managorger, Urabrask, etc.) is a misread, inflating both Beanstalk's deg (33) and the tribal-count family (32 instances)

Passionate Archaeologist â‡„ (every noncreature spell) via 'cast' â€” Archaeologist only triggers on the FIRST spell cast from EXILE each turn, not on casting any spell, so its 26 generic cast edges are largely false; the real (and missed) synergy is specifically with the exile-cast engines

Durnan of the Yawning Portal combatâ†’payoff edges to token-makers (Arasta, Hornet Queen, Sandwurm) â€” Durnan's ability is a tap-and-attack Undercity dungeon venture, not a meaningful 'wide-board combat payoff', so these are weak/incidental links dressed up as synergy

Greater Gargadon sac-fodderâ†’outlet with ~20 creatures â€” Gargadon is a suspend payoff that eats permanents for a hasty body; this deck has no sacrifice-value engine, so treating every creature as 'fodder' for it overstates an interaction nobody actually uses
fn: The core 'exile a card from a hidden zone / impulse-draw, then cast it' engine â€” there is no event for exile-cast, so Outpost Siege, Light Up the Stage, Escape to the Wilds, Ignite the Future, Etali, Stolen Strategy, Chaos Wand, Wild-Magic Sorcerer, Dire Fleet Daredevil, Laelia, Tectonic Giant, Dream Pillager, Volcanic Torrent never connect to Faldorn's Wolf-on-exile trigger or to each other as a family

Passionate Archaeologist as the dedicated 'cast-from-exile' payoff â€” its true trigger condition (first cast from exile each turn) is not modeled, so the one card explicitly built to reward the exile theme is mis-wired through generic 'cast' instead
Faldorn's actual ability (exileâ†’Wolf token producer) is not represented as a produces/consumes 'exile' event, so the commander's central engine has zero true edges despite deg 33 of noise

Landfall payoffs are under-detected: Primeval Bounty, Journey to the Lost City and the heavy land-ramp suite (Cultivate, Kodama's Reach, Three Visits, Nature's Lore, Sakura-Tribe Elder) form a real landfall sub-theme, yet five of those ramp spells are flagged as ISLANDS with zero interactions
issues: No 'exile-matters' / impulse-draw / cast-from-exile family exists â€” a major, common Gruul/Izzet mechanic (and this precon's entire identity) is absent from the taxonomy

The generic 'cast' family acts as a catch-all that fires off any commander with a cast-trigger word and links it to every spell, manufacturing dozens of strength-1 edges that inflate avgDeg (8.62) and pctInteractive (85) without representing real synergy
tribal-countâ†’payoff is mis-applied to Beanstalk Giant (a lands-matter creature) and treats every creature as a 'typecount' contributor, over-counting that family
Land-ramp sorceries (Cultivate, Kodama's Reach, etc.) are modeled as inert islands instead of feeding landfall payoffs â€” rampâ†’sink only fires for mana rocks, missing fetch-land ramp into landfall
sac-fodderâ†’outlet over-fires for Greater Gargadon in a deck with no sacrifice-value engine, treating a suspend creature as a real sac outlet

rawEvals.name: Revenant Recon (Murders at Karlov Manor Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Dimir self-mill / surveil graveyard-value reanimator midrange. Mirko, Obsessive Theorist wants a fat graveyard (it reanimates a creature each end step with a +1/+1 counter scaled to graveyard size), so the deck fills its own yard via surveil/draw-and-discard (Consider, Curate, Otherworldly Gaze, Connive, Notion Rain, Discovery//Dispersal, Mission Briefing) and grinds it for value with recursion (Reanimate, Animate Dead, Necromancy, Charnel Serenade) plus recastable spells. It wins on incremental card advantage and beatdown from reanimated/Mirko-pumped bodies, with a copy-creature subtheme (Lazav, Copy Catchers, Amphin Mutineer, Vizier/Phyrexian Metamorph) and ETB-value/removal creatures (Mulldrifter, Chupacabra, Shriekmaw, Dream Eater). No real combo.
fp: Charnel Serenade â‡„ Counterpoint (graveyard, s1) â€” two spells that merely both touch the yard; neither enables nor pays off the other, this is theme-tag co-membership not synergy
Curate â‡„ Otherworldly Gaze and the whole cantrip-to-cantrip web (Consider/Notion Rain/Deep Analysis/Discovery cross-edges) â€” each just self-mills independently; they don't interact pairwise

Talisman of Dominance â‡„ Twilight Prophet / Pile On (rampâ†’sink) â€” Twilight Prophet has no mana sink ability (it's an Ascend lifedrain payoff), so labeling it a mana sink for the rocks is wrong; Pile On's X is real but minor

Case of the Shifting Visage â‡„ Massacre Wurm/Shriekmaw/Chupacabra (copyâ†’trigger, s2) â€” Case of the Shifting Visage copies an artifact/enchantment or makes a token of a creature you control on solve; pairing it as a copy-payoff for every ETB creature overstates a narrow effect

Copy Catchers â‡„ every ETB creature (copyâ†’trigger, s2, 15 edges) â€” Copy Catchers is a one-shot clone-on-ETB; flagging it as a recurring copy engine against the whole creature base inflates the copy family
fn: Surveil-as-a-mechanic: the deck's core enabler is surveil (Nightveil Sprite, Dimir Spybug, Whispering Snitch, Sinister Starfish scry, Mirko itself), and surveil triggers payoffs â€” Whispering Snitch and Dimir Spybug both reward surveil/yard growth â€” yet Whispering Snitch and Dimir Spybug are flagged as ISLANDS (deg 0). A surveilâ†’surveil-payoff family is entirely missing
Mirko's specific reanimate-each-end-step is not modeled as a recurring reanimation engine distinct from one-shot Reanimate/Animate Dead; the commander's defining loop is invisible
Thoughtbound Phantasm and Dimir Spybug grow from opponents' graveyard/surveil activity (a +1/+1-counter-on-mill subtheme) â€” counters payoff family absent, both are islands despite has-counters caps
Twilight Prophet / Whispering Snitch / Eye of Duskmantle form a lifedrain/Ascend incremental-damage win condition that the taxonomy doesn't connect (lifeloss payoff has no enablers wired to it)
Marvo, Deep Operative (deg 0 island) â€” a surveil/investigate-matters draw payoff left completely unconnected
issues: graveyard family is a symmetric mega-clique: 485 of 530 edge instances come from it, producing N-squared edges among co-tagged cards and grossly inflating avgDeg/edges while every edge is only s=1. It conflates enablers (mill/discard/surveil) with payoffs (reanimation, Mirko, Syr Konrad) instead of being a directional enablerâ†’payoff family
No 'surveil' event in the taxonomy, so a core Dimir mechanic and its payoffs (Whispering Snitch, Dimir Spybug, Thoughtbound Phantasm) fall out as islands â€” a major false-negative source
No counters-matter / +1/+1-counter payoff family: cards with has-counters (Dimir Spybug, Thoughtbound Phantasm, Mirko's counter) are not linked despite a real theme
copyâ†’trigger over-fires on one-shot clones (Copy Catchers, Case of the Shifting Visage) as if they were repeatable copy engines, inflating the copy family (62 instances)
rampâ†’sink mislabels Twilight Prophet (Ascend lifedrain, not a mana sink) as a sink, creating phantom ramp payoffs
Scoring lands at the right band only by offsetting errors: raw edge count over-counts the graveyard theme while per-edge weighting under-credits the deck's genuinely tight, focused yard synergy

rawEvals.name: Prismari Artistry (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Izzet (UR) spellslinger / magecraft "spells-matter" deck with a copy-and-tokens subtheme. Commanded by Rootha, it casts cheap instants/sorceries to trigger magecraft payoffs (Archmage Emeritus draws, Storm-Kiln Artist treasures, Veyran doubles triggers, Prismari Pianist/Muddle/Leitmotif make tokens), ramps off treasure/rituals into big mana sinks (Volcanic Salvo, Chain Reaction, X-spells), and copies impactful spells/creatures (Twinflame, Rite of Replication, Brudiclad token-conversion). Wins by snowballing card/mana advantage from spell triggers, going wide with copy/elemental tokens, then closing with mass-damage burn or a wide Brudiclad/Goldspan beatdown.
fp: rampâ†’sink linking generic mana rocks (Arcane Signet, Sol Ring, Fellwar Stone, Talisman, Cursed Mirror) to every X-spell/mana-sink â€” these are deck-agnostic 'I have mana, you cost mana' edges, not deck-specific synergies; they inflate rampâ†’sink to 120 and pad degree on rocks

Goldspan Dragon â‡„ Rootha 'combatâ†’payoff' â€” Rootha is a token/mana-sink commander with no attack-trigger payoff that keys off Goldspan; the combatâ†’payoff family is being applied loosely to any attacker+token-maker pairing

magecraft edges from pure removal/interaction spells like Abrade, Reality Shift, Arcane Denial, Chaos Warp, Resculpt to every payoff â€” technically true (they are instants/sorceries) but they are interaction the deck casts reactively, not intentional synergy pieces, so counting each as strength-2 magecraft over-represents cohesion

Veyran â‡„ Harmonic Prodigy / Stormcatch Mentor tagged only as 'pump' â€” these are trigger/magecraft doublers stacking with each other, not a pump interaction; the family label is wrong even though an edge belongs there
fn: No storm / cast-count escalation concept: Storm-Kiln Artist making a treasure per spell, then those treasures fueling more cheap spells (often with Veyran doubling), is a self-propelling pseudo-storm engine the taxonomy cannot represent

Trigger-doubling as its own family: Veyran, Voice of Duality and Harmonic Prodigy literally double magecraft/other triggers â€” a multiplicative payoff that should be weighted far above a normal participant but is only seen as another magecraft node (and mislabeled 'pump')

Brudiclad's token-type CONVERSION (make all your tokens copies of your best token) is a distinct go-wide combo with Goldspan/Manaform/elemental token makers; it is collapsed into generic 'tokens' with no recognition of the 'convert + copy' payoff

Cost-reduction enabling the spell engine: Stormcatch Mentor, Thunderclap Drake, Harmonic Prodigy, Dirgur Focusmage all reduce instant/sorcery costs, which directly accelerates magecraft volume â€” the cost-reductionâ†’ability family is barely used (caps present but few edges) and misses the reductionâ†’magecraft loop
issues: rampâ†’sink over-weighted: generic mana rocks generate 120 edge instances by pairing with every X-spell, inflating cohesion for what is really baseline ramp, not synergy
magecraft family double-counts pure interaction/removal spells as synergy edges, so a deck that simply runs many instants/sorceries scores 'cohesive' even where the spells aren't engine-relevant
No trigger-DOUBLER concept â€” Veyran/Harmonic Prodigy are multiplicative payoffs treated as ordinary nodes and partly mislabeled 'pump'
No storm/cast-count escalation family to capture Storm-Kiln treasure-into-spell loops
combatâ†’payoff gate too loose: pairs any attacker/token-maker (e.g. Goldspan â‡„ Rootha) without checking for an actual attack-trigger payoff
hasCombo=false is correct (precon, no true infinite loop), and loops=0 is right â€” but the model has no way to flag the near-combo Brudiclad token-conversion go-wide line as a soft win condition

rawEvals.name: Upgrades Unleashed (Kamigawa: Neon Dynasty Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Gruul modified-matters / +1/+1 counters midrange (Auras + Equipment go-tall/wide).
fp: Ascendant Acolyte â‡„ ~22 creatures (counter-multiplier, s3): Acolyte is NOT a counter doubler â€” it is a creature that moves/places counters once; tagging it is-counter-multiplier and giving it strong s3 edges to every counter card massively inflates the +1/+1 engine.

Grumgully, the Generous â‡„ counter cards as 'counter-multiplier' (s3): Grumgully puts ONE counter on each nontoken creature as it ETBs (Hardened-Scales-ish only for tokens it makes); it is not a doubler of all counter sources, so the blanket s3 multiplier edges over-state synergy.

The ~80 'attach' edges among Equipment/Auras themselves (e.g. Bear Umbra â‡„ Sword of Vengeance, Bonehoard â‡„ Blackblade Reforged, Mage Slayer â‡„ Fireshrieker): two Equipment do not synergize with each other â€” they compete for the same creature. These are co-membership, not real interaction.
Komainu Battle Armor â‡„ Krenko / Ordeal as 'combat-enabler' s3: Komainu pairs creatures rather than granting a Krenko-style payoff; the s3 enabler weight is overstated.
Smoke Spirits' Aid / One with the Kami â‡„ Arcane Signet, Sol Ring as 'rampâ†’sink': these aren't a tuned ramp-into-sink line, just any mana payer linked to any mana rock â€” generic, low-signal.
fn: The 'modified-matters' payoff â€” the deck's actual theme. Chishiro makes a Spirit token AND puts counters whenever an Aura/Equipment ETBs (attach â†’ token/counter event). There is no taxonomy event linking 'a creature becomes modified' to its payoffs, so the central engine is invisible; attach is only scored as a generic co-membership family.

Aura/Equipment recursion & protection: Silkguard (protect creatures) and Swiftfoot Boots (hexproof) protecting the heavily-invested Aura'd commander/threat is a real defensive synergy in a Voltron-ish build, not modeled.
Bear Umbra's 'untap all lands on attack' as an explosive rampâ†’sink enabler (extra mana for the same-turn sink spells) is reduced to a plain attach edge, missing the totem-armor + free-mana payoff.

Big-creature card-draw payoff: Rishkar's Expertise / Soul's Majesty / Hunter's Insight draw based on power, which is exactly what the counters/Auras theme enables â€” flagged as ISLANDS (zero edges) despite being on-theme refuel.
issues: No 'modified' / Aura-or-Equipment-ETB event family, so a precon literally themed around it scores the theme as generic 'attach' co-membership.

'is-counter-multiplier' is applied to one-shot counter PLACERS (Ascendant Acolyte) and to additive effects (Grumgully), inflating both counters (112) and counter-multiplier (90) edge counts and the cohesion score.

'attach' family fires between every pair of Equipment/Auras (n^2 among gear), creating dozens of phantom strong-looking edges; should only edge gearâ†’creature or gearâ†’attach-payoff, not gearâ†’gear.

Power-based card draw (Rishkar's Expertise, Soul's Majesty, Hunter's Insight) has no event, leaving on-theme payoffs stranded as islands and depressing pctInteractive while the rest is inflated â€” the score is built on the wrong edges.

Cohesion 60 'Cohesive' is too high: strip the gear-vs-gear attach edges and the bogus Acolyte/Grumgully multiplier edges and the true synergy density is closer to a mid-band precon; hasCombo:false is correct (no real loop here).

rawEvals.name: Desert Bloom (Outlaws of Thunder Junction Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Naya/Gruul-Selesnya lands-matter landfall tokens midrange. Yuma turns lands into Plant tokens and sacrifices lands for cards; the deck ramps lands out, triggers landfall payoffs (Avenger of Zendikar, Scute Swarm, Omnath Locus of Rage, Nesting Dragon, Rampaging Baloths-style), self-mills lands, then recurs them (World Shaper, Titania, Splendid/Marshal's recursion, Ramunap Excavator, Wreck and Rebuild) to re-trigger landfall and build a wide board. It closes with go-wide overrun payoffs (Return of the Wildspeaker, Rumbleweed, Craterhoof-style alpha strikes).
fp: Angel of Indemnity â‡„ Bitter Reunion / Nantuko Cultivator / Elvish Rejuvenator (copyâ†’trigger): Angel of Indemnity reanimates and is treated as a 'copy' source, generating dozens of copyâ†’trigger edges to any ETB creature, but it returns one creature once â€” these are not a copy engine and not real synergy.

Scute Swarm â‡„ Satyr Wayfinder / Skullwinder / Bitter Reunion / Nantuko Cultivator (copyâ†’trigger): Scute Swarm's 'copy' is landfall-gated self-replication of itself, not a way to copy other creatures' ETBs; pairing it with arbitrary ETB bodies as copyâ†’trigger is spurious.

Marshal's Anthem â‡„ Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Chromatic Lantern (rampâ†’sink): Marshal's Anthem's multikicker is a minor mana use, not a meaningful ramp payoff; flagging every mana rock as feeding it is noise.
Dune Chanter / Heaven // Earth â‡„ mana rocks (rampâ†’sink): these mana-sink labels are weak/incidental and not lines the deck is built around.

The large 'graveyard' family cluster (e.g. Perpetual Timepiece â‡„ Winding Way, Kirri â‡„ Eccentric Farmer): most are mere co-membership (both interact with the yard) rather than a directed enablerâ†’payoff synergy, yet they count as 215 edge instances.
fn: Landfall via LAND RECURSION: the engine World Shaper / Titania / Splendid Reclamation-style / Ramunap Excavator returning sacrificed/milled lands to re-trigger landfall payoffs (Avenger, Scute Swarm, Omnath, Nesting Dragon, Yuma) is the deck's core loop and is not modeled as a directed family â€” there is no 'land-recursionâ†’landfall' or Crucible/extra-land-drop event.

Sacrifice-lands-for-value / fetch-and-sac synergy: Harrow, Springbloom Druid, Hour of Promise and Yuma sacrificing lands pair with Ramunap Excavator / Crucible-style replay and with landfall payoffs â€” an entire 'put lands in yard then replay them' subtheme that produces zero edges (Harrow and Map the Frontier are flagged as islands).
Desert subtheme: Yuma cares about Deserts and the set has Desert-matters cards; there is no Desert/land-typematters event in the taxonomy.

Extra-land-drops / lands-as-resource payoff (Oracle of Mul Daya, Ancient Greenwarden doubling landfall): Ancient Greenwarden's landfall-doubling and Oracle's extra land plays should be high-value landfall multipliers but are only captured as weak generic 'landfall'/'graveyard' edges, missing the multiplier relationship.
issues: The 'graveyard' family is a catch-all co-membership bucket (215 instances here) that simultaneously inflates avgDeg and, being weakly weighted, drags the cohesion band down â€” it should be split into directed self-millâ†’payoff vs reanimateâ†’target rather than 'both touch yard'.

No landfall-engine modeling beyond pairwise 'X plays a land, Y likes landfall': there is no representation that recurring LANDS (not creatures) is what fuels the landfall payoffs, so the deck's defining loop is invisible to the scorer.

copyâ†’trigger family is over-fired: it treats single-target reanimators (Angel of Indemnity) and self-replicating tokens (Scute Swarm) as generic copy sources, manufacturing many false edges (weight 2) that pad connectivity.

Island detection is too coarse: Harrow, Map the Frontier, and Springbloom-style fetch/sac-land effects are core to a lands deck yet flagged as islands because 'sacrifice/fetch a land' is not an event â€” leading to 7 'islands' that are actually on-theme.
rampâ†’sink fires on trivial mana uses (anthem multikicker, X-creatures), producing low-signal edges.

rawEvals.name: Death Toll (Duskmourn Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Golgari (B/G) graveyard-matters self-mill / reanimator midrange with a Spider-tribal sub-theme. Winter, Cynical Opportunist commits cards to the graveyard and grows as the yard fills; the deck self-mills (Stitcher's Supplier, Mulch, Grapple, Grisly Salvage, Nyx Weaver, Old Stickfingers, Skola Grovedancer, Ursine Monstrosity), pays off graveyard size (Carrion Grub, Winter, Old Stickfingers, Grist) and recurs threats (Reanimate, Whip of Erebos, Deadbridge Chant, Polluted Cistern, Moldgraf Monstrosity). A secondary go-wide Spider/insect-token plan (Ishkanah, Arachnogenesis, Hornet Queen, Crawling Sensation, Wrenn and Seven, Demonic Covenant) closes games. Wins by grinding value and beating down with big graveyard-fueled bodies and wide token boards rather than a combo.
fp: Mulch â‡„ Grisly Salvage (and most fuelâ‡„fuel graveyard edges) â€” two self-mill spells do not interact with each other; both just fill the yard. The graph turns a hub-and-spoke enabler relationship into a fully-connected clique.

Professor Onyx â‡„ Rampant Growth / Harrow / Night's Whisper (magecraft) â€” these are real triggers only while Onyx is in play, but they are weighted as standing synergies; Rampant Growth/Harrow are pure ramp whose 'spellcast payoff' value is marginal.

Giant Adephage â‡„ Rendmaw / Vile Mutilator / Noxious Gearhulk (copyâ†’trigger) â€” Adephage copies the WHOLE attacking creature, not just ETB-triggers; tagging arbitrary ETB/attack creatures as a 'copyâ†’trigger' engine overstates a synergy that is really just 'token-doubling on combat damage'.

Arcane Signet / Sol Ring / Mind Stone â‡„ Deluge of Doom / Convert to Slime (rampâ†’sink) â€” generic mana rocks feeding generic X/expensive spells is being scored as deck-specific synergy; this is just 'ramp exists', present in every deck.
Demonic Covenant â‡„ Hornet Queen / Arachnogenesis (combatâ†’payoff) â€” flagged as a combat synergy but the real link is shared insect/Spider token-count, not a combat trigger.
fn: Reanimation as a directional engine is invisible: Reanimate, Whip of Erebos, Deadbridge Chant, Polluted Cistern // Dim Oubliette and Moldgraf Monstrosity all return creatures FROM the yard, but they are lumped into the undirected 'graveyard' bucket with no 'self-millâ†’reanimation-target' relationship â€” the actual payoff line (mill a fatty, then Reanimate/Whip it) is not modeled as a value pair.

No event for delirium / card-type-count payoffs: Grim Flayer, Gnarlwood Dryad and Demolisher Spawn care about 4+ card types in the graveyard, a real build-around mechanic that the taxonomy has no family for (Grim Flayer's graveyard edges are coincidental, not delirium-aware).

Lifeloss/drain as a win path is under-captured: Professor Onyx, Ishkanah, Skola Grovedancer and Demonic Covenant generate incidental drain, but there is no 'lifeloss-payoff' clustering tying them into a recognizable attrition plan.

Scavenging Ooze's graveyard-hate / exile-to-grow is modeled only as a generic 'graveyard' consumer; its self-mill-fueled counter growth (a real payoff line with the deck's own yard) is not distinguished from opponent-hate.
issues: The 'graveyard' family is too coarse and undirected: it conflates fuel (self-mill), recursion (reanimation), and size-payoffs into one symmetric clique, producing 245 edge-instances and a 97% web that mechanically pushes cohesion up regardless of real synergy direction.
No directional enabler modeling for self-millâ†’reanimation or self-millâ†’graveyard-size-payoff; the strongest actual lines in the deck are recorded as undifferentiated strength-1 edges.
Missing delirium / card-type-count family â€” a core graveyard-deck mechanic has no representation.
copyâ†’trigger gate is too loose for Giant Adephage (whole-permanent copy on combat damage is treated like an ETB-copy combo engine).
rampâ†’sink fires on generic mana-rock + X-spell pairs, manufacturing synergy that is present in essentially every Commander deck and not archetype-defining here.

rawEvals.name: Sneak Attack (Zendikar Rising Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Dimir Rogues tribal â€” evasive go-wide aggro that mills opponents (and itself) and converts the mill into reanimation/theft value. Anowon, the Ruin Thief is the Rogue lord that draws+mills on combat damage from Rogues; Soaring Thought-Thief is a second mill-lord. The deck wins by chipping in with unblockable Rogues while milling everyone, then leveraging full graveyards via reanimation (Extract from Darkness, Endless Obedience, Fated Return, Sepulchral Primordial) and graveyard-size payoffs (Consuming Aberration), with a light faeries/+1+1-counter sub-line and a minor aristocrats package (Zulaport Cutthroat, Ogre Slumlord).
fp: Anowon, the Ruin Thief â‡„ Zulaport Cutthroat / Notion Thief / Sygg / Invisible Stalker tagged lordâ†’tribe â€” Anowon only buffs/triggers off Rogues; Zulaport (no Rogue), Sygg, and most of these are not Rogues, so the lordâ†’tribe edge is wrong for any non-Rogue creature it connected to

Fact or Fiction â‡„ Rise from the Grave / Endless Obedience / Fated Return / Necromantic Selection (graveyard) â€” FoF merely fills your own yard and has no payoff relationship with these reanimators; coincidental yard-filling counted as synergy

rampâ†’sink edges from every mana rock (Sol Ring, Arcane/Dimir Signet, Mind Stone, Lockets) to Obelisk of Urd / Open into Wonder / Notorious Throng â€” generic 'I have mana, you cost mana' pairing inflates the largest web with non-synergies

Nighthowler â‡„ Blackblade Reforged / Heirloom Blade / Whispersteel Dagger (attach) â€” Nighthowler is an Aura/bestow creature, not an Equipment carrier; clustering it with the equipment package via a generic 'attach' family is a mismatch
Murder â‡„ Fated Return and In Garruk's Wake â‡„ Fated Return (destroy) â€” destroying things has no real interaction with a single reanimation instant; these are noise edges
fn: Evasion / unblockable as the core engine: Anowon ('Rogues you control can't be blocked... whenever a Rogue deals combat damage, draw and each opponent mills') is the deck's entire gameplan, yet there is no capability or event for 'grants unblockable' or 'Rogue combat-damage trigger.' Changeling Outcast, Invisible Stalker, Slither Blade, Triton Shorestalker, Marang River Prowler, Sure-Footed Infiltrator are unblockable/evasive enablers that should connect strongly to Anowon's draw+mill payoff but are mostly islands or only weakly lord-linked

Self-mill as a self-enabler: the deck mills ITSELF to fuel reanimation and Consuming Aberration/Nighthowler (Bonehoard) size â€” there is no 'self-millâ†’reanimation fuel' or 'self-millâ†’graveyard-size payoff' directionality, so the engine that makes the reanimation suite live is invisible

Changeling Outcast flagged as an island despite being a changeling (it IS a Rogue / every creature type) â€” a tribal deck's universal-tribe enabler should be a maximal lordâ†’tribe hub, not zero-degree; the taxonomy missed changeling typing

Theft/encore reanimation distinction: Sepulchral Primordial and Extract from Darkness specifically steal from OPPONENTS' graveyards (which the mill plan fills) â€” a 'mill opponents â†’ reanimate their stuff' line that the model doesn't recognize
issues: No 'unblockable/evasion-granting' capability and no 'tribal-attack-trigger payoff' event â€” fatal for a commander whose text IS those two things; the deck's win condition is untracked

lordâ†’tribe family applied by color/type-pair too loosely: it links the commander to non-tribe-members (any creature) instead of gating on the actual creature subtype (Rogue/Faerie), creating false hub edges
graveyard family is a catch-all: any yard-filler pairs with any reanimator/yard-payoff with no directionality (fill vs. payoff), heavily over-counting (61 edge instances) and inflating cohesion
Changeling typing not modeled â€” Changeling Outcast wrongly an island in a tribal deck
rampâ†’sink (61 instances) is over-weighted: generic mana-rock-to-X-cost-thing edges dominate the graph and the largestWebShare without representing real synergy
'attach' family conflates Auras (Nighthowler bestow) with Equipment, mis-clustering the equipment package

rawEvals.name: Reap the Tides (Commander Legends Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Simic (GU) landfall / lands-matter ramp midrange. Aesi gives an extra land drop and draws a card on every landfall, fueling ramp that feeds big landfall payoffs (Avenger of Zendikar, Rampaging Baloths, Sporemound, Molimo) and fat blue sea-monster finishers. Wins by out-resourcing the table on card advantage, then closing with tokens or evasive giants.
fp: Molimo, Maro-Sorcerer â‡„ every creature (tribal-countâ†’payoff, ~21 edges) â€” Molimo's power/toughness equals the number of LANDS you control, not creatures of a type; it is a landfall/lands payoff, so wiring it to Coiling Oracle, Mulldrifter, sea-monsters, etc. as a tribal-count hub is wrong

Murkfiend Liege â‡„ all 19 creatures (lordâ†’tribe) â€” Murkfiend Liege is a soft global +1/+1 anthem for green AND blue creatures plus a vigilance-on-others ability, not a tribal lord keyed to a creature type; treating it as a tribal hub massively overstates a generic stat buff

Nezahal, Primal Tide â‡„ Acidic Slime / Coiling Oracle / Mulldrifter / Eternal Witness / Sphinx of Uthuun / Avenger / Reclamation Sage / Terastodon / Scourge of Fleets / Verdant Sun's Avatar / Stumpsquall (etbâ†’blink, s=3) â€” Nezahal does NOT blink your other creatures; its only flicker is exiling ITSELF, so these strong combo-ish etbâ†’blink edges are fabricated
Aesi â‡„ Murkfiend Liege (lordâ†’tribe) â€” same anthem-as-lord mislabel applied to the commander
Aesi â‡„ Molimo (tribal-countâ†’payoff) â€” both are landfall/lands cards; the relationship is lands-matter, not tribal count
fn: +1/+1 counters subtheme â€” Fathom Mage (evolve draws), Avenger of Zendikar (counters on plants), Retreat to Kazandu, Sharktocrab, Shipbreaker Kraken, Stumpsquall Hydra, Khalni/Ior Ruin Expeditions all use +1/+1 or quest counters; there is no counters/evolve/proliferate family capturing this
Land recursion engine â€” Ramunap Excavator (play lands from graveyard) with fetch-style/expedition lands and Yavimaya Elder is a real lands-matter value loop only weakly hinted by graveyard edges

Landfall-draw payoff loop â€” Aesi + Seer's Sundial + Ior Ruin/Khalni Heart Expeditions turning each land drop into cards is the deck's actual advantage engine, but it is modeled only as flat s=1 landfall edges, with no 'landfallâ†’draw payoff' weighting
Mass-bounce / unblockable wincon plan â€” Slinn Voda, Stormtide Leviathan (Islandwalk lock), Whelming Wave, Scourge of Fleets as a tempo/finisher package is not recognized as a synergy family
issues: No way to distinguish a LAND-count payoff (Molimo) from a CREATURE-typecount payoff â€” Molimo is forced into tribal-countâ†’payoff and becomes the single highest-degree node (deg 31), inflating cohesion
