Anthem / soft-lord detection is too loose: Murkfiend Liege (a global +1/+1 buff) is treated as a tribal lord and cliqued to every creature, double-counting with the Molimo clique
is-blink capability mis-tagged on Nezahal, Primal Tide (self-exile, not blink), producing fabricated strong etbâ†’blink edges
Missing families: +1/+1 counters / evolve / proliferate, and a landfallâ†’draw payoff weighting distinct from generic landfall
Flat-clique modeling of landfall and the two pseudo-lord hubs inflates avgDeg (6.14) and edge count (188) without representing distinct synergy lines

rawEvals.name: Deep Clue Sea (Murders at Karlov Manor Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Simic/Bant Clue-tokens "draw-matters" midrange-value engine: Investigate generates Clue tokens (which are sacrificed to draw), and a critical mass of "whenever you draw your second/Nth card" payoffs (Morska, Nadir Kraken, Jolrael, Psychosis Crawler, Bennie Bracks, Alandra) convert that card flow into counters, bodies, life-drain, and tokens. Wins by grinding overwhelming card advantage into a wide board (token doublers Adrix and Nev / Esix / Academy Manufactor) and finishing with Psychosis Crawler drain or evasive token swarms.
fp: sac-fodder->outlet between Sophia, Dogged Detective and nearly every token-maker: Sophia only sacrifices an artifact/creature/enchantment when it ATTACKS for a counter (it is a combat-payoff, not a free sac engine), so the dozens of 'sac-fodder->outlet' edges to Graf Mole, Hornet Queen, Hydroid Krasis, etc. overstate a sac-aristocrats subtheme this deck does not have

copy->trigger between Mechanized Production and ETB creatures (Hornet Queen, Thought Monitor, Wavesifter, Aerial Extortionist, Killer Service): Mechanized Production copies an ARTIFACT at the end of turn and is a wincon enabler, it does not re-trigger creature ETBs, so these s:2 edges are spurious

ramp->sink edges from mana rocks (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Azorius/Simic Signets) to Knowledge Is Power: these treat any X-cost or mana-using card as a meaningful 'sink' for ramp, inflating the ramp->sink family on generic mana usage

Fumigate/Organic Extinction 'destroy' -> Koma, Cosmos Serpent: flagged as an interaction but these are symmetric/own-board wraths, not a synergy with Koma (which makes indestructible-ish tokens at most incidentally)
fn: The Clue/Investigate token cycle as a first-class mechanic: make Clue -> sacrifice Clue paying 2 to draw -> draw triggers payoff. The taxonomy has no 'investigate'/'clue' family and no 'artifact-token -> crack for card' enabler, so the deck's namesake engine is invisible, scattered across generic tokens/sac-fodder

Draw-count multipliers / 'extra draw each turn' as an amplifier family: Teferi's Ageless Insight and Alandra, Sky Dreamer double card draw, and Morska/Nadir Kraken/Psychosis Crawler care about EACH draw â€” there is no event recognizing that a draw-doubler multiplies the value of every other draw source (a true force-multiplier, not a flat s:1 draw edge)

Academy Manufactor's token-type conversion (Clues+Treasures+Food all trigger each other) is a strong build-around with these Clue makers, but it is captured only as generic 'tokens'/'treasure' edges, missing that it triples the deck's primary resource

Counters-matters subtheme: Morska, Nadir Kraken, Tireless Tracker, Kappa Cannoneer, Sophia and Hydroid Krasis put +1/+1 counters as draw payoffs, with Adrix and Nev only doubling TOKENS â€” the classifier lumps counter growth under generic counter-multiplier (6) and does not connect the counter payoffs to the draw engine
issues: No 'investigate/clue' family despite it being a defined evergreen-adjacent mechanic and the entire deck's theme â€” generic 'tokens' cannot distinguish a Clue (crack-for-card) from a creature token

Strength assignment is too flat: the deck's actual wincon edges (draw source -> Psychosis Crawler / Morska / Nadir Kraken draw payoffs) are scored s:1 'weak', identical to incidental draw overlap, so the cohesion math cannot tell a focused draw-payoff deck from a good-stuff pile

Single-axis decks are systematically under-scored: when a deck's whole identity is ONE reinforcing pair of families (draw+tokens here), weightedAvgDeg dilutes it and the band lands at 'Pile of good-stuff' even though it is one of the most internally-consistent precons
sac-fodder->outlet gate is too loose: it treats a conditional combat/attack sacrifice (Sophia) as a general sac outlet, generating large numbers of false aristocrats edges
copy->trigger conflates artifact-copy wincons (Mechanized Production) with ETB-recursion copy effects, creating spurious s:2 'moderate' edges to every ETB creature

rawEvals.name: Lorehold Spirit (Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Boros (R/W) graveyard-value / Spirit-token go-wide midrange built on the Quintorius "exile cards from graveyards to make Spirit tokens" engine. It self-mills (Millikin, Kirol, Advanced Reconstruction, Faithless Looting, Currency Converter, Conspiracy Theorist, Containment Construct), recurs creatures (Hofri Ghostforge, Sun Titan, Karmic Guide, Teshar, Sevinne's Reclamation, Angel of Indemnity), pumps out Spirit tokens via the three Quintorius cards + Lorehold Archivist, and closes with a go-wide overrun (Moonshaker Cavalry, Lorehold Charm) under Spirit lords (Vanguard of the Restless, Quintorius Field Historian, Balefire Liege). Treasure sub-theme (Relic Retriever, Atsushi, Monologue Tax, Seize the Spoils) ramps. No real combo â€” it's a fair value/tokens midrange deck.
fp: Quintorius History Chaser <-> Sun Titan / Karmic Guide / Squee / Vanguard etc. (f: graveyard, s:1) â€” these are scored as graveyard synergies but are just 'both touch the graveyard' adjacency; many are not directed interactions (e.g. Quintorius mills, Sun Titan reanimates a permanent <=3 CMC â€” they don't actually feed each other in a meaningful loop)

The fully-connected 'graveyard' web among self-mill sources themselves, e.g. Millikin <-> Perpetual Timepiece, Millikin <-> Currency Converter, Currency Converter <-> Conspiracy Theorist (discard) â€” multiple enablers are cross-linked to each other as if synergistic when they merely share a keyword and don't combine
Anger <-> Balefire Liege (lord->tribe, s:1) â€” Anger is not a Spirit and Balefire Liege only buffs Spirits; this lord/tribe edge is spurious
Naktamun Lorespinner <-> Balefire Liege (lord->tribe) â€” again not a Spirit, spurious tribal edge

Augusta, Order Returned <-> Drumbellower / Moonshaker (combat-enabler s:3) graded as strongly as real combat payoffs â€” Drumbellower untaps creatures and Augusta counts attackers, a loose at best link being over-weighted to strength 3
fn: The deck's signature payoff â€” EXILING cards from a graveyard to create Spirit tokens (Quintorius History Chaser, Quintorius Loremaster, Lorehold Archivist) â€” has no 'exile-from-graveyard -> token' event; this directed mill-fuels-Quintorius-payoff engine is the deck's actual win condition and is entirely absent from the taxonomy, buried inside generic 'graveyard' adjacency

Spellcast / historic / artifact-or-legendary-cast triggers: Quintorius Loremaster and the 'cast a noncreature/historic spell' payoffs (Laelia, Kirol, Conspiracy Theorist's prowess-style growth) are spellslinger-adjacent triggers with no dedicated cast->payoff family distinct from the generic 'cast' tag
Lifegain matters / drain (Balefire Liege deals damage equal to lifegain, Serra Paragon, Angel of Indemnity, Atsushi) â€” a lifegain-payoff loop exists but lifegain is barely tracked

Death/leaves-the-battlefield aristocrat value (Atsushi dies->treasure+cards, Selfless Spirit sac, Lorehold Charm sac for value, Karmic Guide/Sun Titan reanimating the sacrificed bodies) â€” there is a real sac-fodder-to-recursion loop the classifier does not surface as a death/aristocrats family
issues: No 'exile-from-graveyard -> token' event despite this being a flagship mechanic (all three Quintorius cards + Lorehold Archivist); it collapses into generic 'graveyard'

'graveyard' family is a catch-all mega-bucket (197 edge instances) that fully-connects every mill source, every recursion spell, and every graveyard-hate body, inflating edge count with low-value adjacency while obscuring the real directed engine â€” needs splitting into self-mill-fuel, reanimate-from-yard, and exile-payoff sub-families
lord->tribe edges are emitted to non-tribal members (Anger, Naktamun Lorespinner linked to Balefire Liege) â€” the lord/tribe matcher does not verify the buffed creature's type
combat-enabler graded at strength 3 (combo-critical-adjacent) for loose links (Drumbellower untap, Augusta attacker-count) over-weights situational combat tricks

Despite all this graveyard density the cohesion lands at 48 ('Loosely connected'), likely because weightedAvgDeg (4.46) penalizes the many strength-1 edges; the band undersells a precon that is actually quite focused around one graveyard/Spirit engine

rawEvals.name: Blame Game (Murders at Karlov Manor Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Boros goad-politics / group-slug
fp: Elspeth, Sun's Champion â‡„ ~15 creatures tagged 'lordâ†’tribe' â€” Elspeth is not a lord and grants no tribal anthem; her +1 makes 1/1 Soldier tokens, so calling every creature (Wall of Omens, Windborn Muse, Sun Titan, Etali, Gisela, Feather, etc.) a 'tribe' member of Elspeth is a fabricated family. This single false node is the deck's highest-degree card (deg 32), badly distorting the graph.

Feather, Radiant Arbiter â‡„ Wall of Omens / Solemn Simulacrum / Sun Titan / Stalking Leonin / Angel of the Ruins via 'copyâ†’trigger' â€” Feather only copies/recurs instants and sorceries you cast targeting your own permanents; she does nothing with these creatures' ETB triggers, so these copyâ†’trigger edges are spurious.

rampâ†’sink edges from every mana rock (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Fellwar Stone, Mind Stone, Thought Vessel) to Havoc Eater and Steel Hellkite â€” generic 'I have a mana sink' is not a real synergy line; this is just ramp + a creature with an activated ability, flagged as if it were an intentional engine.

Take the Bait â‡„ Curse of Opulence / Kazuul as strength-3 'combat-enabler' â€” Take the Bait untaps and goads; pairing it at combo-tier strength with random attack payoffs overstates a one-shot political effect.
fn: Damage-reflection / punisher engine: Brash Taunter, Boros Reckoner, Stuffy-style redirection plus Deflecting Palm and Comeuppance turn goaded combat damage back on opponents â€” a core win/defense line. The taxonomy only has a thin 'damage' family (8 instances) and never connects these to the goad engine that FEEDS them (goad forces attacks -> Brash Taunter blocks/takes damage -> pings everyone), so the deck's actual kill synergy is invisible.

Pillow-fort / attack-tax package: Ghostly Prison, Windborn Muse, Soul Snare, Kazuul as deterrents â€” these gate opponents away from YOU while goad redirects them sideways, a deliberate political shell. Three of these (Ghostly Prison, Soul Snare) land in islandCards with zero detected interactions.

Vote/council mechanic: Mob Verdict produces and consumes 'vote' yet is an island (deg 0); Spectacular Showdown and Prisoner's Dilemma are also will-of-the-council/political cards whose group-politics interaction with the goad plan is not modeled.

Goad 'damage payoff' link: cards like Trouble in Pairs, Smuggler's Share, and Nelly herself reward the combat that goad CAUSES â€” the directional line goad-source -> forced-attack -> draw/treasure payoff is the deck's actual loop, but it is only captured as undifferentiated symmetric 'goad' co-membership rather than a source->payoff engine.
issues: No 'goad-source -> forced-attack -> payoff' directional family. Goad is modeled as a flat symmetric tag, so 118 mutual edges inflate degree without distinguishing enablers (Bloodthirsty Blade, Disrupt Decorum) from payoffs (Nelly, Trouble in Pairs, Kazuul) â€” this both over-counts noise and hides the real engine direction.
lordâ†’tribe family fires on token-makers/planeswalkers (Elspeth) that are not lords and share no creature type, producing a large fake hub. Needs an actual shared-subtype + anthem gate.
No 'damage-reflection / redirect' family (Brash Taunter, Boros Reckoner, Deflecting Palm, Comeuppance), which is a recurring Boros archetype and this deck's payoff/defense core.
No 'attack-tax / pillow-fort' or 'vote/council' event, leaving Ghostly Prison, Soul Snare, Windborn Muse, Mob Verdict, Spectacular Showdown stranded as islands despite clear thematic roles.
copyâ†’trigger gate is too loose: Feather (instant/sorcery copy) is matched against creature ETB producers, generating multiple phantom strength-2 edges.

Cohesion 26 ('Pile of good-stuff') under-rates a tightly-themed precon: the metrics correctly show pctInteractive 87 and largestWebShare 87, yet the band label contradicts that by calling it a goodstuff pile â€” the scoring weights weightedAvgDeg (2.02) in a way that punishes a focused single-axis (goad) theme.

rawEvals.name: Spirit Squadron (Innistrad: Crimson Vow Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: UW (Azorius) Spirit tribal go-wide tokens. Millicent, Restless Revenant heads a flyers-matter deck that floods the board with 1/1 flying Spirit tokens (Ethereal Investigator, Hallowed Spiritkeeper, Field of Souls, Promise of Bunrei, Twilight Drover, Hanged Executioner, Oyobi, Geist of Saint Traft), pumps them with Spirit lords (Drogskol Captain, Supreme Phantom, Drogskol Reinforcements), and converts the wide flying board into damage and card draw (Reconnaissance Mission, Benevolent Offering, Millicent's own count-based pump/cost reduction). Secondary lines: flash/instant-speed value and copy/blink via Rattlechains, Nebelgast Herald, Donal Herald of Wings, plus a light counters subtheme (Custodi Soulbinders, Twilight Drover, Rhoda).
fp: Disorder in the Court â‡„ Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Azorius Signet / Marble Diamond / Sky Diamond (rampâ†’sink): a one-shot X-token instant is tagged as a mana sink linked to every rock; this is generic 'spends mana' noise, not a synergy â€” same for Occult Epiphany, Hallowed Spiritkeeper, Priest of the Blessed Graf against all rocks

Millicent â‡„ Windborn Muse / Shacklegeist / Spectral Sailor / Kami of the Crescent Moon (tribal-countâ†’payoff): these are flagged purely for being Spirit bodies that increment a count, but they do nothing to actively enable Millicent beyond existing â€” the edge is true-by-existence padding, not a real interaction line
Darksteel Mutation â‡„ Fell the Mighty / Kirtar's Wrath (destroy): pairing a pacifism aura with board wipes as a 'destroy' synergy is spurious; they share no intentional interaction
Haunted Library â‡„ Fell the Mighty / Kirtar's Wrath (sacrifice): the wraths force sacrifice broadly, not a deliberate sac-fodder-into-Haunted-Library engine

Swords to Plowshares / Crush Contraband / Remorseful Cleric â‡„ Twilight Drover (leaves): removal spells and a creature with a leaves trigger are linked as if removing your own Drover were a plan; incidental, not synergy
fn: Flyers-matter / evasion as a family: nearly every creature is a flying Spirit and key payoffs (Millicent triggers on flyers, Reconnaissance Mission draws on combat-damage flyers, the lords pump fliers) reward evasion specifically â€” there is no evasion/keyword-tribe event so this central axis is invisible
Flash-granting enabler: Rattlechains gives Spirits flash and protects them, a defining Spirit-tribal value line, but Rattlechains shows deg 9 with no edge representing the flash-grant payoff

Ghostly Prison / Windborn Muse pillowfort/Propaganda taxing defense: Ghostly Prison is a flagged island and Windborn Muse's tax effect is ignored â€” the deck's stax/defensive 'attack-tax' subtheme has no family

Storm of Souls / Field of Souls / Kirtar's Wrath as a board-wipe-into-Spirit-tokens recovery package (asymmetric wrath synergy): these turn opponents' or your own dying creatures into Spirits but are largely treated as isolated, missing the 'wrath then repopulate as Spirits' engine
issues: tribal-countâ†’payoff (126 instances) is over-weighted: it fires for every creature being a countable body, producing a near-complete star graph from Millicent/Spectral Arcanist/Benevolent Offering and inflating avgDeg (10.2) and cohesion beyond the deck's true synergy density
rampâ†’sink is too loose: any X-spell or mana-sink creature gets edged to every mana rock (63 instances), generating dozens of low-value 'spends mana' edges that are not real synergies
No evasion/keyword-tribe event in the taxonomy, so a flyers-matter deck's defining payoff axis cannot be modeled
No flash-grant / instant-speed enabler family to capture Rattlechains-style 'cast tribe at instant speed' value
No defensive-tax / pillowfort family, leaving Ghostly Prison and Windborn Muse's relevant effects as islands or unmodeled
islandCards flags Flood of Tears (a board-bounce reset that synergizes with redeploying a wide token board) and Ghostly Prison as zero-interaction, missing their real but non-taxonomized roles

rawEvals.name: Graveyard Overdrive (Modern Horizons 3 Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Sultai (BUG) graveyard / Lhurgoyf-tribal midrange built around Disa the Restless. Plan: aggressively self-mill to stuff the graveyard, then deploy graveyard-size payoffs ("*goyf" creatures: Tarmogoyf clones, Necrogoyf, Barrowgoyf, Mortivore, Lhurgoyf, Coram, Polygoyf) that scale with the yard. Disa makes Lhurgoyf token copies from the top of the graveyard each turn, and Syr Konrad punishes every creature death / card leaving a graveyard with direct damage. Reanimation (Liliana, Junji, Deadbridge Chant, Graveshifter) recurs threats; Izoni provides a sac outlet + card draw; the deck closes via giant trampling bodies plus Altar of the Goyf / Brawn overrun.
fp: Exterminator Magmarch â‡„ ~every instant/sorcery (Maelstrom Pulse, Terminate, Rampant Growth, Chandra's Ignition, Syphon Mind, Final Act, etc.) tagged 'magecraft' â€” Magmarch copies the NEXT spell once; it is not a magecraft payoff and copying a single removal/ramp spell is not a per-card synergy engine

Exterminator Magmarch + Eternal Witness/Graveshifter/Stitcher's Supplier/Archon/Accursed Marauder 'copyâ†’trigger' (s2) â€” these are generic 'copy a creature spell for an extra ETB' value edges, massively inflated into ~a dozen edges off one card
Tempt with Mayhem / Polygoyf 'copyâ†’trigger' fan-out to every ETB creature is similarly over-counted one-time value, not a repeatable combo line
Garruk, Apex Predator â‡„ Ignoble Hierarch 'pump' (s1) â€” coincidental capability match, not a real synergy
Brawn / Altar of the Goyf 'go-wideâ†’payoff' to single token-makers (Ziatora, Siege-Gang, Broodmate Tyrant) at s3 overstates strength â€” this deck is big-bodies, not a true go-wide tokens shell
fn: Disa the Restless's defining ability â€” making Lhurgoyf token copies of creatures milled to the top of the graveyard â€” has no event; the commander shows deg 3 and is treated as a generic token producer, completely missing the deck's namesake engine

Deathreap Ritual flagged as an island, but it draws cards whenever a creature dies â€” a real payoff in a deck full of sac (Izoni, Find//Finality, Riveteers Charm, Grist, Final Act) and self-bricking goyfs; there is no creature-deathâ†’draw family

Syr Konrad's damage-on-death / cards-leaving-graveyard punisher line is under-modeled â€” he is the deck's secondary win condition (drain via mass death + Liliana/Find//Finality/Izoni token sacrifice) but is only tied in via the broad 'graveyard' family, not a death-trigger/aristocrats-drain family
No 'graveyard-size-payoff scales with mill' edge weighting that captures how these creatures can become lethal beaters; lordâ†’tribe via Altar is caught but the raw P/T-scaling dimension is absent
issues: Exterminator Magmarch demonstrates a copy/magecraft gate that is far too loose: a one-shot copy creature becomes a 30-degree hub, inflating both copyâ†’trigger and magecraft edge counts and likely propping up the cohesion score
No commander-ability parsing: Disa's millâ†’token-copy mechanic produces no event, so the most synergistic card in the deck is a near-island
Missing a creature-deathâ†’draw / aristocrats-payoff family causes a genuine engine card (Deathreap Ritual) to be mislabeled an island
Syr Konrad's leaves-graveyard / dies damage trigger collapses into the generic 'graveyard' bucket, losing the drain win-condition signal
go-wideâ†’payoff strength (s3) is applied to a big-creature deck, suggesting the family over-credits any token producer + anthem-ish payoff regardless of actual board-width plan

rawEvals.name: 20 Ways to Win (Secret Lair Commander 2024 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: A 5-color "alternate win-condition" goodstuff pile under Go-Shintai of Life's Origin. The deck's literal theme is jamming ~13+ independent "you win the game" cards (Test of Endurance, Felidar Sovereign, Approach of the Second Sun, Mechanized Production, Biovisionary, Triskaidekaphile, Helix Pinnacle, Simic Ascendancy, Mayael's Aria, Happily Ever After, Revel in Riches, Liliana's Contract, Halo Fountain) and trying to assemble any one of them. It is NOT a unified synergy engine: it wins by parallel, mutually-independent win cons, lightly supported by lifegain (life-total wins), ramp/counters (Helix Pinnacle, Simic Ascendancy), treasures (Revel), and copy effects (Mechanized Production / Biovisionary). Go-Shintai is a value/recursion shell, not a combo piece.
fp: Shalai, Voice of Plenty â‡„ ~everything (lordâ†’tribe, 25 edges) â€” Shalai is not a tribal lord; it gives hexproof to you/your stuff and pumps via its own counter ability. Treating it as a 'lord' that synergizes with every creature is spurious; these are not real synergy edges.

Yahenni â‡„ ~everything (sac-fodderâ†’outlet, 33 edges) â€” Yahenni is a single sac outlet with no sac payoff in the deck (no aristocrats drain, no death-trigger engine). Pairing every creature as 'fodder' for it is meaningless connectivity; the deck has no sacrifice value loop.

Shiny Impetus / Ghoulish Impetus combatâ†’payoff and rampâ†’sink edges (e.g. Shiny Impetus â‡„ Sphinx's Revelation, â‡„ Hellkite Tyrant) â€” the Impetuses are political goad/treasure auras you stick on OPPONENTS' creatures; they do not meaningfully 'pay off' your combat or feed your mana sinks as modeled.

Drown in Dreams / Sphinx's Revelation â‡„ every mana rock (rampâ†’sink) â€” these are X-spells that use mana, but flagging Sol Ring/Arcane Signet/Chromatic Lantern as dedicated 'rampâ†’sink' partners is generic mana-curve noise, not a synergy.

Liliana's Contract â‡„ Mechanized Production / Rite of Replication (copyâ†’trigger, strength 2) â€” Liliana's Contract is a draw enchantment + a 4-demon wincon; it is not a 'copy trigger' payoff for those cards. Wrong family attribution.
Go-Shintai â‡„ Hellkite Tyrant / Mangara / Shiny Impetus (combatâ†’payoff) â€” Go-Shintai does not reward attacking; these combatâ†’payoff edges are artifacts of capability tagging, not real lines.
fn: ALTERNATE WIN CONDITIONS as a family â€” the deck's entire identity. No event/capability exists for 'you win the game' cards, so Test of Endurance, Felidar Sovereign, Approach of the Second Sun, Helix Pinnacle, Simic Ascendancy, Mayael's Aria, Happily Ever After, Triskaidekaphile, Halo Fountain are not recognized as wincons at all.

Lifegain â†’ life-total-wincon enabler: the lifegain cluster (Heliod, Soul's Attendant, Auriok Champion, Sphinx's Revelation, Felidar Sovereign) directly feeds Test of Endurance (50 life) and Felidar Sovereign (40 life), but the classifier never links lifegain producers to those wincons (Test of Endurance is an island with deg 0).

Ramp/counters â†’ Helix Pinnacle (100 power counters) and Simic Ascendancy (20 growth counters): mana ramp and +1/+1 counter generators should enable these counter-wins, but Helix Pinnacle and Simic Ascendancy are near-islands with no edges to the ramp or counters packages.

Copy/token-doubling â†’ Biovisionary (4 copies) and Mechanized Production (8 artifacts): Rite of Replication and Maskwood Nexus are the actual enablers for these wincons, but Biovisionary is only weakly linked (lordâ†’tribe/sac noise) and its real 'make 4 of me' line is absent.
Approach of the Second Sun's cast-twice line and tutoring/recursion support (Sun Titan, Eternal Wanderer blink to re-buy, Go-Shintai recursion) as a path to repeated wincon access is not modeled.
issues: No 'alternate win condition' capability/family in the taxonomy â€” fatal for any deck themed around 'you win the game' cards; these get dumped as utility/islands.

Hub-node inflation: a single broad-tag card (Yahenni as 'is-sac-outlet', Shalai as 'is-lord') generates 25-35 low-strength edges, inflating avgDeg (6.25) and largestWebShare (86%) and making a pile look more connected than it is. Needs payoff-presence gating (no sac payoff => suppress sac-fodder edges; no tribe => suppress lord edges).
lordâ†’tribe family fires on non-lord cards (Shalai) â€” the 'is-lord' classifier is mis-tagging static-anthem/hexproof effects as tribal lords.
rampâ†’sink over-weighted (46 instances) by pairing every X-spell/mana-sink with every mana rock; this is curve, not synergy, and should be heavily discounted.

Political 'goad' auras (Shiny/Ghoulish Impetus) that target OPPONENTS' creatures are being credited as the controller's combat payoffs/enablers; goad direction (your stuff vs opponents') is not distinguished.

No way to recognize that a deck is intentionally diffuse â€” but here the low cohesion score (19) is actually correct, so the scoring outcome is right even though it is right for partly the wrong reasons (it lacks the wincon family rather than correctly judging the wincons as non-synergistic).

rawEvals.name: From Cute to Brute (Secret Lair Commander 2023 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: 5-color "transform/double-faced-card matters" goodstuff midrange. Esika makes the huge density of legendary creatures tap for any color and The Prismatic Bridge cheats creatures/planeswalkers (mostly DFCs) into play each upkeep. The deck is built almost entirely out of double-faced cards â€” Innistrad werewolves (Tovolar's Huntmaster, Bloodline Keeper, Archangel Avacyn, Voldaren Pariah), MDFC planeswalkers (Kytheon, Jace VP, Liliana HH, Nissa VS, Garruk Relentless, Chandra FoK), Kaldheim Gods (Valki, Kolvori, Cosima, Esika), and Strixhaven Deans (Plargg, Valentin, Shaile) â€” that flip into bigger threats ("cute to brute"). It wins by ramping, flipping/cheating big legendaries onto the board, and grinding out a value/midrange board, with a secondary go-wide token line (Rhys, Emmara, Jolrael, Garruk, Arlinn) as a payoff.
fp: Esika â‡„ ~20 creatures tagged lordâ†’tribe â€” Esika is not a typed/tribal anthem lord; her ability lets legendary creatures tap for mana, so these should be a mana-fixing/legendary-matters relationship, not lordâ†’tribe pump (single biggest source of inflation)

Bloodline Keeper â‡„ Plargg, Sphinx, Sisay, Kolvori, Triplicate Titan, etc. tagged lordâ†’tribe â€” Bloodline Keeper is a Vampire lord and there are NO other Vampires in the deck; every one of its ~25 lordâ†’tribe edges is a false positive

Plargg, Dean of Chaos // Augusta lordâ†’tribe edges to most creatures â€” Augusta is a go-wide attack-trigger payoff, not a tribal lord buffing a creature type; the lordâ†’tribe tag is wrong (go-wideâ†’payoff is the correct frame)

Rhys the Redeemed â‡„ Sandstone Oracle / Meteor Golem / Diluvian Primordial tagged copyâ†’trigger s2 â€” Rhys copies a token, it does not copy these creatures' ETB triggers; this is a spurious copyâ†’trigger combo-shaped edge

Arcane Signet/Sol Ring/Fellwar Stone/Chromatic Lantern â‡„ Cosima, Kolvori, Valki rampâ†’sink â€” these 'sinks' are not real mana-dumps for the rocks (Cosima/Kolvori are fixed-cost flip cards), so most rampâ†’sink edges are noise rather than a payoff engine
fn: TRANSFORM / double-faced-card matters â€” the deck's literal theme and name ('Cute to Brute' = flip the small side into the big side). 'transform' is emitted as a produced event by ~12 cards but there is NO family connecting them; the single most defining mechanic of the deck is invisible to the classifier

Legendary-matters / legendary-tribal â€” Esika (legends tap for any color), Sisay Weatherlight Captain (tutor legendaries), Kolvori God of Kinship (counts legendary creatures, deg-gates The Ringhart Crest), Kytheonâ†’Gideon are a real engine; Sisay sits near-island (deg 3) and the synergy is never captured

Cheat-into-play / The Prismatic Bridge engine â€” Esika's back face puts a creature/planeswalker into play each upkeep, a true card-advantage engine pointing at all the fatties; no family models 'free permanent into play' payoffs

ETB-value cluster (Diluvian Primordial, Meteor Golem, Sandstone Oracle, Nissa, Tovolar's Huntmaster) as reusable value with the Bridge/flicker-ish recursion â€” only partially captured via copyâ†’trigger noise rather than a real ETB-value family
issues: No transform / DFC family despite 'transform' being a tracked produced event â€” taxonomy gap that blanks out an entire archetype (this precon is the clearest example)

lordâ†’tribe is dramatically over-weighted: any card with is-lord/is-typed-lord (Esika, Bloodline Keeper, Plargg) fans an edge to nearly every creature regardless of shared creature type, producing 121 edges and inflating cohesion. Lord edges should require an actual shared/relevant subtype on the target

rampâ†’sink fires too promiscuously: every mana rock connects to every card with is-mana-sink, even when the 'sink' is a fixed-cost permanent, not an X-cost or repeatable mana dump (94 edges of mostly-noise)
No legendary-matters family â€” a very common EDH theme (Esika/Sisay/Kolvori/Kamahl-style) has no representation, so legendary-tribal decks read as undifferentiated goodstuff
copyâ†’trigger gate is too loose: token-doublers (Rhys) get tagged as copying other creatures' ETB triggers, manufacturing combo-shaped s2 edges that don't exist
Cohesion appears driven by raw edge count from a few over-firing families; the score rewards 'big precon pile of legends' the same as a tuned synergy deck

rawEvals.name: Creative Energy (Modern Horizons 3 Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Jeskai (W/U/R) energy midrange-aggro. Satya, Aetherflux Genius is an energy-fueled token-copier: attacking makes a token copy of an artifact/creature you control and snowballs energy. The deck pays energy into a critical mass of value engines (Aethergeode Miner self-blink, Whirler Virtuoso thopters, Aethertide Whale, Aethersphere Harvester, Overclocked Electromancer, Conversion Apparatus, Solar Transformer) and wins by going wide and swinging â€” energy is the literal economy that everything taps into and spends. Lightning Runner / Aetherstorm Roc / Bident enable extra attacks and combat snowball; Cayth + Tezzeret's Gambit + Aether Refinery add a counters/proliferate sub-theme.
fp: Energy producer-to-producer edges modeled as synergy: e.g. Solar Transformer â‡„ Conversion Apparatus, or Aethersquall Ancient â‡„ Filigree Racer â€” both merely generate energy and don't enable each other; the shared-resource clique invents pairwise synergy where there is only a common pool.

Confiscation Coup â‡„ many energy cards (e.g. Confiscation Coup â‡„ Whirler Virtuoso): Coup is a one-shot energy SINK for a steal effect, not part of any recurring energy engine with those token-makers.

rampâ†’sink edges from every mana rock to every mana-sink (e.g. Arcane Signet/Sol Ring â‡„ Hourglass of the Lost / Sphinx of the Revelation): generic 'rock makes mana, big card costs mana' is true of any deck and is not a deck-specific synergy.

lordâ†’tribe edges from Angel of Invention to non-tribal random creatures (e.g. Angel of Invention â‡„ Roil Cartographer / Combustible Gearhulk): Angel is a Servo/anthem and one-shot pump, not a tribal lord buffing arbitrary artifacts/creatures as a 'tribe'.
tribal-countâ†’payoff edges treating artifact/energy-card count as a tribe (e.g. Burnished Hart â‡„ Scurry of Gremlins): inflates a generic go-wide payoff into a tribal-synergy edge.
fn: Satya's signature ability â€” attacking exiles the top card and creates a token COPY of an artifact or creature you control â€” is the deck's actual payoff/copy engine but is only tagged generic combatâ†’payoff; there is no event for 'attack-trigger token-copy' tying her to the best ETB/token targets (Myr Battlesphere, Whirler Virtuoso, Angel of Invention).

Energy-as-counter / proliferate interplay: Tezzeret's Gambit and Cayth proliferate, and energy is tracked as a player counter that some cards care about â€” the taxonomy treats energy and proliferate-counters as separate families and misses that proliferate can grow the energy pool.

Thopter/Servo token go-wide as a distinct payoff cluster (Whirler Virtuoso, Angel of Invention, Stone Idol Generator, Professional Face-Breaker feeding Scurry of Gremlins / Salvation Colossus overrun) is only partially captured via go-wideâ†’payoff; the token-engine-to-anthem line is underweighted relative to the energy noise.
Treasure subtheme (Goldspan Dragon, Professional Face-Breaker) as rampâ†’energy/sink fuel is barely represented (treasure family = 2).
issues: Shared-resource families (energy, and to a lesser extent rampâ†’sink) are modeled as fully-connected cliques, producing producerâ†”producer false edges and massively inflating degree/edge counts â€” energy alone is 368 of 611 edges. Needs directional producerâ†’consumer gating and de-duplication.
No event for a commander's attack-trigger token-COPY ability (Satya); copyâ†’trigger exists but isn't applied to her, so the deck's namesake engine is invisible to the model.

lordâ†’tribe fires for non-tribal anthems (Angel of Invention) against arbitrary creatures, and tribal-countâ†’payoff treats artifact/permanent counts as a 'tribe' â€” both over-generate edges in non-tribal decks.
Energy and proliferate-counters are siloed families even though energy is itself a counter that proliferate interacts with â€” a missing cross-family link.
rampâ†’sink fires for generic mana-rock + expensive-card pairs that are not deck-specific synergies, padding cohesion.

rawEvals.name: Painbow (Dominaria United Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Five-color (WUBRG) "goodstuff" midrange pile built around Jared Carthalion. It ramps via rocks/dorks/land-ramp into big multicolored bombs (Chromanticore, Fusion Elemental, the Apex creatures, Maelstrom Archangel), plays a heavy removal/wrath suite (Time Wipe, Merciless Eviction, Duneblast, charms), refills with card draw, and wins by attacking with large multicolor creatures â€” optionally boosted by Surrak's overrun-on-cast and Rienne's multicolor payoff. There is no dedicated combo or tight engine; it is a value/threat-density deck, exactly the "Pile of good-stuff" band the classifier assigned.
fp: Rienne, Angel of Rebirth â‡„ ~every creature (lordâ†’tribe): Rienne is a multicolor recursion payoff, not a tribal lord; in a 5-color deck nearly all creatures are multicolored so she connects to everything, manufacturing ~15 phantom 'tribe' edges with no shared deck-wide tribal plan

Surrak Dragonclaw â‡„ ~every creature (lordâ†’tribe): Surrak grants flash/uncounterable and is a one-shot overrun, not a tribe anchor; tagging him as a lord linked to all 20 creatures inflates cohesion massively
Two-Headed Hellkite â‡„ Rienne / Surrak (lordâ†’tribe): a single dragon being counted into a 'tribe' is meaningless here

Mana Cannons / Lavalanche / Radiant Flames / Painful Truths â‡„ every mana rock (rampâ†’sink): generic 'ramp exists + X-spell exists' product; these are just normal ramp usage, not a designed sink engine â€” 70 such edges drastically over-represents intent
Primeval Spawn â‡„ Maelstrom Nexus / Hero of Precinct One / Zaxara (cast): cast-trigger links are real triggers but treated as synergy where it is incidental goodstuff overlap
fn: Land-ramp sorceries (Cultivate, Kodama's Reach, Farseek, Explosive Vegetation, Migration Path) are flagged as islands with zero edges and no is-ramp tag, even though they are functionally identical ramp to Arcane Signet/Coalition Relic which got 6+ rampâ†’sink edges each â€” the ramp taxonomy ignores fetch-land/basic-ramp spells, creating inconsistent false-negative islands

Color-fixing / WUBRG-enablement is the deck's true cohesion axis (Prophetic Prism, Abundant Growth, Faeburrow Elder, Chromanticore, Transguild Courier, Coalition Relic feeding 5-color bombs like Fusion Elemental and the WUBRG charms) but there is no 'fixingâ†’multicolor-payoff' family, so this central theme is invisible
Path to the World Tree (devotion/landfall enabler) and Atla Palani egg-token line have no relevant event, leaving them near-islands
Jared Carthalion's monarch/superfriends counter-and-token escalation as the commander's own value engine is only weakly modeled (deg 4)
issues: Ramp tagging is inconsistent: artifact/creature mana sources get is-ramp and many edges, but land-ramp sorceries (Cultivate/Farseek/Kodama's Reach/Explosive Vegetation/Migration Path) are untagged and become false islands

lordâ†’tribe family fires on any is-lord/is-typed-lord card linked to all creatures with no shared creature-type or coherent tribal density check â€” produces huge phantom webs in non-tribal 5-color decks (Rienne, Surrak)
rampâ†’sink is an unbounded cartesian product (sources x sinks) that over-weights generic mana-into-X-spell as if it were designed synergy
No 'color-fixing â†’ multicolor payoff' family, so the defining mechanic of a Painbow/5-color deck is unrepresented
Mana-sink classification is coarse: X-spells, burn wraths, and a damage enchantment all collapse into one undifferentiated rampâ†’sink bucket

rawEvals.name: Draconic Domination (Commander 2017 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Five-color Dragon tribal big-creature midrange/goodstuff. The Ur-Dragon ramps Dragons via cost reduction, draws cards when Dragons attack, and the deck wins by ramping into a board of fat fliers (Utvara Hellkite, Broodmate Dragon, Scourge of Valkas, Wasitora) and beating down. Secondary lines: extra combat (Hellkite Charger + Savage Ventmaw infinite combat), Dragon Tempest / Scourge of Valkas as ETB damage payoffs, a light reanimation sub-theme (Bladewing, Teneb, Scion, Boneyard Scourge, Palace Siege), and a handful of mass-removal/board wipes.
fp: Taigam, Ojutai Master â‡„ Cultivate/Farseek/Kodama's Reach/Rain of Thorns/Fortunate Few/Crux of Fate (family 'magecraft'): Taigam only rebounds/double-casts INSTANTS and SORCERIES that target a single creature, and his payoff is irrelevant to ramp spells like Cultivate â€” these are spurious 'magecraft' edges, Taigam is not a magecraft/spell-payoff engine for this deck

Darksteel Ingot â‡„ Crux of Fate / Deathbringer Regent / Fortunate Few / Rain of Thorns / Scalelord Reckoner (family 'destroy'): the rock is indestructible, but that is not a synergy â€” pairing a destroy-spell with an indestructible artifact is not an interaction that does anything for the gameplan

Curse of Verbosity/Curse of Bounty/Curse of Opulence â‡„ Lightning Greaves (family 'attach'): Curses attach to a PLAYER and Greaves equips a creature â€” these are unrelated 'attach' keywords miscollapsed into one edge
Dromoka/Sunscorch â‡„ Ramos, Dragon Engine (family 'counters'): these put +1/+1 counters on themselves while Ramos accrues its own counters from spells cast; there is no counter-synergy between them
Intet/Taigam â‡„ Ramos (family 'cast'): generic 'cast' co-occurrence, not a functional engine
fn: Hellkite Charger + Savage Ventmaw = INFINITE combat steps / infinite attacks (Ventmaw makes 6 mana on attack, Charger untaps all attackers for 5+tap, then add Dragon Tempest/Scourge of Valkas for an arbitrarily large or lethal loop). The classifier logged ordinary 'rampâ†’sink' and 'combat-enabler' edges but set loops=0 and hasCombo=false â€” a genuine two-card combo engine was missed

ETB/attack damage-multiplier engine: Dragon Tempest and Scourge of Valkas deal damage equal to/ for every Dragon that enters; with token-makers (Utvara Hellkite, Broodmate Dragon, Wasitora) this is a go-wide burn payoff with no dedicated family ('etb-dragonâ†’damage')

Reanimation payoff line: Bladewing the Risen, Teneb, Scion of the Ur-Dragon, Boneyard Scourge, Palace Siege form a recur-fat-Dragons engine that is only captured as scattered weak 'graveyard' edges, with no reanimatorâ†’fatty-payoff family

Cost-reduction tribal payoff: Dragonlord's Servant, Dragonspeaker Shaman, Herald's Horn, The Ur-Dragon and Fist of Suns all reduce Dragon casting cost â€” a real ramp-equivalent tribal engine that is largely uncaptured (these appear as deg-1 near-islands)
issues: Combo gate too strict / loop detector missed the canonical Hellkite Charger + Savage Ventmaw infinite-combat loop; hasCombo=false and loops=0 understate a real (if mana-fragile) two-card engine

'magecraft'/spellcast family over-fires: any noncreature spell + Taigam gets an edge regardless of whether Taigam's rebound actually rewards that spell type â€” inflates edge count and largestWebShare
'attach' family conflates Aura/Curse attach-to-player with Equipment equip-to-creature
'destroy' family treats indestructible-rock + removal-spell as a synergy when it is merely resilience, not an interaction
No taxonomy event for tribal cost-reduction (cost-reducerâ†’tribe), so key Dragon-tribal enablers fall out as islands/deg-1
No 'ETB-creature-count â†’ damage' event to model Dragon Tempest / Scourge of Valkas, a core payoff of the deck

Islands list (Fist of Suns, Kindred Discovery, Elemental Bond, Mirror of the Forebears) flags several cards that ARE on-theme (Fist of Suns = 5-color cost reducer; Kindred Discovery/Elemental Bond = Dragon ETB/attack card draw) â€” reveals missing 'creature-enters â†’ draw' and 'WUBRG cost reducer' modeling

rawEvals.name: Legends' Legacy (Dominaria United Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Mardu (WBR) "legends matter" tribal â€” a go-wide/midrange aggro deck that floods the board with legendary creatures and wins through combat, backed by legendary-tribal anthems/payoffs (Dihada, The Circle of Loyalty, Arvad the Cursed, Day of Destiny, Shanid, Blackblade Reforged, Heroes' Podium), a light reanimation sub-theme (Alesha, Teshar, Bladewing, Primevals' Glorious Rebirth, Garna), Treasure ramp (Captain Lannery Storm, The Reaver Cleaver, Dihada), and a legend-copy package (Cadric, Verrak, The Peregrine Dynamo). Wins by attacking with a wide, pumped legendary board.
fp: The Circle of Loyalty â‡„ most legendaries (lordâ†’tribe, s2): Circle only pumps/buffs Knights and only makes Knight tokens; it is NOT a generic legendary lord, so dozens of these tribal edges (e.g. to Etali, Traxos, Kothophed, Moira) overstate synergy
Day of Destiny â‡„ broad legendary list (lordâ†’tribe): Day of Destiny pumps only creatures of one chosen creature type, not all legendaries â€” most of its ~16 lord edges are false

Kaya's Wrath â‡„ go-wide/tribal-count payoffs (e.g. Krenko, Oketra's Monument, Cadric): a board wipe is anti-synergistic with the deck's own go-wide token producers, not a 'go-wide payoff' â€” these edges invert the real relationship

Faithless Looting / Etali / Bell Borca â‡„ the three Amonkhet Monuments (cast): the Monuments only reduce cost or trigger off creature spells of their color identity, so pairing noncreature card-draw or Etali's free-cast with 'cast' triggers is mostly noise

Garna â‡„ many creatures (lordâ†’tribe): Garna is not a lord/anthem â€” it returns nontoken creatures that died and gives haste on a turn-trigger; tagging it as a tribal lord for the whole board is wrong
fn: Heroes' Podium (island, deg 0): a core piece â€” anthem for legendary creatures AND a tutor that fetches a legendary creature; the 'legendary-count payoff' + tutor role is entirely absent from the taxonomy so it reads as non-interactive
Blackblade Reforged (deg 1, only 'attach'): its power/toughness boost scales with number of lands/legends and it has reduced equip on legends â€” the scaling-with-board mechanic is unmodeled

The deck's true unifying mechanic â€” 'legendary creature count matters' (Arvad, Shanid, Dihada's draw, Circle of Loyalty, the legend-rule copy interactions) â€” has no dedicated family; it is flattened into generic lordâ†’tribe and tribal-countâ†’payoff

Reanimation/recursion engine is under-captured: Alesha, Teshar (recur on historic/legendary cast), Bladewing, Primevals' Glorious Rebirth and Moira form a graveyard-value web, but most are only loosely tied via Dihada's mill rather than to each other as a reanimation package
Legend-rule downside: many singleton legends create tension/anti-synergy (can't have two copies via Cadric/Verrak token-copies of nontoken legends) â€” no model of this self-interference
issues: No 'legendary-count payoff' or 'historic matters' family despite this being a flagship legends-matter precon â€” the single most important theme is invisible to the taxonomy and absorbed into generic lordâ†’tribe

Lordâ†’tribe edges ignore the lord's actual subtype/condition: Knight-only (Circle of Loyalty) and choose-a-type (Day of Destiny) lords are wired to the entire creature base, structurally inflating lordâ†’tribe to 134 and weightedAvgDeg
Board wipes scored as 'go-wideâ†’payoff' / 'tribal-countâ†’payoff' (Kaya's Wrath) â€” sweepers should not be treated as payoffs for the controller's own tokens; family sign/direction is wrong
Static anthem/tutor permanents with empty produces/consumes (Heroes' Podium, Blackblade Reforged) fall through to islands, undercounting real interaction for an otherwise-cohesive deck
Cost-reducer Monuments generate 'cast' edges from any noncreature spell, ignoring that they only trigger/reduce for creature spells of a specific color â€” cast family gate is too loose

rawEvals.name: Draconic Dissent (Battle for Baldur's Gate Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Izzet (UR) goad / "forced combat" political pile led by Firkraag, Cunning Instigator. It wins by goading opponents' creatures into attacking each other (and into your goad-attack payoffs), then capitalizing on the resulting combat with damage-reflection and punisher pieces. Two intertwined sub-engines: (1) goad-go-wide chaos â€” Disrupt Decorum / Bloodthirsty Blade / the Impetus auras / Baeloth / Vengeful Ancestor / Death Kiss force attacks; (2) damage payoffs â€” Stuffy Doll, Brash Taunter, Niv-Mizzet Parun, Drakuseth, Blasphemous Act / Chain Reaction (mass damage that you survive), and Reins of Power-style "steal their team, swing" tricks. It is a Jund-style group-slug deck wearing Izzet colors, not a focused combo deck.
fp: rampâ†’sink edges (Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Mind Stone / Izzet Signet / Hedron Archive â‡„ Death Kiss, Curse of the Swine, Chain Reaction, Steel Hellkite): mana rocks are generic acceleration, not a dedicated 'sink' engine. Death Kiss and Steel Hellkite use mana incidentally; pairing every rock with every X-spell/activated ability is noise, not synergy.

magecraft family on Will Kenrith â‡„ Spectacular Showdown / Compulsive Research / Chain Reaction / Blasphemous Act / Chaos Warp / Disrupt Decorum / Curse of the Swine / Aether Gale / Domineering Will / Reins of Power: Will Kenrith has no magecraft ('whenever you cast an instant/sorcery') ability â€” these are false. There is essentially no magecraft theme in this deck.

The full goad clique as pairwise edges (e.g. Mocking Doppelganger â‡„ Bothersome Quasit â‡„ Angler Turtle â‡„ Agitator Ant, etc.): goad is a unilateral effect on opponents' creatures; two of your own goaders do not synergize with each other. These mutual-goad edges massively over-count interactivity.

copyâ†’trigger Artificer Class â‡„ Solemn Simulacrum / Thunder Dragon / Pursued Whale and Will/Rowan Kenrith â‡„ ETB creatures: Artificer Class chapter III copies an artifact you control on ETB and the Kenrith copy effects are spell-copy / loyalty, not reliable ETB-doublers â€” these are weak speculative links flagged at strength 2.
landfall Wayfarer's Bauble / Solemn Simulacrum â‡„ Geode Rager: a single one-time fetch trigger is not a landfall engine.
fn: No 'damage-reflection / punisher' family: Stuffy Doll, Brash Taunter, and (to a degree) Kazuul punish or redirect damage â€” the deck's signature 'you-hit-me-you-lose' line (e.g. Blasphemous Act + Brash Taunter/Stuffy Doll dealing damage back to a chosen opponent) is captured only loosely under generic 'damage' and not recognized as the intentional engine it is.

Goad-attack-payoff is under-modeled as a directed mechanic: the taxonomy has no event for 'opponents' goaded creatures attack -> I draw/gain (Curse of Verbosity, Loot Dispute, Baeloth treasure, Vengeful Ancestor) AS A CONSEQUENCE OF MY GOAD.' The real engine is goad-source -> forced attack -> my combat-trigger payoff, a directed chain the classifier flattens into separate goad and combatâ†’payoff cliques.
Mass-damage-as-removal that you survive (Blasphemous Act, Chain Reaction) synergizing with one-sided survivors / indestructible Stuffy Doll is not modeled as a board-wipe-payoff family.

Politics/deterrent package: Propaganda, Dissipation Field, Disrupt Decorum's chaos are a coherent 'pillowfort + redirect aggression elsewhere' plan that the taxonomy has no concept of, leaving Propaganda and Dissipation Field as islands.
issues: Goad is modeled as a symmetric pairwise family, producing an n^2 clique (105 goad instances among ~15 cards) that inflates edge count, avgDeg (7.81) and largestWebShare (85%) without representing real cohesion. Goad should be a directed enabler->payoff relationship, not goader<->goader.
magecraft family fires on a card (Will Kenrith) that has no magecraft ability â€” the capability-tagging or family rule is mis-detecting 'spellcast-payoff' as magecraft, generating ~10 false edges.

ramp->sink is over-fired: any mana rock paired with any X-cost or activated-ability card creates an edge, drowning the graph in generic-acceleration noise (44 instances). Needs a tighter 'dedicated mana sink' gate.

No taxonomy for damage-redirection / punisher creatures (Stuffy Doll, Brash Taunter) as a coordinated engine, nor for goad-triggered attack payoffs as a directed chain â€” the two real win-engines are present in the cards but not named.

hasCombo=false and loops=0 are correct: this precon has no infinite combo, so the flag is right. The weightedAvgDeg of 2.38 vs avgDeg 7.81 correctly signals that most edges are weak â€” the weighting is doing useful work even though the raw graph is inflated.

rawEvals.name: Rebellion Rising (Phyrexia All Will Be One Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Boros (R/W) go-wide tokens aggro. Neyali, Suns' Vanguard impulse-draws off attacking creatures and doubles token-producing spells. The deck floods the board with creature tokens (Battle Screech, White Sun's Zenith, Finale of Glory, Martial Coup, Hordeling Outburst, Assemble the Legion, etc.), pumps the team with anthems/lords (Intangible Virtue, Phantom General, Goldnight Commander, Jor Kadeen, Adriana, Elspeth Tirel), and swings for lethal combat damage, often with an overrun effect (Heroic Reinforcements, Goldnight Commander) or Craterhoof-style alpha strike. It wins through combat with a wide board plus team pump â€” not a combo deck.
fp: rampâ†’sink between generic mana rocks and X-spells is over-flagged â€” e.g. Sol Ring â‡„ Hour of Reckoning, Fellwar Stone â‡„ White Sun's Zenith: rocks fueling a big X spell is normal mana use, not a deckbuilding synergy. 87 rampâ†’sink instances inflate the count.

Clever Concealment â‡„ every mana rock as rampâ†’sink â€” Clever Concealment is a protective phase-out/flicker spell, not a ramp piece feeding a sink; the 'is-ramp'/'is-mana-sink' tag on it is a misread.

Hate Mirage â‡„ Soul-Guide Lantern / Solemn Simulacrum tagged copyâ†’trigger at strength 2 â€” Hate Mirage copies opponents' creatures; pairing it with your own ETB artifacts as a copy engine is not a real interaction in this deck.

Adriana / Jor Kadeen lordâ†’tribe edges to non-token bodies (e.g. Solemn Simulacrum, Myr Battlesphere) are technically anthem coverage but flagged as 'lordâ†’tribe' tribal synergy, which overstates a tribal theme the deck doesn't really have (it's generic creatures/tokens, not a creature type).
fn: Neyali's impulsive-draw / extra-combat enablement is not modeled: her ability exiles top card on attack and lets you double the next token spell â€” the deck's central engine (cast-token-spell â†’ Neyali doubles it) has no dedicated event; it only shows up as generic 'tokens' edges.

Extra-combat / attack-trigger payoff loop is under-captured: Hexplate Wallbreaker and Heroic Reinforcements granting haste + the go-wide payoffs that trigger again on a second combat aren't represented as an extra-combat family.

Sacrifice-fodder â†’ outlet for token aristocrats is correctly ABSENT (no Siege-Gang sac engine beyond its own ability) â€” Siege-Gang Commander's sac-tokens-for-damage outlet is not flagged as a sac-fodderâ†’outlet synergy with all the token makers, a minor miss.
Monarch (Court of Grace) as a card-advantage axis tied to going wide / keeping monarch via a board is not modeled as a payoff.
issues: Edge double/triple counting: one go-wide relationship generates separate tokens + go-wideâ†’payoff + tribal-countâ†’payoff edges, inflating familyBreakdown totals and avgDeg (17.85) without representing distinct synergies.
rampâ†’sink family is too loose â€” any mana producer paired with any X-cost or expensive spell creates an edge, making it near-meaningless filler (87 instances here).
'lordâ†’tribe' conflates generic anthem/+1+1 effects (Phantom General buffs only tokens; Adriana buffs whole team) with true tribal type-matters synergy, with no distinction.
No taxonomy event for impulsive/exile-draw or 'double your next spell' commander abilities, so Neyali's signature engine is invisible to the classifier.
No extra-combat-step family despite multiple cards (Hexplate Wallbreaker, Neyali) enabling it â€” a real aggro engine the model can't see.

rawEvals.name: Paradox Power (Doctor Who Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Bant/Sultai-flavored "Doctor's Companion" superfriends-style value midrange built around time counters / suspend ("paradox") and +1/+1 counters. The Thirteenth Doctor untaps a Doctor's Companion each turn and manipulates time counters; the deck wins through incremental card advantage (Bill Potts magecraft scry, Heaven Sent, Start the TARDIS, Confession Dial, River Song's Diary recursion), a counter/proliferate subtheme doubled by Sisterhood of Karn, and a wide token board (The Fugitive Doctor, Graham O'Brien, Iraxxa) buffed into combat damage. It is a grindy goodstuff value deck, not a combo deck.
fp: The 'cast' family (136 instances) â€” e.g. The Thirteenth Doctor â‡„ Yasmin Khan, The Flux â‡„ Iraxxa, Bigger on the Inside â‡„ Memory Worm â€” these are not synergies, just 'both are spells I can cast'; they massively inflate avgDeg (9.39) with non-interactions.

Bill Potts â‡„ Cultivate / Farseek / Growth Spiral tagged 'magecraft' (s2): technically a scry triggers, but pairing a ramp spell with a scry-on-cast payoff is incidental, not a built-around line â€” Potts triggers on EVERY noncreature so these edges are noise inflation.

Karvanista â‡„ Madame Vastra and Karvanista â‡„ Osgood 'combatâ†’payoff': Karvanista's relevant text is hexproof/protection, not a combat-damage payoff engine with these tokens â€” a weak/spurious classification.
Sonic Screwdriver â‡„ Preordain 'scry': Screwdriver's scry and a scry sorcery don't combine into anything; coincidental shared keyword.

rampâ†’sink edges from every mana rock to Carpet of Flowers (itself a ramp source, not a sink) â€” Carpet is mislabeled as a mana-sink, producing false rampâ†’sink pairs (Sol Ring â‡„ Carpet of Flowers, etc.).
fn: Time counters / suspend / vanishing â€” the literal 'Paradox Power' theme. The Thirteenth Doctor adds/removes time counters and the deck pays off having time counters (Flaming Tyrannosaurus, Heaven Sent, Iraxxa, Memory Worm, The Foretold Soldier). No family models time-counter manipulation or suspend payoff; these are flattened into generic 'has-counters'.

The Doctor â†’ Companion untap engine: The Thirteenth Doctor untaps a Doctor's Companion each upkeep (is-untapper), enabling extra activations/mana from Sonic Screwdriver, Osgood, Nardole, Yasmin Khan. The 'untapâ†’tap-ability' enablement family exists in the taxonomy but produced NO edge from the commander to these tappers â€” a missed core line.
Proliferate as a counter-payoff: the deck leans on growing counters but proliferate/counter-synergy beyond Sisterhood's flat doubling isn't modeled as its own family.

The three island cards (TARDIS, Become the Pilot, Return the Past) are real engines the model has no event for: TARDIS is a suspend/blink Saga-vehicle, Become the Pilot grants the Doctor's-Companion untap-and-attach interaction, and Return the Past is mass graveyard recursion â€” all genuinely interactive but invisible to the taxonomy.
issues: No event for time counters / suspend / vanishing despite it being a top-line MTG mechanic and this deck's named theme â€” collapsed into undifferentiated 'has-counters'.
The generic 'cast' family dominates edge count (136 of 330) and inflates avgDeg/largestWebShare without representing synergy; it should be excluded or heavily down-weighted in cohesion.

untapâ†’tap-ability enablement family failed to fire on the commander's signature untap-a-Companion ability even though The Thirteenth Doctor is tagged is-untapper and several tappers exist â€” gating too strict or untap-event not linked to tap-abilities.
Carpet of Flowers is tagged both is-ramp and is-mana-sink, generating false rampâ†’sink self-class edges.
magecraft edges count every noncreature spell uniformly; no weighting for whether the spell's effect actually matters, so ramp/removal spells inflate the magecraft cluster.
islandCards reveals three genuinely interactive cards (TARDIS, Become the Pilot, Return the Past) with zero edges â€” a coverage gap for suspend/attach/mass-recursion mechanics.

rawEvals.name: Divine Convocation (March of the Machine Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Jeskai (W/U/R) go-wide convoke tokens. It floods the board with small creatures and tokens (Secure the Wastes, Battle Screech, Elspeth Sun's Champion, Goblin Instigator, Migratory Route, Kykar, Nesting Dovehawk) then converts that width into power/value: convoke payoffs (Hour of Reckoning, Conclave Tribunal, Chant of Vitu-Ghazi, Venerated Loxodon, Path of the Ghosthunter) and width payoffs (Seraph of the Masses, Flight of Equenauts, commander Kasla rewarding convoke casts with draw/scry). A secondary "draw = tokens / counters" subtheme runs The Locust God, Nadir Kraken, Chasm Skulker, Improbable Alliance feeding Skullclamp and convoke. It wins via a wide board + a convoke-cast alpha strike or evasive fliers, not via a combo.
fp: Cut Short â‡„ Shatter the Source (rampâ†’sink): two convoke removal instants flagged as a ramp/sink synergy pair â€” neither ramps nor sinks the other; they merely share the convoke keyword, no real interaction
Stoke the Flames â‡„ Temporal Cleansing (rampâ†’sink): unrelated convoke spells linked purely by both being 'is-ramp + is-mana-sink', a clique artifact

Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Commander's Sphere â‡„ basically every convoke spell (rampâ†’sink): rocks are real ramp, but the edge to each individual convoke spell as a 'sink' is noise â€” the rock doesn't synergize with Devouring Light any more than with any card

Ephemeral Shields â‡„ Hour of Reckoning / Wear // Tear (destroy): Ephemeral Shields gives indestructible (its 'destroy' consume tag is the sacrifice/destroy clause), being matched to your own board wipes is not a meaningful synergy edge

Nesting Dovehawk â‡„ Banisher Priest / Angel of Finality / Conclave Tribunal (copyâ†’trigger): Dovehawk copies a token on attack; pairing it as 'copyâ†’trigger' with exile-ETB removal creatures overstates a real engine that mostly matters for value-ETB bodies
Deluxe Dragster â‡„ Battle Screech (graveyard): a lone, tenuous graveyard edge that does not reflect any graveyard theme in the deck
fn: CONVOKE as a mechanic: the single most important keyword in this precon has no family. Convoke is what links token-makers (Secure the Wastes, Battle Screech, tokens, Kykar Spirits) to convoke spells (Hour of Reckoning, Conclave Tribunal, Venerated Loxodon, Path of the Ghosthunter, Chant of Vitu-Ghazi) and to Kasla's draw/scry payoff â€” it was collapsed into generic rampâ†’sink instead of a tokens/go-wide â†’ cost-reduction bridge

Kasla's 'cast a spell with convoke â†’ draw/scry' payoff: the commander's actual reward loop (convoke a spell, draw a card) is not modeled as a distinct trigger family; it only shows up as draw/rampâ†’sink noise

Token-as-convoke-fodder synergy: tokens existing specifically to be tapped for convoke (the deck's core engine) is absent â€” go-wideâ†’payoff captures combat width but not the convoke-fuel relationship
Anthem/lifegain incidental themes (Suture Priest, Mentor of the Meek) tied to creatures entering are under-captured beyond a lordâ†’tribe stub
issues: rampâ†’sink family is catastrophically over-fired (605 of ~705 family instances): tagging any mana-spending or convoke spell as both is-ramp and is-mana-sink creates a near-complete clique that dominates cohesion, avgDeg, and largestWebShare
No 'convoke' (tokens/go-wide â†’ cost-reduction) family exists; convoke is the deck's central mechanic and gets mis-routed into rampâ†’sink
rampâ†’sink should require an actual mana-accelerator â†’ expensive-payoff direction, not pairwise links between every X-cost or convoke spell
Commander-specific payoff (Kasla: convoke-cast â†’ draw/scry) is not represented as a dedicated trigger, undercounting the true engine while over-representing generic ramp
is-mana-sink is too loosely assigned â€” almost every spell qualifies, destroying its discriminating power
copyâ†’trigger over-weighted: Nesting Dovehawk gets strength-2 edges to every ETB creature regardless of whether copying that body is meaningful

rawEvals.name: Coven Counters (Innistrad: Midnight Hunt Commander 2021 Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Selesnya (G/W) +1/+1 counters midrange with a go-wide token sub-plan and a light humans/coven tribal overlay. It accumulates +1/+1 counters (Leinore, Mikaeus the Lunarch, Kurbis, Citadel Siege, Juniper Order Ranger, Orzhov Advokist), multiplies them (Biogenic Upgrade, Dearly Departed, Doubling-style triggers), and converts the counter/creature mass into evasion and card advantage â€” Champion of Lambholt (makes the team unblockable), Abzan Falconer (flying), Inspiring Call and Shamanic Revelation (draw off counters/creature count), Herald of War (cost reduction). It closes by beating down with a wide, oversized board, with Hour of Reckoning / Cleansing Nova as asymmetric wipes and Bastion Protector / Unbreakable Formation protecting the board. No combo; it wins through grindy counters-and-bodies aggression.
fp: Bastion Protector â‡„ ~30 creatures via lordâ†’tribe â€” Bastion Protector only pumps/protects LEGENDARY creatures, but it is edged to non-legendary bodies like Ainok Bond-Kin, Champion of Lambholt, Gyre Sage, Eternal Witness, Trostani's Summoner; these are not real lord buffs.

Riders of Gavony â‡„ many non-Humans via lordâ†’tribe â€” Riders only grants protection/anthem keyed to a chosen creature type (Humans), yet it is linked to Beasts/Plants/non-human bodies like Wild Beastmaster, Gyre Sage, Victory's Envoy, Yavimaya Elder as if a generic lord.

Kyler, Sigardian Emissary tagged is-typed-lord and edged broadly via lordâ†’tribe â€” Kyler is a counters payoff (gets +1/+1 counters equal to Humans you control, then distributes), not an anthem lord for the whole board; the lordâ†’tribe framing mislabels its actual counters/humans interaction.

rampâ†’sink edges like Sol Ring / Arcane Signet / Talisman of Unity â‡„ Custodi Soulbinders / Wild Beastmaster â€” generic mana rocks feeding a creature with an activated sink is real but very weak/ubiquitous; counting 87 rampâ†’sink instances overstates these as deck-defining synergy rather than baseline ramp.

counters edges between two passive counter-getters (e.g. Enduring Scalelord â‡„ Gyre Sage, Ainok Bond-Kin â‡„ Custodi Soulbinders) â€” both merely 'have counters'; they don't interact with each other, so the dense all-pairs counters mesh is largely non-synergistic co-membership rather than true edges.
fn: Coven (control three creatures with different powers) â€” Leinore, Dawnhart Wardens-style coven payoffs and the set's signature mechanic have NO event/family in the taxonomy, so the deck's namesake synergy is entirely invisible.

Counter-doubling / proliferate-style multiplication is partly captured, but the 'distribute/move counters' payoffs (Kyler distributing, Elite Scaleguard tapping based on counters, Citadel Siege's Khans-mode tap) are flattened into generic 'counters' without modeling counterâ†’evasion or counterâ†’tempo lines.

Graveyard/reanimation recursion package â€” Moorland Rescuer, Angel of Glory's Rise (reanimates Humans), Eternal Witness, Kessig Cagebreakers (uses creatures in GY) form a recursion sub-theme that is barely edged; the synergy of board wipe + Moorland Rescuer/Angel of Glory's Rise recursion is missed.

Asymmetric board-wipe synergy â€” Hour of Reckoning and Cleansing Nova are wraths the deck dodges via convoke/tokens/Bastion Protector indestructibility + Unbreakable Formation; this 'we wipe, they lose more' line is not modeled as a synergy, only as scattered 'destroy' edges.
Champion of Lambholt's team-unblockable payoff off counters/tokens (a genuine win-enabler) is reduced to a generic 'counters' node and its evasion-enabler role is not represented.
issues: No 'coven' event/family despite this being a Midnight Hunt Coven precon â€” the taxonomy lacks the deck's headline mechanic, a systematic blind spot for set-specific keywords.

lordâ†’tribe family is too loose: it does not check the lord's actual granted type (legendary-only for Bastion Protector, Humans-only for Riders of Gavony) and fires against the whole creature base, inflating avgDeg (13.35) and edge count (423).
'counters' capability creates an all-pairs clique: any two 'has-counters' permanents are edged even when neither affects the other, producing 115 mostly-inert counter edges and overstating cohesion.
rampâ†’sink over-counts generic mana rocks (Sol Ring, Signet, Talisman) as synergy with any mana-sink creature, padding 87 instances; should be weighted near-zero for ubiquitous ramp.

Cohesion 70 ('Very cohesive') is inflated: the deck IS thematically tight, but the score is propped up by clique-style false edges; a counters precon's true synergy density is real but lower than the graph implies â€” score is somewhat too high, while hasCombo=false is correctly set (no combo here).

rawEvals.name: Blast from the Past (Doctor Who Commander Precon Decklist)
rawEvals.archetype: Doctors-matter legendary/historic tribal midrange in Bant/Esper-ish (actually GWU) colors. It wins by deploying a critical mass of legendary "Doctor" and companion creatures, anthem/counting them with Day of Destiny and Five Hundred Year Diary, growing them with +1/+1 counters, and beating down â€” supported by Doctor + Companion pairing, "time counter" / Time Lord saga value engines, and a strong removal/wrath suite to reset opponents while the legendary board persists.
fp: rampâ†’sink en masse: every mana rock (Sol Ring, Mind Stone, Talismans, Thought Vessel, Arcane Signet, Sonic Screwdriver) wired to Peri Brown / Jamie McCrimmon / Alistair / Vrestin â€” this is generic mana-to-X-spell plumbing, not a designed combo; 49 instances vastly overstate cohesion
Peri Brown â‡„ Susan Foreman / Jamie â‡„ Susan (rampâ†’sink between two mana-dorks) â€” two ramp pieces is not a sink relationship

Heroic Intervention â‡„ Time Wipe / Crisis of Conscience â‡„ Heroic Intervention (family 'destroy') â€” these are unrelated protection/wrath spells lumped together by a shared 'destroy' tag, not a real interaction
Ace's Baseball Bat â‡„ Hero's Blade ('attach') â€” two independent Equipment do not synergize with each other
The Night of the Doctor â‡„ Tegan Jovanka / â‡„ Heroic Intervention ('destroy') â€” coincidental shared keyword, not an intended line
fn: Doctor + Companion partner pairing â€” the set's defining mechanic (Companions attach to a Doctor and share its turn/abilities); there is no event for it, so the core build-around is unscored

Time counters / Time Travel â€” abilities that add/remove time counters (sagas like The War Games, The Caves of Androzani, An Unearthly Child, Trial of a Time Lord, The Five Doctors) drive the deck but have no taxonomy event and sit as deg-0 islands
Saga chapter-trigger value engine â€” multiple Saga enchantments (historic permanents) are treated as inert utility/islands instead of staged value engines

+1/+1 counters subtheme â€” Ace, The First/Fifth/Sixth Doctors, Jo Grant, Leela, Vrestin all place counters and Day of Destiny/Diary reward a wide board, but there is no counters-matter / proliferate family tying them together

historic / legendary-matters payoff is only half-captured: 17 island cards including TARDIS, Heroes' Podium, Bessie, The Five Doctors are legendary/historic enablers that the legendary-count engine actually cares about
issues: No event family for set-defining keywords: Doctor+Companion pairing, Time Travel / time counters, and Saga chapter triggers â€” entire mechanical pillar invisible, causing 17 islands that are actually thematic engine pieces
rampâ†’sink is structurally over-weighted: it forms a complete bipartite mesh (every rock Ă— every mana-sink), inflating edge count (49 of 176) without representing deck-specific synergy
'destroy' family conflates protection spells (Heroic Intervention), targeted removal, and board wipes (Time Wipe) into mutual edges that are not real interactions
No counters-matter / proliferate family to unify a clear +1/+1 counters subtheme
Equipment-to-Equipment 'attach' edge (Ace's Baseball Bat â‡„ Hero's Blade) is a false relationship from shared capability rather than directed enablerâ†’payoff
